# Thread: Trying to help my wife,  looking for information on pituitary tumors.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-to-help-my-wife-looking-for-information-on-pituitary-tumors.11362/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PaulWatson — 2014-07-19T19:31:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-to-help-my-wife-looking-for-information-on-pituitary-tumors.11362/post-134927>

Hello,

I have been a fan of Dr Kruse for a while, read several articles & listened to him on podcast interviews. My wife & I have been paleo/primal since early 2013, she was Gluten free before that. Over the years we have been dealing with her health the best we can. She has been continuously experiencing numerous symptoms over the last 2 decades, some even from as early as she can remember (Too many to list but some big ones are misdiagnosed Duanes syndrome, hormone levels very low, headaches, trigeminal nerve pain, fatigue, body aches, nausea, very sensitive to stress & temperature, digestive issues, the list goes on) We have been to several experts (Including Dr Mercola who's practice is just a few minutes from our home) & many, many general practitioners have offered very little or no help.

Up until now the best help has come from a functional neurologist / chiropractor who has been giving her adjustments & gave her eye exercises (opto-drum & similar) that have helped to relieve the pain, & has helped with some other digestive issues through supplementation.

This week we may have finally gotten some answers, an MRI found an 11mm tumor on her pituitary. We are now in that place with a initial finding but waiting for the appointments with neurosurgeons & endocrinologists. We are so thankful to finally know something & that action is ahead, but it is also a very frightening thing to be waiting, all we have are a few paragraphs that we do not fully understand & appointments several weeks out.

I read online that ketogenic diets can be very helpful with malignant tumors, does anyone know if a keto approach would be beneficial to benign tumors as well?

Any advice or information is greatly appreciated.

Paul

### Jude — 2014-07-20T06:03:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-to-help-my-wife-looking-for-information-on-pituitary-tumors.11362/post-134967>

Am unable to help with info ...........but am able to sincerely wish you and your dear wife all the best.
My cousin had a large pituitary tumor successfully removed some 35yrs ago and is still working strongly at 80!

### caroline — 2014-07-20T07:19:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-to-help-my-wife-looking-for-information-on-pituitary-tumors.11362/post-134968>

I can't offer much either ... but hope you find answers to your questions.

Where are you located? Dr. Kruse does have a practise....

Do you have Dr.K's book? The Epi-Paleo RX

have you been reading Dr. K.'s pertinent blogs to your wife's condition?

There is a neurologist on the forum who pops in when he has time ...

### Jack Kruse — 2014-07-21T00:15:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-to-help-my-wife-looking-for-information-on-pituitary-tumors.11362/post-135010>

Most pituitary tumors are benign and these days just about 95% are treated medically. The key is to find out is the tumor a secreting or null cell tumor and is it compression her optic chiasm........If there are eye signs a neurosurgeon will be who you will see for the short term fix......if it is not causing any field cuts you will be treated by the endocrinologist. The treatment of these tumors has changed a lot in 20 years.

### PaulWatson — 2014-07-21T05:02:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-to-help-my-wife-looking-for-information-on-pituitary-tumors.11362/post-135037>

Thanks to all of you for your kind wishes & information.

Caroline - I have Dr Kruse's book on my bookshelf but haven't read it yet, I was on the hormone cure & Dr Wahl's book due to where the problems & diagnoses were going at that time, but will be re-prioritizing my reading according to the new diagnosis. Once we had the current MRI findings I came here immediately. I have searched the blogs & forum here but really cannot find much information & the rest of the internet seems pretty thin once you get past the preliminary information. We are in the Chicago area so pretty far from Dr Kruse, but certainly not out of the realm of possible. It looks like Loyola University is where were being referred to for a neurosurgeon, Loyola is an excellent facility & we have every hope that they will be what we are looking for but if not we are not opposed to travelling for excellent care.

Dr Kruse - Thank you very much for your personal response. In the last year & half we have immersed ourselves into the paleosphere, reading many books & listening to podcasts while at work. I have found many of the thought leaders, yourself included to be truly inspirational & motivating, your devotion to this movement and to the health of strangers is nothing short of heroic.

I'll quote the mri report below but it does not seem as the tumor is secreting & her symptoms are consistent with non-secreting tumors. there are eye disturbances as some loss of field but also since childhood her left eye is a lazy eye, this was originally diagnosed as a result of Duanes syndrome, only recently her functional doc, Dr Baldino discovered that was not the case as when she covers her right eye there is control over the left & after some exercises he has her do some of the eyes function is restored & some of the pain & discomfort is alleviated.

### PaulWatson — 2014-07-21T05:10:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-to-help-my-wife-looking-for-information-on-pituitary-tumors.11362/post-135038>

MRI Report

"Findings: Multiple planes and sequences are submitted pre and post administration 7.5ml of Gadovist with additional thin slice images through the sella pre and postcontrast administration.​

There is a pituitary mass paracentrally on the left with deviation of the pituitary stalk to the right. The mass measures approximately 11 mm superior to inferior and anterior to posterior. This demonstrates heterogeneous enhancement. Axial FLAIR and T2 images demonstrate intrinsic fluid-fluid level. Necrosis as well as old hemorrage in the differential. There is no evidence of intrinsic high intensity bleed on T1-weighted image.

Ther are no othe rintra-axial or extra axial masses or fluid collections. No midline shift, mass effect or ventricular enlargement. Gray and white matter are appropriate in signal without abnormal enhancement. Posterior fossa is appropriate in appearance. Signal void is present in the visualized portions of the internal carotid arteries and basilar artery. No air fluid levels in the paranasal sinuses or fluid signal in the mastoid air cells.

Visualized portions of the trigeminal nerves and gasserian ganglia are without significant or asymmetric abnormal enhancement. Parotid gland is incompletely visualized.​

### Jack Kruse — 2014-07-21T06:16:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-to-help-my-wife-looking-for-information-on-pituitary-tumors.11362/post-135041>

secretion is determined by the blood tests not the MRI. Eye findings are done by formal visual field testing with the opthamologist.

# Thread: Kristen's Lyme journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kristens-lyme-journal.11364/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Ksilva — 2014-07-19T22:37:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kristens-lyme-journal.11364/post-134935>

Hello anyone reading...

I am new here, so I don't know what I'm doing. I am in college theoretically, had to drop out because of health problems for two years now. It is a long story....but I hope you will bear with me...

I had been on antidepressants for about eight years, stopped them after I thought medical marijuana could help instead, went through withdrawal and was hospitalized and incorrectly diagnosed bipolar. I was put on 73957 medications, gained a lot of weight very quickly, came home a month later and stopped all of them. I tried losing the weight by cutting out all carbs, and quickly lost about fifteen pounds before it came to a sharp halt, and I couldn't lose anything no matter the calorie level, for months. I fell into an eating disorder and depression. I had not slept more than three solid hours for several months by now because my body was a mess as you can imagine. The very-low carb diet seemed to be a final drop kick to my adrenals, as my anxiety and insomnia kicked up several notches, along with a new case of jitters.

I was put on a low dose of mood stabilizer that didn't do much, and a benzo for sleep...which was a humongous mistake. I lost the rest of the weight plus more, and lots of muscle...but had to go to rehab and gain back some weight. Now we are in June 2013, whereas all this started in June 2012. June is not a good month for me it seems. They also took me off two more medications while I was in rehab, so I was in withdrawal the whole time. I came out of rehab and stuck to my plan, but my weight kept creeping up. I tried exercising and it went up faster. I stopped exercising, it still went up. I started exercising again after it seemed stable for a month, and gained six pounds in five days from just walking a mile a day. Of course no one believes me when I say this, I do not blame them necessarily but it hurts all the same...I still cannot walk much without putting on weight...I will get to this in a minute.

I saw a nutritionist every week. I kept to the meal plan, even tried eating more to see if that'd help. No. I tried eating less, and my metabolism decided maintaining my body fat on less food would be just fine (in other words, it just slowed down). I cut out gluten. Then I cut out sugar, real and fake. Then I cut out most fruit (except a handful of berries a couple times a day). My stomach was always bloated, I couldn't and still can't hold down vegetables. I had long since cut out soy. I finally cut out dairy a few months ago. Nothing even made a dint. By now I had gained almost 40 lbs... 30 lbs over what I've weighed most of my life...but everyone said it would swing back.

This whole time I had been shuffled from psychiatrist to psychiatrist, because doctors told me I was fine. I refused all meds, I knew there was something wrong, I still know, but no one believed me... Once you get into the system of psychiatry people tend to do that. In the meantime I had been trying to taper off the benzo, and this, I believe, is what put the nail in the coffin of my long lost health. I developed Paresthesia, my insomnia got worse again, my mood sank, and my weight was shooting up even more...and now I had muscle and nerve pain along with it. My body also developed a bad sweating habit and body odor. But everyone insisted all my symptoms boiled down to my stressing out. What a nice catch-22.

Finally I got an appointment with a PCOS doctor. She took tests and diagnosed me with PCOS in April this year. She told me my only symptom was acne, and refused to work with me, telling me yet again to eat less, and that my symptoms would get better if I lost weight. My depression and morale was (still is) somewhere underneath the sewer lines by now. Low carb wasn't helping...there must have been something else wrong... My friend told me to get tested for Lyme.

I received my diagnosis of Lyme disease two months later, in June this year.

I have been on antibiotics for a couple weeks now and my weight is still rising. I have now gained 55 pounds in one year. I cannot walk, due to severe pain. I also cannot exert myself much without gaining weight, and I have narrowed this down to the high level of cytokines, from both withdrawal and Lyme disease, circulating in my body and being stirred up by movement, causing fat cells to expand and "catch" the toxins. I have also read Jack's post on Lyme and leptin resistance, and this somewhat confirms my theory. I imagine I sound like a lunatic for saying all of this, but at this point no one else knows what is wrong, and not many people seem to want to bother listening, so I am left to my own resources.

I really, really hope someone can help me on this website. I can only sit at home and wait to get better, but the pattern has been such that I only get worse and so I do not see a life ahead of me anymore. I'm sorry to end on a negative note. Thank you for reading.

### Ksilva — 2014-07-19T23:21:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kristens-lyme-journal.11364/post-134943>

For reference...

Meds I have accumulated (none of these work anymore, am trying to wean off slowly one at a time; also, they are not causing weight gain, I have been on all of them before):

Lamictal, 50 mg
Wellbutrin XL, 150 mg
Trazodone, 25 mg
Ativan, 1.25 mg

Other:
Cefuroxime, 1000mg, for Lyme

Supplements:

Prebiotic
Probiotic
B-12 methylcobalamin
Digestive enzymes - can only take in morning before breakfast, otherwise stomach ache all day
Berberine
5-MTHF - not a mutation but have trouble processing folate
Inositol - for PCOS, insulin resistance
Alpha Lipoic Acid - insulin resistance
Cod Liver Oil
Omega-3 fish oil with chromium
D3
DIM - for high estrogen
Magnesium - CALM powder and transdermal oil
Vitamin C powder
Melatonin, Sleep Tonight, Doxylamine - all for sleep
Progesterone cream/capsules - for low progesterone
Green powder

### caroline — 2014-07-20T05:41:19+0300

I am so glad you have found your way here. Why don't we work a little on your circadian cycles first....

This is what your day must look like ...

Upon rising .... Go outside and look at the sun

BAB within thirty minutes of rising .... Leave at least 4 hours between meals and before bed ...And absolutely no snacking.

Drink bottled spring water

Get outside as much as possible - bare feet and little clothes

What is your non native EMF and blue light exposure?

No fake lights after sunset

Get all electronics out of where you sleep and turn off the breaker if you can. Make bedroom as dark as possible.

You have to reset the Rolex in your brain .....

Don't underestimate any of the above ... Commit 100%

You need to eat a ton of seafood .... Oysters, oysters, oysters!

Think of it like this, also Jack said it a couple of times...

You have a bucket in which you try to grow a plant... If the bucket has holes you will work the hell out of yourself to fill it with energy, it drains and growth can not be made. Fix the holes and you can start to harvest.

It is all about energy... If you loose energy you will gain mass, universal law and not proven false yet. You loose energy in this modern world because we are living in a constant microwave oven, exposed to things you where not designed for.

In this world today we all loose energy to the world around us, not a single living thing is safe. This is what Jacks methods will offset...

Dear Silva.
You need to go outside in the nature. Barefoot, with as little clothes as possible. Get air and sun on your skin. Swim in natural rivers/ocean/lakes, drink plenty of natural water.
Eat lots of wild seafood. Rest. Sleep in the night, have it as cool as possible. Avoid technology and fake light.
You will heal, just give it time!

Until you have gone through it, start doing what we tell you - we have all done it and do every day - you can too! It is very easy. Instead of putting your money on supplements, put them into oysters and seafood. Eat them raw.
Start with small steps if it sounds too heavy and too much of a change to do it all in one. Do step by steps. As much as you can. Just do not forget, the more you do the faster you will heal. Still, you will need lost of patience, healing does not happen in one day.... but you will not regret it, I promise you!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-travel-to-tropical-climate-after-cold-adapting-im-afraid.11366/post-135048>

I've only been at "bare skin" CT a couple weeks and I getting quite adapted. I had been surfing in cold water quite a bit but the shift came after a week of bikini dips in the ocean and nighttime cold baths. Now I am sweltering in our 65 degree summer weather. Wearing anything but a tank top is uncomfortable. What's going to happen if I go on our planned trip to Nicaragua for a several weeks? I'll be doing medical missions work so I won't be able to just hang out in the pool or the ocean. Should I find a place to work in Tibet instead? Last year when I went to Mexico I couldn't function on humid days without hiding in an air-conditioned room for a few hours in the afternoon… That was before I was cold adapted.

> In [Norse mythology](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Norse_mythology), **Freyja** ([Old Norse](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Old_Norse) the "[Lady](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lady)") is a goddess associated with love, sexuality, beauty, fertility, gold, [seiðr](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sei%C3%B0r), war, and death. Freyja is the owner of the necklace [Brísingamen](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Br%C3%ADsingamen), rides a chariot pulled by two cats, owns the boar [Hildisvíni](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hildisv%C3%ADni), possesses a cloak of [falcon](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Falcon) feathers, and, by her husband [Óðr](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/%C3%93%C3%B0r), is the mother of two daughters, [Hnoss](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hnoss) and [Gersemi](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gersemi). Along with her brother [Freyr](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Freyr) (Old Norse the "[Lord](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lord)"), her father [Njörðr](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nj%C3%B6r%C3%B0r), and her mother ([Njörðr's sister](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sister-wife_of_Nj%C3%B6r%C3%B0r), unnamed in sources), she is a member of the [Vanir](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vanir). Stemming from Old Norse _Freyja_, modern forms of the name include **Freya**, **Freija**, **Frejya**, **Freyia**, **Frøya**,**Frøjya**, **Freia**, and **Freja**.

hello

well, i discovered this site today and its one of the most interesting things ive read in a long time. i am just getting into paleo for health reasons, but i really like the whole brain health angle here and all the ideas. what got me interested initially was the thoughts on our evolution from ape to human. really fascinating stuff.

i have made an introduction yesterday on the "marks daily apple" site. its about how i started the paleo diet in hopes of healing my broken tooth. well, im learning so much about how this diet could help me, and i do have alot of issues to work on, so i think im going to begin with reading about leptin and see how i can fit in some of the other concepts to my routine.

i started paleo yesterday and since then i havent had my usual nausea and needing to lie down in the daytime. before that i was cooking from a budget cookbook with lots of white flour. and, ive tried several vegan diets in the past and found them very hard to follow. i had some success with them healthwise, but im learning that it might have been to my cost.

# Thread: is there a way to easily find a thread I've posted or one I've commented on?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-way-to-easily-find-a-thread-ive-posted-or-one-ive-commented-on.11371/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sandra — 2014-07-22T01:44:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-way-to-easily-find-a-thread-ive-posted-or-one-ive-commented-on.11371/post-135147>

... or do I have to actually 'watch' the threads I want to keep track of, or add them to my news feed?

Thanks

### sjoshua — 2014-07-22T02:16:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-way-to-easily-find-a-thread-ive-posted-or-one-ive-commented-on.11371/post-135151>

click your username in the top right, and a menu should appear.

click 'Your Profile Page'

Once there, click the 'postings' tab

You can see everyone else's stuffs the same way, you'd just have to get to their profile page (which can be done by clicking their username, under their picture, in any thread)

# Thread: lavendercat's journal :)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lavendercats-journal.11372/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 102
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### lavendercat — 2014-07-22T08:45:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lavendercats-journal.11372/post-135173>

hello

well, i came to this site yesterday and was blow away by the information here. i have been following the paleo diet for 3 days now, but i was really impressed by the "epi-paleo" diet plan and will be adapting to that as soon as i can.

i initially started this diet after i saw the posts on wellness mama about healing your teeth. i was intrigued by the idea of traditional diets, especially by drinking bone broth. i was desperate to do something about a broken tooth, as i cant afford to go to the dentist. so this was enough to start me off.

i have done the low fat vegan diets for large amounts of time, and also just the "SAD" standard diet, although i am from the uk.

i have had mental health problems all my life. this led to getting stuck in the mental health system, lots of medication, and then came the physical problems such as diabetes, hypothyroid, and obesity. thanks to this site i can see that they are all linked, so now i can begin to address the problems.

well, i have been careful to have a cup of coffee each day to try to minimise the coffee headaches. despite this, last night i started to get a headache. i went to bed at 9pm but i woke up in the night feeling very ill. i had a headache, and waves of nausea, and was very hot. i had to lay on the floor, and i was really quite worried because it seemed to me like it was almost hard to breathe. thankfully after a short while i was sick, and felt better afterwards.

i got up at 6am feeling much better. i have managed to have breakfast within 30 minutes of rising, to try to start fixing my leptin. i have chosen eggs this morning and unfortunately i didnt quite finish the whole lot before i began to feel slightly nauseous again, with a slight headache.

im not sure what is causing this. i think it might be a reaction to the new diet. anyway i am going to go and lie down. i have a feeling that i might be sick again.

### HoneyChild — 2014-07-22T12:12:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lavendercats-journal.11372/post-135178>

Welcome, I hope you can find solutions to all your problems. Because you're in the UK, you might want to look at buying oysters through this website, I've found it the cheapest and easiest way of buying them. It sounds like you really need a fill up your tank of DHA and oysters is the best way to do this, although it takes a while to get used to them.

[http://www.frozenfishdirect.co.uk/buy//oyster-11070-in-detail.html](http://www.frozenfishdirect.co.uk/buy//oyster-11070-in-detail.html)

### lavendercat — 2014-07-22T15:39:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lavendercats-journal.11372/post-135184>

thanks  and thanks for the link. i will look into that.

i went back to bed and ive just got up. i was feeling better but now ive started to feel a bit ill again. i had a look online and it seems sometimes people get this because of the amount of fat in the diet which their bodies arent used to. just a theory but it makes sense. im not sure what to eat though.

### lavendercat — 2014-07-22T19:59:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lavendercats-journal.11372/post-135206>

hmm, well im afraid i am going to have to try and transition into this more slowly. its really a shame because i can clearly see how this diet would help and where i have been making myself ill with other diets. i really thought jumping straight in would do me only good, and i was looking forward to the benefits.

but, if my body isnt used to this amount of fat to the extent where its making me puke, i need to scale back a bit and work into it slowly. i have gone back to my normal diet for now, which is basically "eat anything" while avoiding the most sugary things.

i will never do a low fat vegan diet again. i can really see the problem with those now. i think i will continue on with my budget cookbook for a bit, and then maybe try something like the "eat clean" diet, to get alot of the refined junk out. after that i will try the traditional foods/ gut healing diet, which i am interested in. then normal paleo. and then finally this one, the epi-paleo. epi-paleo seems to me like the ultimate diet, which i want to be on.

so anyway this process should gradually take the crap out of my diet while getting used to more meat and more fat slowly.

i started this paleo diet because i was in a desperate situation with my teeth and also feeling sick all the time. since reading some of the info on this site, i can clearly see that if i dont do something soon, i am headed for a disaster. already i have the diabetes/ thyroid/ obesity thing which i can see now are all linked.

so, i will be back in a while. i need to get the house in order. ive been feeling so ill lately that its a terrible state.

bye for now

### lavendercat — 2014-07-24T02:22:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lavendercats-journal.11372/post-135347>

well, after going back to my "normal" diet the sickness feeling returned today. ive had to go and lie down several times, and im not getting anything done in the house.

so, what to do ? im really not sure what the problem is or how to address it. maybe cutting out wheat would help ? or milk ? every time i look it up on google, you get people saying they feel sick all the time and their doctor has given them a proton pump inhibitor.

so, i decided to try the epi-paleo diet but with less fat. i dont know why this didnt occur to me before. i had some salmon and lettuce, with no added fat. i am going shopping tomorrow so i can get some seafood in. ive looked into it and it seems to be cheaper than meat. also i feel better about eating seafood. i feel bad about eating animals, even if i need to do it for my health.

im glad i have an excuse to do the epi-paleo diet right away.

not really relevant to this but i will bung it in anyway, im pretty sure i have been misdiagnosed with schizo-affective disorder. i was never happy with it in the first place. there are alot of symptoms i have that just dont fit in that category, unless you also assume that i have about 3 other serious mental disorders along with it.

but, ive been reading about aspergers. this actually matches ALL my symptoms, and would explain every mental health problem ive had since i was a small child. if it turns out that i do have this, then it means i have been given a bunch of anti-psychotic and anti-depressant drugs for no reason over a 5 year period. which led to more mental and physical problems than i had in the first place.

it turns out that i could sue the NHS over that. but i probably wont. i could maybe make a complaint, but i dont think i would make a dent in the amount of cluelessness.

so, ive just had a couple of eggs with some leeks. i know im not supposed to be eating late at night, but i was hungry, as i often am before i go to bed. im going to bed now and heres hoping im not violently ill in the night.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-07-24T04:58:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lavendercats-journal.11372/post-135384>

Lavender never forget your present. Enjoy the discomfort. Why?
Sometimes the wrong choices bring us to the right places. Embrace your now, you never know where it will bring you unless you live it out.

When your life stop's pleasing you.....you've lost your randomness......change it with your new creations. Chaos is what we've lost touch with when we gain comfort.

The Epi-paleo Rx is designed around our epigenetic mechanisms, and not our cultural or medical beliefs.
The foremost reason that happiness is so hard to achieve is that the universe was not designed with the comfort of human beings in mind.

I constantly seek to "unknow" things. Test everything that can be tested. As soon as you think you know something, that's when you stop questioning it. Understanding kills curiosity. Understanding kills your progress. Remain curious by asking better questions once you think you have wisdom in your pocket.

Nothing is "proven in life".

"Proven" is a dangerous word used outside of pure mathematics. We should not use this word when talking about science. Science is always tentative because it is limited by our current boundaries of understanding. This makes the truth a moving target with time. When you begin to invoke the “proven theory defense” you begin to sound like someone more interested in money and journalism than studying a natural phenomenon.

If a window of opportunity appears to you, don’t 'draw' the blinds................or do you?

With DHA in your mouth you will never draw the blinds.........you will let the sunlight in and the power of nature will heal you.

### lavendercat — 2014-07-24T06:01:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lavendercats-journal.11372/post-135399>

well, thats very interesting and i wish i could follow what you say more exactly.

unfortunately i am a comfort loving creature. i base all my daily actions on how i feel at that moment. if i feel bad mentally or physically i will do anything to feel better. thats my whole motivation for doing anything.

yes, this has led to illness. but, the only way im going to change is slowly and gradually by applying one thing at a time.

i read those articles you mentioned. they were very interesting but i didnt see an immediate solution to get my body used to the diet in some other way than doing it gradually. so, i am going to try the low fat vegan diet again, and then go from there by slowly adding more fat and protein.

i understand that my timing is all out of whack and that this is important. even now i am sat up in the middle of the night typing this. well, id like to address this but the immediate need to eat something at each meal and have it not make me sick is more pressing i feel.

### lavendercat — 2014-07-24T10:05:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lavendercats-journal.11372/post-135414>

back to the low fat vegan diet. i follow Neal Barnard's guidelines which are to eat grains, beans, veggies and fruit. there isnt much processed stuff.

oatmeal for breakfast with a handful of raisins. then i walked up to the shop to collect my thyroid meds before it gets too hot and there are more people about. it was strange being outside. im not doing myself any favours by staying indoors all the time. apart from the lack of vitamin D, it feels more and more strange when i go out now. the light is so bright, and im terrified of who i will run into.

but, the main thing for now is that i dont feel sick. that means i can get on with things that need doing in the house.

the next thing i will try is to focus on a "wholefoods" diet. basically the vegan diet but with added fat. then after that i want to look into something like the "i quit sugar" book. she seems to talk about getting rid of the "healthy sugars" like fruit juice and dried fruit. i have actually bookmarked on amazon a whole bunch of books that i can use to slowly transition to where i want to be.

but for now- i dont feel sick, and i can hopefully do some chores today.

### lavendercat — 2014-07-25T01:15:44+0300

### lavendercat — 2014-07-25T03:59:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lavendercats-journal.11372/post-135495>

hi

well, ive been looking at "i quit sugar". the first step seems to be to cut out things like fruit, dried fruit, honey etc, as well as the obvious white sugar. there is a nice, transitional type guide to it all, and it gives suggestions, like eggs on toast instead of muesli.

at first i was writing down all the recipes, but they are a little fancy and i suddenly realised i could maybe apply the principles to my budget cookbook.

so the first thing is that when making bread, dont add fruit or dried fruit. a few weeks ago i was making a soda bread every day and adding sultanas to it. also, to make it with wholemeal flour instead of white.

so, i threw out the packet of dried fruit that i had. also some candied peel that i made last week. and also some oranges that are past their best anyway. i hate wasting food, but i feel its necessary in this instance. i havent thrown out the white flour. i will probably use that, but just get wholemeal flour next time.

i feel sick again. it seems to come and go. its not too bad right now, and im trying to start on some kitchen chores. its been very hot in the uk this week, which i hate, and its not helping really. i really need to try and get ontop of all the washing up.

### lavendercat — 2014-07-25T04:21:21+0300

i am getting really angry about this and im sick of tinkering with my diet. (actually tinkering with my diet is my favorite thing to do, but right now im just desperately trying stuff to stop me feeling sick).

i just want a short term solution to stop feeling so sick, so i can get on with things that need doing in the house. after that i can start looking into my other problems and optimum diets and such.

i am sipping some water with a drop of oil of peppermint. i have read that ginger is also good.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lavendercats-journal.11372/post-135516>

right. i think im getting somewhere.

the shop doesnt open for a while, so i thought i would look into the benefits of green juice. well, one of the green juice recipes included apple cider vinegar, and this juice was good for the stomach.

hmm. interesting. it just so happens i have some apple cider vinegar. (the fermented kind, with "the mother"). so i thought i would give it a try.

put some in a glass of water and began to sip it. it seems to be helping. it is making me burp, and the burping is a feeling of relief. so, i am sipping lots of water with the ACV in.

hmm. so i look into that. its interesting because i have come across the "traditional food" thing recently and the use of fermented foods. and then theres the GAPS diet. i dont know huge amounts about it but it was something i wanted to look into, because of my mental health problems.

i was thinking maybe i should just stick with the ACV in water for now, and not drink any green juice yet, but to buy some veggies incase i get hungry.

so then i came across this article: [http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2012/03/18/mcbride-and-barringer-interview.aspx](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2012/03/18/mcbride-and-barringer-interview.aspx)

so im looking at that. i havent finished reading yet but it seems that eating plants would be detrimental at this point.

this also might explain why i have the mental health problems, if the gut and brain are linked like that. right, so if i can help my stomach a bit so im not feeling sick, this might be beneficial overall, so maybe its good that ive been feeling sick and having to address this.

### lavendercat — 2014-07-25T09:09:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lavendercats-journal.11372/post-135517>

right. so this page says what i need to do: [http://www.gaps.me/preview/?page\_id=28](http://www.gaps.me/preview/?page_id=28)

now i am not feeling so sick i can go to the shop and get some chicken to make stock.

### lavendercat — 2014-07-25T15:20:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lavendercats-journal.11372/post-135528>

feeling a bit better. ive had a good sleep. im basically just eating chicken and drinking water until i feel well enough and can get it together to wash the big pan, and then make some stock.

i dont feel sick. i am burping quite alot and it feels good when i burp. today we will probably go to the supermarket and i can buy more chicken.

### lavendercat — 2014-07-25T18:39:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lavendercats-journal.11372/post-135551>

feeling ok.

going into town was hard. its very hot outside and hot in the car. i havent been to town for quite some time and i didnt realise how much i hated it. the noise, the people everywhere, the buildings, the cars

got some chicken and fish. i am intimidated by a whole chicken so i got drumsticks. all the fish seemed to be in some kind of sauce, so i got pink salmon in cans.

by the time we got back i was in serious "emergency mode". i wasnt feeling sick but felt very strange and ill. perhaps some kind of "die off" thing from all the cider vinegar. anyway i quickly shoved my food in the fridge and freezer and went to bed.

feeling ok now. need to eat.

### lavendercat — 2014-07-26T00:09:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lavendercats-journal.11372/post-135563>

still feeling ok. slight headache.

spending most of my time in bed, either sleeping or just lying there. just lying there is unusual for me- usually i feel i have to get up and start looking for information or do something about whatever is bothering me that day.

couple of things i notice: im not so itchy. always in recent years i itch. not a major thing but slightly annoying. but this seems to not be the case today.

also it seems my usual sense of urgency is gone. i feel more laid back.

also there is a big sense of relief. not just mentally but physically.

i am eating chicken when i get hungry, and drinking water. i am slightly worried that i might get constipated so i will look into that now.

its interesting, what im doing reminds me of the "dukan diet" that i tried a few years ago. this is a weight loss diet that consists mostly of eating meat. its quite contraversial and of course the low fat vegan people say its the worst thing you could ever do. but its intriguing that i seem to be doing something similar now. i have the book so im going to look at it later i think.

### lavendercat — 2014-07-26T01:04:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lavendercats-journal.11372/post-135567>

looked into constipation. i think it is going to be an issue.

i need to get on and make some bone broth, and put veggies into it. most of my adult life i have had to make sure i had some kind of fruit or vegetables in my diet to keep regular. a few years ago i was put onto an injectable for my diabetes and i had to stop taking it because of the incredibly painful constipation (which was misdiagnosed as a UTI at first).

so, im just getting ready to tidy up in the kitchen and do some dishes. i wont be able to do them all tonight but i can make a start. to be honest i would rather just laze around but i need to get onto making the broth. the GAPS diet doesnt say "eat only chicken", but i have found doing this invaluable while i get into a position where i can make some broth.

### lavendercat — 2014-07-26T02:11:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lavendercats-journal.11372/post-135574>

hi  its midnight here in the uk  im going to bed soon.

i dont have that book yet, no.

just did some cleaning up in the kitchen. im working my way through "1001 albums to hear before you die" and have got up to "bringing it all back home" by bob dylan. i really like it, and listened to a few tracks while i tidied up.

i definately feel a bit more stable mentally, but its early days. i am still reacting strongly to everything. earlier on i really felt like my mind was going downhill, but after sleeping i feel better.

its such a suprise to me to find that all my problems are linked. this is a message that is just not getting out there. i think alot of people dont feel great but they soldier on for years. its only when you get really ill that you are forced to take action. well, the doctors are worse than bloody useless. theres the prescription meds and the over the counter meds, which are crap and often make the problem worse while adding other serious problems. and then, if you're a bit out there you might even try to solve it yourself- at which point you run into all the low fat vegans and raw vegans. !! it can take years and years to battle through all the misinformation, and some people dont have that sort of time.

### lavendercat — 2014-07-26T05:44:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lavendercats-journal.11372/post-135612>

just got up at 3,30 am. ive been sleeping alot these last few days.

really feeling quite good. very slight headache.

its brilliant not to have to eat all the time. i was a little hungry before i went to bed but it didnt feel urgent and i couldnt be bothered to eat. with carbs, i would have had to eat, or be painfully hungry and unable to sleep.

my boyfriend is having a rough night. he's not been well this week. he doesnt have any health problems other than sometimes being stiff in the morning and sometimes feeling tired. he hasnt been ill for quite a while. not sure what it is- first he was very stiff and had some dizziness.... then a headache and now he's got a sore throat. he's tossing and turning in bed and seems to have some mucus.

well, once i have myself sorted out a bit more i can start working on his diet. he seems to like this idea. i will do it gradually like i was trying to do with mine. he likes the meals in the budget cookbook, so thats a good place to start. his diet isnt brilliant, but he seems to be able to eat most things without a problem. he is a fussy eater, very slim, and doesnt eat alot. he doesnt like alot of fat in his food. most of the time when i dont cook he will eat 4 pieces of toast.

i think the reason my health problems flared up so much recently is that i began this budget cookbook. well, at the start of the book there are all these homemade breads, made with cheap white flour. i was making sometimes two loaves a day and eating bread all day long before i got round to making something else. my mum is coeliac and this might explain why i got so ill when i did.

### lavendercat — 2014-07-26T15:49:30+0300

i feel completely different today. not sure how exactly but its like my awareness is different.

my tooth is still playing up. it feels a bit sore. since i only have half a tooth there it is probably decaying. i am waiting for a form through the post that i can fill in to try and get help with paying since we are on a low income. i cant get my teeth done until this happens. i really hope its not going to get any worse.

i have a strange sort of itchy feeling on my arms. the skin looks a bit flakey and dry.

anyway i feel like i need to lie down again.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-07-26T16:53:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lavendercats-journal.11372/post-135628>

You leave many clues why you're felling badly. The tooth being a big issue. You need to act on these things to get well. Nothing life ever stays the same, so should you. The slower you adapt the worse you're going to continue to feel. If you're brave enough to say goodbye to things harming your health, life will reward you with a new hello. Things change, friends leave. Life is constant motion, so begin to live that way.

Ironically, most people try to stay the same, doing what they have always done and wondering why their life is the way it is........they live the same way 80 times over in a year and call it a life.

This site is the antidote for that bad thinking. You need to grow bolder based upon what you have put in this journal so far. I am not being harsh or critical. I am responding to what I see, read, and sense.

I try to become a good egg for people in my life. Many modern humans need help in "rewilding" themselves from the zoo life they believe they must live. Sooner or later they will welcome you back for assisting their formerly unfortunate life with common sense. There is always time to change your trajectory. Once you do extend that hand to other unfortunate people who have yet to realize they have built walls around them instead of a bridge or a helicopter pad for others to reach them.

well, im doing what i can.

the cat has just been sick again. the floor needs cleaning. i guess i will try to do that after the dishes. i want to change her diet too. we give her dry food at the moment and also wet food out of the pouches. i know how bad it is for them.

there are quite a few flies in here. they are driving me nuts. i really need to clean the house. it is such a mess.

just had some prawns. i am going to go and lie down again. the flies are driving me mad and theres a strange sort of pressure feeling in my head.

i am not eating much. i am a bit sick of eating meat all the time and also im just not as hungry. im very worried that im going to quickly lose alot of weight  but if i go back to eating bread im going to feel nauseous again.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-07-26T17:31:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lavendercats-journal.11372/post-135632>

You must begin to tap the power of persistence. The more seafood and oysters (DHA) you eat the more persistent change becomes in your life. For example consider this: Harry Bernstein wrote 40 books but destroyed the manuscripts after they were all rejected by publishers. Bernstein was 93 when his wife of 67 years died and started writing to help deal with his grief. He spent 3 years writing his memoir "The Invisible Wall."

He sent the manuscript to many New York publishers, all of whom turned it down. Then he sent it to an editor at Random House in the UK, where it sat in a pile of unsolicited manuscripts for a year before it was read by a stunned publishing director who immediately recognized it as inspired writing. It was published in 2007 when Mr. Bernstein was 98. He would write three more acclaimed books, all of which were published, up until his death at 101. Bernstein called his 90s, "the most productive years of my life."

so i got up. im going to carry on with the dishes now.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-07-26T17:59:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lavendercats-journal.11372/post-135634>

That is a great start. When your life stop's pleasing you.....you've lost your randomness......change it with your new creations. Chaos is what we've lost touch with when we gain comfort. This is why I always tell those changing embrace the discomfort. Comfort got you to where you are now.......it is not your friend. You must re-wild yourself.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-07-26T18:01:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lavendercats-journal.11372/post-135635>

Unless you are prepared to give up something valuable you will never be able to truly change at all, because you'll be forever in the control of things you can't give up.......don't be afraid of life's chaos. Embrace it. What we call chaos today is just patterns we haven't recognized yet. Once you get enough DHA in your cell membranes in your head you will see that chaos turn to common sense. This is very counterintuitive for people.......and it is why many newbies come here they think I am harsh arrogant or an asshole. I must get you off your current mark to get you to your optimal best.

I don't see how I can write anything of value if I don't offend someone. Don't change your mind just because people are offended; change your mind if you're wrong. Learning isn't acquiring knowledge so much as it is trimming information that has already been acquired........I love being an arborist for my tree of life. I want you to realize that too.

i think one of my issues as well with pushing myself is not knowing when to stop. not so much recently but over the past 20 years ive had this thing where i would get completely obsessed with something for about 4 days. for that time i would eat, sleep and breathe the subject. everything would be related to it. under those circumstances, when im obsessed with something i can function at a hugely advanced pace than i normally do. i do things gladly that i would never usually even contemplate. ive learnt to recognize that crazed feeling that im becoming obsessed and i need to back down and try to relax. otherwise i will crash and burn.

i think this will become less of an issue as my mind gets better. im hoping to get more awareness about whats really going on.

### caroline — 2014-07-27T06:33:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lavendercats-journal.11372/post-135692>

We want our supplements in their natural package!

Can you buy cans of sardines? Mackerel? Oysters? Mussels?

Once you have your broths you cn just toss in a few veggies if you want.

You have a lot of things roaming around in your head..... Maybe make a list .... And then prioritise the list.

It is essential to get some seafood and bone broths in you.....

it seems that circadian rhythms are very important. so i might try and learn more about that. i found this book:

then once ive read that, they've done one about the seasons as well.

then im going to look into seasonal eating, and regional eating. my dad will be pleased to hear about this.

my dad is a gardener. he does it for his job and his hobby. he does quite alot of the things that Dr Kruse mentions already, because he doesnt really like the modern world. he lives on a small farm, and for the last 35 years has worked for the people who own the farm.

he doesnt have a computer, a mobile phone, or even a landline phone. he goes to bed at 9pm and gets up at 6, and has done so since i can remember. he doesnt like to use electricity. at most he will have the radio on. when it gets dark in the winter he will often light a candle rather than turn the light on. he doesnt use excess heating in winter. if he doesnt have visitors he will only use the coal fire. every day he is outside working in the garden. (he gets bored in winter though).

he is very healthy. the only problem he's had is that he smoked rollups since the age of 9, and had to give up recently because he had a very bad chest infection. also while i was growing up he suffered quite badly with ulcers. these went away when he got double pneumonia. i think the thing that killed the pneumonia might have killed off what was causing the ulcers.

when i went to stay a few years ago, he refused to cater to my vegan diet  i have written to him about the new diet and i think he will be pleased that im no longer vegan.

### NeilBB — 2014-07-31T02:38:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lavendercats-journal.11372/post-136210>

"The truth will set you free, but first it will make you miserable." -James A. Garfield

"We swallow greedily any lie that flatters us, but we sip only little by little at truth we find bitter." -Denis Diderot

It's human nature to recoil from painful discoveries. The question is what will a person do once the dust settles.

Wholesale change can take time. One of the problems with joining this site now as opposed to say a year ago is that there is so much info here now that is so counterintuitive to most people and so divergent from mainstream dogma that it really would be completely normal for a newcomer to become overwhelmed and even angry about it and to lash out as an initial reaction, especially for someone who is ill. But I think a person really has to ask himself/herself what attracted me here in the first place and whether that attraction is still valid.

"Am I considering leaving because I really consider the information faulty, or because I just don't understand it all yet, or simply because it makes me uncomfortable and I don't want to deal with it." Big difference there.

Joining these forums as a newbie and trying to keep up with some of these folks could easily be a bit like jumping onto a NASCAR race track riding a bicycle. I do sympathize.

# Thread: The Bermuda Triangle

**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 33
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PaulG — 2014-07-24T04:55:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-bermuda-triangle.11381/post-135382>

Lying on the grass this morning looking up at the clouds I wondered what was happening in the place on the exact opposite side of the planet and then I started thinking about quantum tunneling, spooky action at distance, wormholes etc which frankly ruined my back to nature moment.

Back indoors I used this website [http://www.antipodesmap.com](http://www.antipodesmap.com) to find my quantum twin location and what was it..! The flipping Bermuda Triangle which perhaps explains a lot about my current circumstances. lol.

Well to be accurate but less dramatic it is just north of Bermuda so outside the triangle.

I then recalled Jack was going to write about the Triangle but I don't think it happened.

See
[http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.ph...-north-of-2-trillion-dollars.8179/#post-95315](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/if-the-1859-emf-event-happens-today-the-costs-would-be-north-of-2-trillion-dollars.8179/#post-95315)

### nonchalant — 2014-07-24T04:59:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-bermuda-triangle.11381/post-135385>

Wow, Paul, neat! Do you have sudden appearances of ships, planes, people?

If mass and energy is "top notch" in its relationship to one another, can we change how we see and feel time? I think so...

Time links to mass, energy, and both tie to DHA. No one seems to see it as I do yet. I think it will change. I told you long ago in a blog that time was a function of mass. Now I told you DHA is the most favorable way to alter the thermodynamics of mass. What I'm describing here without you realizing it yet.......is consciousness. Time can and does change. Consciousness is what changes it. This is why time when you are young feels longer than time when you are old. Quantum time is a function of the quality and quantity of tunneling of electrons in the cytochromes. Quantum time determines decision time and error rate in our actions and behaviors. Both typically increase whenever the difference that must be discriminated is reduced. So a failure of tunneling of electrons leads to poor decision making, abhorring change, altering dopamine levels in your frontal lobes, and due to a lack of DHA in cell membranes, dehydration in cells, and a lack of proper ketosis or the chronic use of technology and or blue light devices.

Light is at a constant speed everywhere in the universe at 186,000 mph. Space is a vaccuum where matter is sparse. But what happens when light meets matter? Light slows. This means space and time are relative to your position in life, because light slows depending upon what it interacts with. In other words, light always moves but space and time bend, distort, and react to light when light interacts with any matter. When light can not move all time stands still. How does light stand still? In a sun it happens when the electric or magnetic field or both work together to contain a photon within the sun. Might the same thing happen in you mitochondria? When matter interacts with light heat is liberated. So is time a thermal phenomena. It gets more interesting when you realize light released from a mitochondria interacts with water around the MINOS. Some forms of matter interact with it more than others. No all matter is as sticky to light. Water and DHA are two things that interact with light in quite different manners. Water absorbs IR heat best. DHA captures electrons and photons.

Light is the only thing with a constant relationship to time. Einstein said lights speed is constant with respect to time and this means space and time must bend in relation to light. Light can be a wave or particle. That particle in light is called an electron and the wave form is called a photon. DHA captures both parts of light in a cell membrane. Light is an electromagnetic disturbance that define’s time presence. Therefore DHA levels are tied to how you perceive and sense time. When light hits any matter its speed changes and time slows from 186,000. That slowing of light allows time to emerge to any observer. The type of matter it hits reveals time to the observer. This makes time relative to all observers. There is no cause and effect because cause and effect is a property of how light interacts with matter. This is why time is a function of mass. All matter has mass. Light has no mass when it is released and allowed to interact.

There is always time, when time is yours.

Time needs light to be moving in order for it to emerge.................Now you may be seeing why during darkness we sleep.......and time seems to vanish from the perception of our electromagnetic computer.

Time is a measure of change in the universe. So why is time arrow moving forward, and not backward? Time arrow follows entropy........from low entropy to a high entropy state..........change implies metastability. When things are at equilibrium time stands still...........in death is the only time humans reach equilibrium. In death time stands still. Is time a thermal statistic? All life liberates heat after interacting with light. All life dies when light is no longer liberated from mitochondria..........so heat drop and a cadaver becomes cold.

Intriguing confusion huh? Or maybe an amazing idea?

The earlier link I posted was chapter 2 of an online book

Better links I have subsequently found

**Homepage of TOPOLOGICAL GEOMETRODYNAM**
[http://www.tgdtheory.fi](http://www.tgdtheory.fi)

**Overview and Introduction in concept map format (for visual learners and people wanting quickish overview)**
[http://www.tgdtheory.fi/cmaphtml.html](http://www.tgdtheory.fi/cmaphtml.html)

**Main website of Matti Pitkanen for his online book Quantum TGD**
[http://www.tgdtheory.fi/public\_html/tgdeeg/tgdeeg.html](http://www.tgdtheory.fi/public_html/tgdeeg/tgdeeg.html)

[http://www.emergentmind.org](http://www.emergentmind.org)

> This site has been set up as an open venture between scientists, scholars, meditators and all
> those who believe that we are approaching a conceptual threshold in our understanding of how
> physics, physiology and consciousness interact.
>
> For the past few decades, experimental evidence has been accumulating in almost all areas of
> science that non-local and mind-directed effects upon the physical world are more ubiquitous than
> we had so far cared to admit. As institutional research becomes more open about these matters,
> a theoretical framework is beginning to emerge. We may have, at this point, a vague idea about
> the extraordinary potential lying in store for humanity, but we are still very much at a loss about
> how to approach this "new science".
>
> The East has the benefit of thousands of years of experience in this field, but the language of its
> investigations seems so intuitive, so subjective, that it is almost untranslatable in scientific jargon.
> And yet, translate we will have to: the greatest challenge lying ahead in the decades to come will be
> the design of appropriate experiments and "test particles" in order to explore the strange geometry of
> the mind-matter continuum.
>
> We believe that both East and West can benefit from joining efforts at this point. Such steps have
> already been taken (for example, by the repeated meetings between the Dalai Lama and western
> scientists, as described in the [Mind-Life series](<http://www.emergentmind.org/other_websites.htm#The Mind and Life Institute>)). If this endeavor is approached with the right mix of
> humility, awe and determination (as opposed to the arrogance that has characterized much of our
> scientific progress), we believe that the goals of both traditions can come to fruition: for a persuasive
> proof of the "holographic paradigm" is most likely to tear down the barriers of fear and cynicism and
> facilitate a mass transition toward the ideals of compassion and spiritual fulfillment.
>
> However, in order to build a new culture, we must first construct its language. To that aim, we have
> devised this site with the following functions in mind:
>
>
> -   To make investigators with different backgrounds familiar with the questions, impasses and research
>     directions being followed in other fields of study.
>
> -   To provide an archive of articles pertinent to the scientific research of mind-matter connections.
>
> -   To provide a forum for the exchange of ideas between scientists, scholars and all those who feel
>     they may be able to offer practical insights into this question.
>
> -   Last, but not least, to contribute to the development of that critical mass necessary to precipitate
>     the new paradigm shift.

There are so many layers to this existence, and most people are living in only one. If that.

When you get Tensegrity 4 Monday you will see why the CT protocol is easy to understand. Just consider this answer to Paul on CT 12: Paul asks on the CT 12 blog: Does CT help in summer? Paul cold works all the time because of its effects on matter in us. We are made of protein polymers. Magnetism acts to condense these protein polymers in us. This saves us energy. Anything that saves us energy makes us more healthy. Magnetism has a huge effects on electrons. Electrons control the phenotype of how chemistry works everywhere in the universe. This includes your cells. Magnetism acts like a giant invisible circuit board for the currents emitted from DHA. Magnetism is tightly coupled to cold temperatures naturally. Colder temperatures increase the strength of a magnets and their magnetic field. Why is that? Cold can have an effect on magnetism, because cold can have an effect of how fast or slow electrons move. This links the sun’s power to the magnetic field of the Earth no matter what season you are in Paul. It also links the Schumann resonance strength to the sun. When the sun is shining, the Schumann resonance is strongest. When the sun is shining the Earth’s magnetic field is weakest. This implies that the Schumann strength varies inversely with the magnetic field’s strength. At night time, the magnetic field is stronger on Earth than it is when the sun is out.

When you consider how that group uses non native EMF more than any group I know, I raise my concerns. I do it not to be a prick.......I care about them missing a key point. People do not care how much you know until they realize how much you care. Nora Volkow shows this effect in her latest research. Otto Warburg, Russ Adey, Allen Frey are others who have shown the effect. In fact, in most cancers with a redox shifted Q cycle, Warburg found that they also prefer carbs to massive exclusion. The paleo oncologist Colin Champ has also one of the few sane voices in this debate. It began with the foolishness of some of the paleo leaders and the safe starches debate and the latest version is now present in new forms. In an infant its unmyelinated growing brain wants ketones to myelinate and finish neuronal growth. The immune system expands its power using ketones. this is why autoimmunity is so prevalent. It is also why women are more affected by AI's. They have less myelin to protect them from non native EMF. Children are also at extreme risks because of the lack of myelination. This happens to be why they are now getting adult diseases and things like Autism at alarming rates. Starvation is a survival story and is a ketone story. The context in biology is always the background for environmental drivers. These drivers are what drives epigenetic signaling. @Caloriesproper and myself have made this point over and over again. ### nonchalant — 2014-09-01T22:16:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kruses-theorum.11382/post-139565>

I'm enjoying reading Tensegrity 4, but one paragraph has stopped me cold. Jack's statement that 'Cavities amplify standing waves' has really ... um, struck a chord with me. I have become convinced that I can occasionally "see" the effects of the Schumann resonance, though I'm having difficulty reliably reproducing the effect. Please hear me out. After being outside in the morning sunshine for a while and then going inside to a dark room, I can sometimes see a fluttering in the periphery of my vision. The effect lasts just a few seconds, and then all is back to normal.

I had experienced this effect just a couple times, wondering if it were some strange health thing, and then I bought a Schumann resonator, the Total Shield. It has a small red light that blinks to the time of the resonator. I had the device for a week or so, when one morning I woke up and while in the bathroom (where the T.S. was located) I felt like my entire body was resonating to the time of this device. My vision was fluttering as well. I got weirded out, and unplugged it. But I kept 'vibrating' for about 15 minutes. I returned the device. A few months later, though, I saw this same fluttering (no body vibration, though) again, and I recognized it. It was much weaker than what I got with the Total Shield.

Since then I have been trying to reproduce it, with very limited success. One time I saw it when I was still outside, facing the sun, but with my eyes closed. It only lasted a few seconds again.

I also often see water quivering to a similar frequency, but I'll stop before I lose all credibility.

### Paul S — 2014-07-24T19:17:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rain-soft-ro-filter-feedback-please.11385/post-135466>

I moved into a new home recently and DW and I are looking at water filtration and softener systems. One is the Rain Soft EC4 softener system (removes chlorine, and other contaminants as well), but they are also proposing adding the Rain Soft Ultrarefiner II Reverse Osmosis filtration system. It is mounted under the kitchen sink, and only runs RO water through the sink faucet and within the RO filtration system, it is not a whole house system. I asked about pipe damage and they indicated since it is direct mounted to a single source (the kitchen sink), it won't damage the plumbing in the home and it also removes flouride, chlorine and other contaminants. Since we currently buy bottled RO water (5 gallon jugs), this system would be a cost saver in the near term/long term.

Does anyone have any experiecne with their RO system or claims? If I recall, on this Forum (I tried searching, but could not find the old posts/threads on it), the main concern was plumbing damage in a home? Anyone with experience with their claims or would recommend/not recommend either RO system or softener?

# Thread: Irisin and Vitamin D.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/irisin-and-vitamin-d.11391/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2014-07-25T16:43:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/irisin-and-vitamin-d.11391/post-135544>

I am not sure why this study has made waves.......irisin is the CT hormone........when it is cold you should not see high levels of vitamin D naturally. Irisin converts WAT to BAT for those of you who do not know. It put beta 3 agonists on laser beam activation to burn FFA as pure heat.

In a new study, researchers found that vitamin D supplementation has no effect on levels of a relatively newly discovered hormone called irisin.

Irisin is a hormone secreted from muscle in response to exercise that was isolated and discovered in 2012. Researchers believe it may be involved in the beneficial effects of exercise and have suggested various roles it may have in overall health.

One effect of irisin is to convert white fat (the type of fat that stores calories) to brown fat (the type of fat that helps burn calories). Researchers have also found that the hormone may help improve glucose tolerance and increase insulin production, suggesting a role for it in the management of diabetes.

Irisin, then, appears to share some of the same effects on health as vitamin D. Research shows vitamin D to have beneficial effects on muscle strength, glucose tolerance, and insulin production.

Recognizing the similarities between the effects of vitamin D and irisin, a research team from France and Belgium recently conducted a study to determine whether vitamin D and irisin interact with each other in the body.

This was a small pilot study in which the researchers recruited 29 healthy young adults. The participants were given a single dose of 100,000 IU of vitamin D and were monitored over the course of four weeks.

The research team were curious if vitamin D supplementation would lead to an increase in irisin levels, possibly revealing a new interaction and mechanism for vitamin D in glucose tolerance, insulin, and muscular strength.

So, the researchers administered the supplements and measured vitamin D and irisin levels at baseline, 3, 7, 15, and 28 days. Here’s what they found:

-   Baseline vitamin D levels increased from a of 19.8 ng/ml to 29.7 ng/ml at 3 days and to 33 ng/ml at 7 days.
-   Vitamin D status decreased significantly between 7 and 28 days.
-   There was no significant change in irisin levels over the course of the study period.
-   One participant experienced an increase in irisin that resulted in 3-fold higher irisin levels than the study population average.

The researchers concluded,

“In this study, we failed to show that a single large (100,000 IU) dose of vitamin D increased the circulating levels of irisin in young healthy individuals.”

The researchers note that not all of the participants were deficient in vitamin D at baseline, which may have impacted their results. Furthermore, a consistent and physiological dose of vitamin D given daily over the course of 4 weeks may lead to different results compared to a single, large dose.

There was no placebo group to compare supplementation to, meaning we do not for sure that vitamin D has no effect on circulating irisin levels.

While this study failed to show an interaction between vitamin D and irisin, further research is warranted. As has been suggested with trials on vitamin D and specific health conditions, vitamin D may only influence production of irisin in individuals with abnormal irisin levels.

This was the first study to evaluate whether vitamin D interacts with this newly discovered hormone. Further trials are needed to fully explore the potential role for vitamin D and irisin and to determine if an interaction exists between these hormones in those with relevant health conditions, such as diabetes.

**Source**

[Cavalier, E. et al. Evaluation of circulating irisin levels in healthy young individuals after a single 100,000 IU vitamin D dose. Annals of Endocrinology, 2014.](http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0003426614000717)

Healthy young adults, but baseline vitamin D averaged 19.8 ng/ml? Perhaps this was a wintertime study? That would explain the outlier. He had been outside building snowmen and making snow angels.

It's easy to see the link between CT and irisin. Its interesting developing muscles by just sitting in water.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-07-25T17:22:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-surgery-and-dha.11392/post-135546>

I did several brain surgeries and the families wanted to know how best for their loved one to recover. My response was simple for the NON GEEKS. I responded, "become cold dark and boring and learn to sleep well." They asked me how scientifically sleep affected the surgery. Then I gave them the GEEK answer: The reduced temperature induced by melatonin in sleep is needed for Central Nervous System's autophagic repair, for another, less well known reason. The lowered temperature sets the stage for the biologic quantum effects to be optimal on our neurons microtubules that facilitate learning and neuronal spouting that occurs brain-wide. When you are cold dark and boring eat lots of fish. It is the band aid of neurons. They are now interested in the blog.

### caroline — 2014-07-26T02:02:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-surgery-and-dha.11392/post-135572>

I have been thinking a lot about "words" the last couple of days.

You have said that you focus like a Laser..... and you do - in person and on your podcasts. You are extremely direct - you get in and get the job done.

I suppose this is your medical training - when dealing with patients and their families.....
you have to identify the problem quickly, come up with a workable solution, explain in words us mere mortals can understand and also the prognosis in these same kinds of words ... all the while keeping your emotions at -0 .... but also having some level of compassion.

And now ..... you get to add another couple of layers ..... get cold dark and boring and eat fish!

This maybe my biggest take away from jackkruse.com ........be direct, say what you think in simple terms, mean what you say.

In other words - live your life .....cut the crap.

I feel so much better now that I can say ...WTF .... it is direct and there is no mistaking what I mean. there aren't a bunch of words that I am hiding behind.

These three words change everything .......

at first I thought it has nothing to do with QED .... but of course it has everything to do with it. I am discovering my own n=1.

This has been a steep learning curve for me ......and I love every second!

I am also able to get a nice shock value! I have been too nice for too long .... and so when I say WTF - I get shock .... and then gales of laughter. There is no mistaking what I mean!

For a change and probably the first time ever - I am real....

### Josh — 2014-08-08T22:10:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-surgery-and-dha.11392/post-137314>

[http://jn.nutrition.org/content/early/2014/07/30/jn.114.193854.abstract](http://jn.nutrition.org/content/early/2014/07/30/jn.114.193854.abstract)

> **Abstract**
> Modifiable lifestyle changes, including dietary changes, could translate into a great reduction in the global burden of cognitive impairment and dementia. Few studies evaluated the benefits of fish intake for delaying cognitive decline, and no studies were conducted in a Chinese population, which may differ with respect to types, amounts, and correlates of fish consumption compared with Western populations. We hypothesized that higher consumption of fish would predict slower decline in cognitive function, independent of a wide range of potential confounders. This prospective cohort study comprised 1566 community-dwelling adults aged ≥55 y who completed a cognitive screening test at ≥2 waves of the China Health and Nutrition Survey in 1997, 2000, or 2004, with a mean follow-up of 5.3 y \[age at entry (mean ± SD): 63 ± 6 y\]. Diet was measured by 3-d 24-h recalls at baseline. Outcomes included repeated measures of global cognitive scores (baseline mean ± SD: 19 ± 6 points), composite cognitive Z-scores (standardized units), and standardized verbal memory scores (standardized units). Multivariable-adjusted linear mixed-effects models were used to evaluate the relation of fish intake with changes in cognitive scores. Age was found to significantly modify the association between fish consumption and cognitive change (_P_ = 0.007). Among adults aged ≥65 y, compared with individuals who consumed <1 serving/wk (i.e., 100 g) fish, the mean annual rate of global cognitive decline was reduced by 0.35 point (95% CI: 0.13, 0.58) among those consuming ≥1 serving/week, equivalent to the disparity associated with 1.6 y of age. Fish consumption was also associated with a slower decline in composite and verbal memory scores. No associations were observed among adults aged 55–64 y. Our findings suggest a potential role of fish consumption as a modifiable dietary factor to reduce the rate of cognitive decline in later life.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lloyd-morgan-interview-from-electricsense-com-limited-access-dont-tarry.11393/post-135552>

So, this is who he is:

My name is Lloyd Morgan. I am an electronic engineer by training, a Director of the Central Brain Tumor Registry of the United States (see [www.cbtrus.org](http://www.cbtrus.org/)) a member of the international science group, the Bioelectromagnetics Society (see [www.bioelectromagnetics.org](http://www.bioelectromagnetics.org/)) and a volunteer with the National Brain Tumour Foundation (see[www.braintumor.org](http://www.braintumor.org/)). Since 1995 I have devoted myself to the study of electromagnetic fields and health problems. With such a focus in mind I have attended, and sometimes presented at, multiple science meetings each year including, among others, those of the Bioelectromagnetics Society, the Neuro-Oncology Society, the American Academy of Environmental Medicine and the Brain Tumor Epidemiology Consortium.

[http://instantteleseminar.com/?eventid=57881091](http://instantteleseminar.com/?eventid=57881091)

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2014-07-27T01:57:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lloyd-morgans-website-another-ee-convert.11394/post-135665>

ok let me just write it here

so my colleague was seeing a you guy in the clinic, he is 31 years old, and works for the military
he presented to her with a sudden onset numb arm with pain in the R side neck
she immediately tested for thoracic outlet syndrome, found some clinical tests positive and treated accordingly
she had not been able to make much change and sent him for MRI as well as ultrasound of the subclavian artery, carotids and vertebral artery, all clear
he has been coming back a bit, sometimes better, sometimes not, so she referred him to me
this guy gives an amazing history, he is able to describe every sensation he experiences so well and so clearly
so recently he has been getting really bad at work, and better when not at work
it has been happening every day, and to the point where his CNV III goes numb, he has trouble articulating words, and feels like his face is melting on the left, as well as blurry vision, sometimes a funny sensation in ear, and the arm numbness
it is essentially a troke presentation without stroke
currently i am calling it migraine presenting as stroke because it is transient, and he says he feels like something gets "squished" in the left supraclavicular fossa
......
so. last week he texts me, says he just walked into the shopping mall, and felt explosive phenomena as described
couldnt talk, face and arm numb, woozy and hard to see out of left eye
he is always tired, sleep is never restful, he has a smart meter on his house, works in a room of 300 computers in a military base, and needs a laser retinal scan every time he goes into work for security measured

i believe he is getting acute calcium efflux and vasospasm of the left subclavian artery affecting both his arm and his head...
waiting on more scans and an EMG, but because it is transient and environmental i am not sure if they will find anything
