### cinnamon — 2015-10-21T23:52:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cinnamons-journal.11447/post-180101>

Earlier today someone posted a story on the Quantum Health Facebook page about a mom giving her one year old melatonin to help with poor sleep. Jack responded: “And this is why you got to run from a relationship with people with no dopamine in their brain.”

So, my best friend for nearly the past 20 years has been showing ever increasing signs of low dopamine levels. I read through the symptoms and he exhibits quite a few. Only mildly depressed during winter, though – he has a tendency to be manic during longer light cycles. He is clearly impacted by light and seasons. This summer was the worst yet and if I told you the story of the last few months you would have a hard time believing it is all true. His behaviour is the biggest storm in my personal weather system.

I have decided that the stress of dealing with this cannot continue indefinitely. It makes me quite sad, but I can’t let my life be all about dealing with this. It is going to take some time and finesse to extricate myself from the situation. We are entangled in several ways, not the least of which is financially, as we own a house together. There are several decisions that have to be made that will necessarily involve him and it would be helpful if there was clearer thinking. I am wondering if there are some “stealth ninja” (thanks for that idea, scompy!) ways to help him improve his dopamine levels, at least in the relatively short term?

In particular, I have observed that he is always in a problem solving mode, but not seeing the big picture. So, everything and everyone is an obstacle or an opportunity. He can be quite manipulative and aggressive in this state. Also, he is easily distracted so his focus is off. I don’t think he has any perspective on how he is from where he is, so it is difficult to discuss with him.

After we get the house ready for sale and move out, I should be sufficiently disentangled that the choices he makes will not be my problem any longer. Because of promises that have been made to others that are also impacted, this is unlikely to be earlier than next summer.

I see that there is light on the other side of this, but I am going to need some strength to navigate my way. Any suggestions to help me cope would be very much appreciated!

love,
Michelle

### caroline — 2015-10-23T10:30:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cinnamons-journal.11447/post-180239>

tough situation Michelle - maybe you will have to manufacture a situation - like money related - to get his full attention?

### cinnamon — 2015-10-23T18:15:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cinnamons-journal.11447/post-180268>

Hi Audrey!

I think I have his attention. And the situation is already there without any manufacturing! LOL I'm just wanting to find some practical ways to help him keep his focus and to see the big picture when we are going through creating and executing the plan to disentangle. If he can do that, it will reduce the stress level for me and others involved. I realize I can't change him - only he can do that.

I think applying Dr. Kruse's protocols should improve his dopamine levels naturally. And he is open to a lot of what Dr. K. has to say! But even though he knows better, he has a tough time doing better. I suppose that it is a function of low dopamine, but when he has a choice to make between short term pleasure seeking behaviour and doing the right thing for his long term well being, he will exhibit a bit of anxiety over it and then choose pleasure almost every time. His choices for pleasure include playing music (jamming with a band) and drinking alcohol. (Sometimes at the same time! LOL) Now I don't think either of those things is inherently bad, but if you end up staying up until all hours and screwing up your sleep because of them, when you are already in a dopamine deprived state, they are not the best choice. Yes, they give you a "quick hit" of dopamine and that is why you are compelled to choose them. But your brain and thinking gets worse and worse over time without proper sleep.

He even understands all of what I just said - if you discuss it with him before it starts escalating. And if the behaviour was just some drinking and playing music, that would be okay by me. But as his thinking deteriorates, his personality changes and he gets into a "game playing" mode that becomes very stressful to be around. This year was the worst ever. That's why, even though I love him very much, I have to get out. As I said before, his behaviour is the biggest storm in my personal weather system. He is standing between me and optimal. And nobody gets to do that indefinitely.

### Optimalbound — 2015-11-16T17:56:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cinnamons-journal.11447/post-182076>

> [LBK said:](/goto/post?id=179137)
>
> I would like to know what you find! For now, I'm going with glasses.

Today I've gotten a couple of packs of [Dailies Total1 lenses](http://www.dailies.com/products/dailies-total1.shtml), which on paper look quite promising. They do not have any UV filtering added, and are supposedly six times more permeable to oxygen than normal contacts. Data sheet [here](http://ecatalog.alcon.com/CL/DailiesTotal1_us_en.pdf).

I also handed a copy of Ubi-24 to my optician. As could be expected, she was puzzled by my asking for the _least_ UV-blocking lenses, but seemed open-minded and intrigued about it, and said she'll read the blog with interest.

I will of course be using glasses most of the time, but I'm not quite ready to give up lenses for activities such as scuba diving or skiing, where glasses are simply too impractical and the amount of UV exposure is either very low or too much (in my n=1 experience), anyway.

### cinnamon — 2015-11-27T20:50:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cinnamons-journal.11447/post-182751>

Yesterday, I went to a tanning salon for the first time ever.

There was this gorgeous looking bed there that promised red light AND UV! It was called the Angel something or other and apparently there are only 2 in all of Canada!  It was pretty expensive, so I resisted the sales pitch (though I enjoyed hearing all about the benefits of red light) and bought some minutes on the lowest level sunbed, after being assured it had UVB.

They told me that I should start with 7 minutes, sold me some goggles and showed me to a room. While I was still trying to figure out the goggles, the bed started going already! LOL I jumped in, so as not to waste my minutes!

It was pleasant, though very very bright. It was warm, but I didn't even break a sweat in that short time. When I got out, my face was a little glowy and I felt somewhat energized. Afterward, I walked home, so I was outside for quite a while in the afternoon light. It was not sunny out, but it was warm for this time of year - maybe about 12-14 degrees C (around 55 for those who prefer F). I felt good.

A few hours later, I could feel that feeling you get when you have had maybe just a little _too_ much sun? My skin felt a bit tight in a few places and I felt a little chilled. When I looked, my skin had actually tanned a bit, no sign of a burn or anything. I felt a little anxious, though. Maybe it was just the sudden intense burst of energy thrown at me? My sleep last night was a little more restless than usual. I haven't seen UVB since early September, so maybe it was a slight shock to the system? LOL

I will wait a few days before going back. And I think maybe next time I will try 5 minutes and see how I do. I want to find the sweet spot where it's all good!

I might bring a spray bottle with epsom salts solution. Oh and a couple of extra bottles of spring water. I was thirstier than normal after.

And when it gets cold again, I might hang around outside the salon without my coat on for a few minutes before I go in, just to cool my skin.

Does anyone have suggestions for how to get the most out of tanning? I've got 143 minutes more to spend and no prospects for natural UVB until April!

### DavidL — 2015-11-27T21:19:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cinnamons-journal.11447/post-182752>

I tan between 9 am and 12 pm every second day. I am at 50 degrees north. Sunrise on November 27th is 8:13 am.
Been taking resveratrol, K2, CoQ10, elk antler, with water that was left on my magnetico all night long before I go tanning.
In the tanning bed I start without goggles, eyes closed at first.
I listen to Gregorian Chants as I tan.
After a period of time, I put the goggles back on. They are red.
Since the UV index is only 0.6 at this time of year, I have found tanning to be a game changer.

### DavidL — 2015-11-28T00:19:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cinnamons-journal.11447/post-182755>

My current biohack is waking up with light of day. I do not set an alarm and allow myself to wake up naturally.
I have been waking up naturally near the end of NAUTICAL TWILIGHT.
Pic below is the amount of light I am currently exposed to.
If it's a cloudy day my wake time is usually in the CIVIL TWILIGHT range.

On days that I do not tan I sungaze barefoot on the cold concrete of my front step.
Everyday I make a point to go out in my backyard as the light of day approaches CIVIL TWILIGHT for a grounding and sungazing session.
I have a maple tree that I pruned for the perfect spot to ground my hand to as I face the sun.
My backyard faces south west.

### cinnamon — 2015-12-05T03:08:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cinnamons-journal.11447/post-183347>

Time for an update on my tanning hack!

I went twice this week - on Monday and Thursday. Each time I went for just 5 minutes. I left the goggles off for half the time and just kept my eyes closed.

I felt good afterward on both days. I had good energy, and again walked to the salon and home (about a 40 minute walk each way).

There were two things I noticed:

1\. I found my cold tolerance for being inside the house has not been as good as in recent weeks. I keep the thermostat at 15 C (59 F) during the day (and a little lower at night). I found I am still okay from when I wake up until late afternoon. Then, my feet tend to get cold. On the Monday, after tanning, this impacted my sleep because it seemed to take more than an hour for them to warm up after going to bed. This is something I have experienced in the past, but not at all this fall before starting the tanning. Yesterday, I had a similar impact, but not as strong and it did not interfere with sleep. I'm hoping it's just a transitional thing.

2\. I'm hungry! It's been a weird thing to feel very little hunger this fall. But, in the last few days, I've had a few episodes of tummy growling! They have occurred at different times - early morning, late afternoon, early evening and even shortly after going to bed. So strange! Maybe the UVB light is getting my gut microbiome excited that it might be summer and time for some carbs? LOL

Oh, and another thing about tanning. I've only been exposed to 17 minutes over 3 sessions, so that isn't much - but I definitely already have a lovely tan developing. The last two times, I made sure to be well hydrated and I put some epsom salts solution on before heading out to the salon. I have also been eating some DHA every day! It seems my skin is very responsive to the UV light it is getting!

My plan for next week is to again visit the salon twice. I think I will go for 5 minutes on the first visit. Depending on how I feel afterward, I will consider whether to make the second visit 5 minutes or to increase to 6.

### cinnamon — 2016-05-31T04:01:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cinnamons-journal.11447/post-195671>

A strange thing happened this morning and so I want to make a note of it. I was sitting out in my backyard, my bare feet on the ground and the sun streaming toward me, when I suddenly felt an odd sensation. It was a kind of an electrical tingly feeling over my whole body and it went on for about 20 seconds or so. I sometimes get a similar thing when I'm standing under a cold shower, but this was much stronger.

What else to remark about it? It was about 8 am or so. I had been out in the sun for most of the previous hour and a half (aside from time fixing breakfast). I was not wearing much - just shorts and a bandeau top. I was drinking cold spring water.

I'm hoping it's a good thing because this morning my brain appeared to be functioning much better. I did an entire NY Times Sunday crossword puzzle by myself in about an hour and a half!

### Jack Kruse — 2016-05-31T19:23:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cinnamons-journal.11447/post-195702>

This usually happens to me when I ground on the tree roots by my magnolia in the spring.

### cinnamon — 2016-06-01T02:17:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cinnamons-journal.11447/post-195757>

Well anything associated with the NOLA magnolia tree has to be good news! It was not at all unpleasant, and I have to say that I was a little disappointed it didn't happen again this morning!

My brain wasn't as sharp either. But, I am patient. And confident that it was a good sign.

Perhaps it's just a great improvement in dopamine levels that has me feeling that way? We have had such wonderful late spring weather - summer heat with lots of wind.

### cinnamon — 2016-06-02T03:19:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cinnamons-journal.11447/post-195843>

Well, it happened again today. And it happened more than once.

First it happened similarly to a couple of days ago, when I was grounding in the early morning sun. I noted that I had my feet actually on top of some weeds that were growing up between the concrete patio slabs. I noted that my skin had that familiar "goosebump" flesh that I get with CT. It lasted about a minute or so.

The second time it happened was while I was tanning around midday. I was not actually grounded at the time, but had just spent the previous 15 minutes or so grounded and before that had a cold shower and misted myself with epsom salts spray. The sun was shining intensely, but the wind was cool. Again, I had the goosebump flesh thing going on, despite the fact that my skin was getting very hot from the sun. The sensation lasted maybe a minute or so.

Later this afternoon I was out walking around for a couple of hours and I had that sensation on and off multiple times. I was wearing leather-soled shoes, so I suppose I was grounded at least some of the time. The wind was cool and the sun was bright the whole time. It was lovely!

Other things I noticed today:

-   My vision seemed to have improved a bit again - especially when out in UV light.

-   I was in a grocery store for a few minutes during my walk. I had to abandon a shopping cart I was using in the middle of the store as it was giving me mild electric shocks as the wheels moved over the floor.

-   My brain was pretty good today.

-   My mood was great!

### cinnamon — 2016-06-02T19:00:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cinnamons-journal.11447/post-195882>

I was thinking this morning that I forgot to mention about the humming. I found myself spontaneously humming a lot yesterday.

I noticed starting a couple of years ago that if I was out walking by a park and took my shoes off to walk through the grass in the park, I would catch myself humming while on the grass. There were at least 3 different parks where it happened. I was usually wearing leather-soled shoes already, so I think maybe it was just that I was making a _better_ connection barefoot. The humming wasn't any tune I recognized, but it was musical. And it felt good!

Yesterday, I found myself spontaneously happily humming on and off all day.

Now, after what I said to Jack yesterday about his knee, I'm wondering about my antenna "tuning in the radio of the earth" or something like that? LOL I suppose that would sound nutty to a lot of people, but somehow I think on this forum, you will know what I mean!

### cinnamon — 2017-06-04T00:51:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cinnamons-journal.11447/post-215376>

Thursday, June 1, 2017 was a red-letter day. My Quantlet arrived!!

So, for those beta testers in Canada, get ready! I was surprised when an email arrived on May 31 saying UPS was expecting to deliver a package to me. It arrived at about 10:30 am on June 1. The only thing to be aware of is that there are some fees to pay to get your package, so you need to have a credit card handy for the UPS guy.

I was like a kid on Christmas morning, tearing open the package! And then getting it onto the charger ASAP.  I waited and waited for the flashing orange LED to turn pink. I waited 4 hours. And then another 4. And then it got late and I decided to leave it on the charger overnight. I had heard so much about the importance of the first charge and didn't want to mess up!

In the morning, with the LED still flashing orange, I took it off the charger. And guess what? The LED turned pink the minute I took it off the charger. So, maybe I didn't need to wait so long? LOL I have since charged it several times and each time, it only turns pink when I take it off the charger. Probably this was just a misunderstanding on my part, but maybe it might clarify things for somebody else?

I'm starting to play with it and I expect I will have more the report over the coming days.

It came at a very good time as I've been sick for the past couple of weeks. I even made a trip to the ER on Tuesday. It looks like I had a bout with food poisoning two weeks ago. I test positive for salmonella.  It's been a crazy struggle to maintain hydration with constant rounds of diarrhea. I'm trying to rebuild gut flora with probiotics, and wanting not to have to give in to a round of antibiotics. I'm hopeful, but this has been going on for such a long time, that I'm concerned.

I am a walking billboard for the idea that your mass is NOT related to the calories you consume. I might have lost a pound or two, but I look remarkably hale despite the fact that from a practical standpoint, I haven't maintained a single thing I've eaten for the past 2 weeks. Could it be that over the past more than 5 years since I found Jack, I've been relentlessly teaching my body to get energy from sources other than food? To be fair, I have been tired and unable to keep up much of a regular routine, but I look pretty good!

Before the trip to the ER, I spent about an hour outside in the sun in a kiniki bikini drinking cold water. The ER doc, who had heard my story about 10 days of continous diarrhea came into the room with my bloodwork, calling it "pristine, even your liver enzymes are perfect!" I think he was surprised. Hell, I was surprised!  But my money is on sunshine for the win!

I have still been around, mostly lurking in the background and not really able to keep up with everything. But that is in part because I've been doing hard work. I mean the kind that changes things, not just reads about them! LOL  On that score, it is beyond excellent that my Quantlet has now arrived as my address won't be good anymore soon!

### caroline — 2017-06-04T02:54:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cinnamons-journal.11447/post-215380>

Hi Cin ...so nice to see you here this morning!

I can't wait to hear all about what you have been doing xo

### cinnamon — 2017-06-10T22:19:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cinnamons-journal.11447/post-215769>

All, right, so another week has passed. And I am getting frustrated.
I know food isn't all that important in the scheme of things, but I think I should be able to eat some of it without my body deciding to flush my colon.

Has anybody else experienced something similar? If so, what did you do to get things back on track?

Here's a summary:

-   3 weeks ago - food poisoning (salmonella enteritidis confirmed through stool sample testing).

-   Massive watery diarrhea over the next 24 hours or so.

-   Diarrhea continued several times a day.

-   On day 8, I started taking probiotics (5 billion active cells - take 3 times per day)

-   On day 10 I went to the ER. They gave me a litre of fluids by IV because they were concerned about dehydration. That is where they did the stool sample. That is also where I noted above that they did bloodwork where everything was "pristine".

-   On day 13, with the diarrhea continuing, I got some stronger probiotics. This is a refrigerated bottle from the health food store that claims each capsule has 100 billion active cells. I took 2 per day on days 13 through 17.

-   On day 18, I woke up thinking I have to try something else. I skipped breakfast and went out to buy activated charcoal. I took about 500 mg of it in the store, right after I purchased it. It seemed to slow things down and I was feeling better. Later that day, I ate a meal and that triggered more massive watery diarrhea. I took quite a bit of activated charcoal that night (I guess about 1.5 grams or so).

-   On day 19, I didn't eat anything. My energy levels were fine and it was not a problem at all to fast. I was able to consume several litres of spring water without it all ending up in my colon. I did have some diarrhea, but it (aside from being an outrageously evil looking black colour from the charcoal ) did not have excessive amounts of water.

-   Yesterday, on day 20, I kept fasting until about 4 in the afternoon. Again, good energy levels, good ability to consume water without it ending up in my colon. At 4, I had a light meal of about a cup of cooked broccoli with butter and a couple of slices of smoked salmon. My gut didn't like it and I had a lot of rumbling and some gas pains a couple of hours later. I had some diarrhea, but I thought maybe not so watery? I felt somewhat hopeful as I went to bed.

-   This morning, on Day 21, I thought maybe I need to add back the probiotics. I took one 100 billion cells dose and then had a simple omelette for breakfast (3 eggs, butter, a few mushrooms). I was okay for about 2 hours afterward. And since then, I have already had 3 rounds of watery diarrhea. I can see the undigested mushroom pieces in the toilet.

Other things to note are:

-   I don't have an appendix.

-   I have been very good about avoiding artifical light, being outside grounded for the early morning sun, getting good UV exposure, going to bed in a dark room by 10 pm.

-   I have been using my quantlet for 2xP30 sessions everyday for a week.

Just to be clear, when I say watery diarrhea, I mean it's like turning on a tap. It is not clear, but the amount of water that is in it must be at least several times more than normal. It really is like the colon is being flushed out and it seems to be worse if I have eaten and then had water. It is a serious impingement on my ability to leave the house and get things done.

I will likely go to my family doctor next week if this keeps up. But I'm not hopeful that that will be helpful.

Do I need to use something like immodium to get this to stop? It is really worrying that large volumes of water are ending up in my colon and then not being reabsorbed by the body, but instead flushing everything out. It seems I can stop this by fasting, but it would be nice to be able to eat (how else can I replenish my DHA?).

I would be grateful for any observations or suggestions or experiences anyone might be willing to share.

Eat 1/2 cup of cooked white rice and 1/4 cup apple sauce. Repeat 2-3 hours later.

I can't remember the last time I had either white rice or applesauce! I've been doing the epi-paleo thing for over 5 years now, so will have to go shopping to find these things. Should I make my own applesauce, or is the commercial stuff okay, in your experience?

### Allin — 2017-06-10T23:43:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cinnamons-journal.11447/post-215782>

Works great with adults, children and even dogs. Commercial plain applesauce is fine. Plain jane white rice is fine too. If you go to a store which has rice in bulk bins, just get 1/4 pound and small jar of plain applesauce.

Hi drezy. Yes, I have a Cornet ED88T. My environment at home in Toronto, is neither great nor horrible. By that I mean, there are definitely some EMF issues here, but the sleeping area and most of the areas I spend my time in are pretty low. And looking at my meter just now, I don't notice any spike in levels over what I saw when I measured a couple of months ago.

I am moving in about a month, but not entirely sure where just yet. (In the great spirit of jumping without a safety net!).

Interestingly, the food poisoning incident happened in Mexico. At that location (20 degrees latitude) the sun was fantastic, but there were very very high levels of EMFs in the house where I slept each night. There was a cell tower almost on top of the house. I was there for a total of three weeks, with the food poisoning event occurring 5 days before I left to travel home.

### caroline — 2017-06-11T06:21:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cinnamons-journal.11447/post-215794>

wow - sorry to read all that Cin. My only thought - are you sure it was food poisoning?

I got some kind of an intestinal critter about 8 months ago and I have not been the same. I am reacting to so many more things now....except I can eat raw steak and salmon and shrimp. Vegetable \[except carrots\] are doing me in.

Maybe when you do eat to start with homemade bone broths and take it really slow. Are you losing weight?

### cinnamon — 2017-06-11T16:09:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cinnamons-journal.11447/post-215809>

Okay, so it is now the morning of Day 22. And wouldn't it be great if it was the end of my sorry tale? LOL

I had the rice and applesauce yesterday evening. I felt a bit odd afterward, with a sensation of mild swelling in ankles and hands about 3 hours later, just as I was heading to bed. I had ingested about a litre of spring water during that time. I also woke in the night (my guess is maybe 4 am? but I have no clock, so not sure) and I was sweating and my toes were cramping. I drank more water and went back to sleep. I woke again, with the sweating and cramping, and got up, as it was first light. I drank more water and then spent about 2.5 hours outside in the early light (so nice). I had a cup of green tea, and then made another mushroom omelette. I have eaten it (together with 100 billion probiotic cells!) now over an hour ago. And so far (can we get a drum roll please? ) NO diarrhea! Yay!

So, it is good news so far. I will drink more water and hope for the best. I'll give an update to you all a little later today.

My curious mind wants to know why the rice/applesauce trick works. Anybody know? (And if this really is the ticket, damn but I wish I knew about it at least a week ago!)

### cinnamon — 2017-06-11T16:25:39+0300

Hi caroline,

Thanks for sharing your experience. I have a vague recollection of Jack saying food wasn't all that important except to get DHA, right? So, I suppose raw salmon and shrimp are pretty good to have on the list of things you can eat.  But seriously, I'm whining at 3 weeks and 8 months sounds like an eternity!

I guess I can't be 100% sure that it was food poisoning, but I did have stool samples tested and the only thing they came back with was salmonella enteritidis. I sure hope it isn't anything more. When I talked to the public health officer he said that this type of salmonella normally goes away on its own and doesn't usually have lingering side effects.

And yes, I've lost some weight. Probably only a couple of pounds during the first two weeks. But I think a bit more in the past week. I am hungry - so I hope I can eat. I do think I'll have to take it a bit slow at first. The bone broths are a good idea!

### cinnamon — 2017-06-11T17:36:09+0300

Crap! Literally

So, 2.5 hours after breakfast and another round of diarrhea. I felt nauseous and uncomfortable for about 15 minutes before it happened. And once again, undigested food.

Do I go back to the rice and applesauce? Fasting?

### Allin — 2017-06-11T21:38:36+0300

### Inger — 2017-06-11T22:13:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cinnamons-journal.11447/post-215841>

Cinnamon... I am sorry you struggle.. belly issues are no fun...
Have you tried fresh raw oysters or fresh raw seafood? My belly loves that... it is the most easily digestible food ever for me... Also my body loves raw grassfed beef fat...

Funny that I can do raw fish head smoothies, lamb ball smoothies, all kind of fermented meats.. and my belly stays sooo happy, but if I eat restaurant food that is heated for long and many times, like real dead food... my belly rebels. I know it is against CW but that is how my body works...lol

I think with salmonella you might need some patience... it might take a few weeks... My brother had it once and for him too it took that long to get rid of the runs.
I would concentrate on doing all the good stuff, and eat the best I could afford, and i think the body will heal eventually

I wondered if Iodine could help and found this when I googled it;

[http://www.healing-from-home-remedies.com/old-lugols-iodine-remedy-for-food-poisoning.html](http://www.healing-from-home-remedies.com/old-lugols-iodine-remedy-for-food-poisoning.html)

**Old Lugol's Iodine Remedy for Food Poisoning**
by R.D.

Here's an old home remedy that has been around forever. It is said to kill salmonella, giardia and any parasite eggs in the stomach. It's also supposed to help with gas, bloating and heartburn. I, thankfully, haven't had the opportunity to try it but here's the recipe:

Don't try this if you are allergic to iodine!

1/2 glass of water.
Add 6 drops of Lugol's Iodine Solution.
Stir and drink completely

You should feel much better within an hour. Sometimes 2 hours later a second does of six drops in a cup of water may be used only if needed. Only use ½ dose for a child.

You should feel much better within an hour!

\- - - - -

I am on a iodine trip and have great benefits so I thought if you have it at home you could try?

this link tells the same thing, Iodine kills food poisoning bacteria and salmonella...
Maybe together with oysters it would be even better?

Thanks, Inger. I haven't ever added extra iodine as I was just not sure, you know? There were so many stories that came from those who did, and some of them were a bit scary. I will keep it in mind, if things don't improve.

Here is an update for those following along.

I did not eat anything more after my two servings of rice and applesauce yesterday afternoon. I drank about 1.5 litres of water between 6pm and 9pm and went to bed by 10pm.

I had bad cramping in my feet last night again. It woke me (maybe about 4 am?) and I drank more water, then got up to pee. I went back to bed, and was able to get back to sleep. Then the cramping started again (my right foot, starting with the toes but then into the arch area). I got up, as it was light out. I went outside and drank more water while grounding in the morning light. 15 minutes later my foot was still cramping, so I added a small amount of salt to my water and drank that. Another 15 minutes passed and it did not seem to be helping.

In the wonderful morning light, my brain remembered there was a salmon fillet thawing in the fridge. So I ran into the kitchen and sliced a few bits off the salmon. Then I sat in the early light (6:30 am) and ate it, raw. It was not very much, maybe 20 to 30 grams or so. I did NOT have any probiotics. And I have not had anything else since, except spring water.

It is now 4 hours later. And I have not had any sign of diarrhea!
I have not even had any discomfort.

I'm a bit afraid to have more, but I think probably I will have a small salmon lunch today.

I wonder if skipping the probiotics might have also helped?

Anyway, fingers crossed and feeling somewhat hopeful this morning!

### Earth Monkey — 2017-06-13T02:10:18+0300

Thank you, Earth Monkey, for your insight here. This episode is something completely new to me and I have struggled a bit to understand it. I don't know of any water damage or mold issues surrounding me, though I imagine they are often not visible. It is possible that there are histamine intolerance issues here, though the coincident timing with the food poisoning episode makes me unsure about that.

I don't have much knowledge of how to approach possible histamine intolerance. My inclination is to follow Jack's advice to focus on "redox before detox" and keep doing the early morning grounding in the sun, reducing exposure to artifical light and sleeping early in a dark room. Do you have other suggestions regarding how to handle these types of issues?

### cinnamon — 2017-06-13T23:32:42+0300

### cinnamon — 2017-06-17T16:03:09+0300

I'm doing much better now!

Wednesday wasn't great as I slipped back a bit that day. It's when I discovered that I need to stay away from eggs for a bit.

Things were pretty good on Thursday and yesterday was very good. I think I've passed the point where I'm afraid to eat too much.

### Earth Monkey — 2017-06-18T11:27:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cinnamons-journal.11447/post-216476>

> [cinnamon said:](/goto/post?id=216103)
>
> Thank you, Earth Monkey, for your insight here. This episode is something completely new to me and I have struggled a bit to understand it. I don't know of any water damage or mold issues surrounding me, though I imagine they are often not visible. It is possible that there are histamine intolerance issues here, though the coincident timing with the food poisoning episode makes me unsure about that.
>
> I don't have much knowledge of how to approach possible histamine intolerance. My inclination is to follow Jack's advice to focus on "redox before detox" and keep doing the early morning grounding in the sun, reducing exposure to artifical light and sleeping early in a dark room. Do you have other suggestions regarding how to handle these types of issues?

Cinnamon, I suspect my histamine sensitivity increased when I got food poisoning from a bad oyster at a buffet restaurant. I was very sick and noticed symptoms starting after this. Old/bad seafood generally has the highest levels of dietaty histamine (and is what Scombroid poisoning is). You can get over it though. My sensitivity has lessened since then through elimination, slow re-introduction and, I suspect, my recent focus on all-things-Kruse (eg, getting plenty of sunlight), and only recurrs when I'm exposed to environmental triggers (eg mold-toxic environments). The following site has been very helpful to me over the years too: [https://healinghistamine.com/](https://healinghistamine.com/) Yasmina has a very balanced view and it's-not-forever focus. Good luck

### cinnamon — 2017-08-21T02:02:58+0300

### cinnamon — 2017-09-28T22:11:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cinnamons-journal.11447/post-224436>

In the shower this morning I was thinking about the fact that I have gained weight in the past few months. Some of that was expected, after all I lost weight, including some muscle mass after the salmonella incident earlier this summer. And of course the light cycles are getting shorter and there has been summer fruit. But still, my jeans are tighter than I remember.

I was thinking about what else has changed. Quite a lot I suppose as I did move in July. But then I found myself thinking about my Quantlet. I got it on the first of June and have been using it daily for all but 3 or 4 days since then.

I have played around with it a bit and set up some custom protocols. I originally used mostly the P30 protocol because I didn't need to have the android tablet with the app on it to run that one - it is the default program. Then I noticed that the cooling unit (the TEC) really uses up the battery quickly, so I got into using "wellness" or "Sleep/Relax" more frequently. And I set up a custom protocol that runs for an hour with continuous red, ir and violet light. I liked the longer protocols as I didn't have to go back to the tablet as often to set them. I would say I used the Quantlet for about 30 minutes a day the first week of June, then 60 minutes per day from then until end of July and probably 60-90 minutes per day in August and September.

I have been using it early in the morning and am often wearing it while eating breakfast. I do not use it later than about solar noon.

I have also been getting lots of natural light and regularly spent time over the summer outdoors in the morning and at midday. And, as always, I continue to be very careful about artificial light, especially after sunset.

Back in June and July I was hungry often. I was unable to eat for the first 3 weeks or so (see earlier posts in my journal for that sad tale). But then over the rest of June and July I ate a lot and craved food. That is not normal for me but because I had lost weight following the salmonella situation, I indulged my cravings. My weight had dropped from 120 lbs to 107 and I was skinny and lacking muscle mass.

By early August I noticed that my weight was pretty much back to normal and I was rarely hungry. I ate less, but still I ate, even if not hungry. It was a strange thing in some ways because I felt some compulsion to eat - maybe my body knows that winter is coming and after the stress of the sudden loss, it needs a little extra? I would say the quantities that I was eating were not unusual for me and I continued to put on weight. Today I weigh 133 lbs. That is as much as I have ever weighed in my entire life and is 25% more than I weighed in late June. My normal weight fluctuates between 120 and 125. I think it's enough and I'd like it to stop going up, please and thank you very much!

Yesterday, I was reading this thread:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/fruits-out-of-season.11002/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/fruits-out-of-season.11002/)
And I started wondering about the impact of UV on NO and what it does to the Electron Transport Chain. I wish I understood this better, but if I am reading this correctly, it is a mechanism that allows us to get energy directly from the sun.

In the above thread, David says:

"So eating when UV is present isn't worth it...must be why breakfast is needed before UV is present.... "​

**But if you do eat while UV is present, what happens to that food energy? Where does it go?**

I'm curious to know what you mitochondriacs think about UV light and food. Should we eat if we are getting plenty of UV? Or should we at least watch the timing of eating and exposure to UV light? What do you all think?

I'm wondering if I should try not using violet LEDs on the Quantlet until an hour or so after I eat breakfast and see what happens.

### cinnamon — 2018-10-03T02:27:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cinnamons-journal.11447/post-252217>

Wow, it's hard to believe a year has passed!

I finally did the 23andme and here are the results:

Maternal Haplotype: H

MTHFR C977T: -/-
MTHFR A1298C: -/-
VDR Bsm: -/-
VDR Taq: +/+
CBS C699T: +/-

I believe this confirms that, as I suspected, I have an "uncoupled" haplotype.

I need to do some research to understand the impact of the homozygous result for VDR Taq. I had a quick look on line but, as usual, it is tough to wade through the noise. I am wary of those who focus on oral supplements for Vitamin D. So far it seems there is a relationship between VDR Bsm and VDR Taq, and it has been suggested that it may not be much of an issue to be homozygous in one, so long as the other is -/-? Has anyone else had the same result? Did you find that it presented an issue for you?

CBS C699T having a heterozygous result is also interesting. I have to look at that too, keeping in mind that I don't want to fall too far into the SNPs rabbit hole and instead focus on improving redox potential.

I hope this message finds you all well!
I will try to post a bit more soon. Yes, I definitely need to update my avatar pic!

love to all,
cinnamon

### caroline — 2018-10-03T07:16:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cinnamons-journal.11447/post-252228>

Hey Cin ..... nice to see a bit of an update.

How are you?????

"On Customs and Excise duty in Felixstowe, Bob and Dave manage to get themselves trapped in the back of a pilchard lorry -- and Bob hates pilchards. A tenuous line of reasoning brings Dave to the conclusion that they must be in Venice -- when in fact they're in Amsterdam. **Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-major-fatigue.11454/post-136730>

So... I've made a lot of strides the past few years to really clean up my family's and my diet, get the toxins out of our house, and just generally move towards healthier and more natural living. Despite this, I continue to feel worse and worse and worse. The last few months it's really escalated to the point where I'm so fatigued I can hardly keep with with necessary things around the house and I'm gaining 3+ pounds a week. I'm up nearly 50 pounds (yikes!). I have lots of other weird symptoms to go along with all of this too.

Anyhow, had some labs ordered. What's your take?
Cortisol
AM 4.1 (13-24)
Noon 3.2 (5-8)
Afternoon 1.2 (4-7)
Night .4 (1-3)

Salivary Estirol 0.1 (1-4)
Testosterone 37.64 (20-60)
Progesterone 136.8 (50-400)

I also had a GI pathogen screen done. The results showed that there was moderate growth of Escherichia coli isolated.

[http://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2014/08/140801091114.htm](http://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2014/08/140801091114.htm)

There always seems to be many articles relating to Optimal principles on this website.

I used to get a magazine, Science News, (actual _paper_ - remember?!) from a person at Georgia Tech and devoured the latest news. That's where I first saw the Chixaloub crater info which answered a question I've had since a young boy - what killed off the dinosaurs?

Another article reported on wooden cutting boards being more sanitary that the new-fangled plastic ones that grew bacteria at a faster rate. The tannins in the wood is a natural inhibitor. This started me taking a critical view of "new" things sometimes being crappier than the old.

NSH

Dr. K. tells us that DHA has to be bound to the Sn-2 position on the glycerol backbone to be most effective.
Is this where DHA automatically goes? or does it ever take a detour for any reason?

### Inger — 2014-08-10T16:14:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/osf-8.11456/post-137482>

I would love to hear more about your new place Gretchen!
How does it feel... what is different now.... how does the family like it.. the kids.... did you feel any difference as soon as you were there? Sleep etc?

### Shijin13 — 2014-08-10T16:42:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/osf-8.11456/post-137484>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=137482)
>
> I would love to hear more about your new place Gretchen!
> How does it feel... what is different now.... how does the family like it.. the kids.... did you feel any difference as soon as you were there? Sleep etc?

In the 10 days we've been here - I've discovered that I can eat anything w/o any weight gain. (and I mean anything!) the other weekend I had a craving for ice cream. So I had some. What was interesting is I had a couple of spoonfuls and after that the Ice Cream was meh to me. I don't want it. I had my taste. I've never experienced that before EVER.

Chocolate has also become meh to me. And I LOVE and CRAVE chocolate normally. Though I think the Chocolate thing goes back to when I did HCG back in May. HCG re-wired my Chocolate craving. I have a bar of 90% dark chocolate in the fridge that's been there before HCG. I can have some - but it doesn't give me the satisfaction I previously got from it.

Also sleep has been amazing! I feel like I'm sleeping better than ever before.

The house is so peaceful and quiet. we have bullfrogs that sing to us at night, along with tree frogs and cicadas. I also don't get eaten alive by bugs here either. when we come home from being in the city - its like peace washing over us. Its almost as if we've crossed in to the Fairy lands. we've crossed realms.

the neighbors have horses. the outdoors smells better, the moon is brighter, its engaging. every morning we wake up in a fog of swirling mists and whimsy. We have apple trees, pear trees and peach trees that need love and attention to begin bearing fruit again. We have to build a new garden but we have plenty of space and we're looking to see if we are zoned to have chickens...

now if I could just finish unpacking so I can enjoy the outside more!

# Thread: Dealing with Osteoarthritis
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dealing-with-osteoarthritis.11457/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 54
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### TrickiDicki — 2014-08-05T05:26:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dealing-with-osteoarthritis.11457/post-136816>

Hi guys, Jack,
I'm male, mid 40s. As a child I suffered from Perthes disease, a degenerative disease of the femur head. As I grew older I was less affected by the problem apart from the odd flare up. A couple of years ago I started low carb Paleo (a la bulletproof) and saw great results in weight loss, vitality and mental capability but earlier this year I suffered an acute flare up of my hip condition. X-rays and MRIs confirmed I have severe osteoarthritis in both hips. I'm now booked for the first total hip replacement surgery in January, 5 months away.
Some recent investigation suggests that a low carb diet can impact the production of proteoglycans which are important for cartilage health, synovial fluid health etc.
So this brings me to ask for some advice. Does my reasoning make sense, that the low carb diet may have contributed to a worsening of a chronic osteo injury? If so, is it possible to firstly stop the decline but more importantly start reversing the damage? Is it possible to see any improvement within a 5 month period before I go the surgical route?
To reverse the degenerative process I figure I'm going to need to amp up stem cell activity, blood supply to the area and minimise systemic inflammation.
I'm currently doing CT once or twice a week and taking saunas once or twice a week, get sun exposure as often as possible, taking cod liver oil, glucosamine/chondroitin/MSM supplement, collagen powder and eating bone-based stews, vitamins B, C and multivitamins. I am drinking spring water and get good sleep.
I'm planning to add vitamin K2 to the mix, double down on the cold water therapy and add some low inflam glucose source daily (rice, sweet potato).
Any thoughts or advice appreciated.
Thanks

### nonchalant — 2014-08-05T05:45:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dealing-with-osteoarthritis.11457/post-136818>

Hi Tricki, welcome!
If you could post a pic, Jack might answer you.
I also went low carb high fat, and saw great health benefits, but my spine (which was already damaged by grains) went into a freefall. I developed scoliosis, and my right hip became a mess, making walking difficult. I believe, from what I have learned here, that the reason for this quick deterioration was dehydration, on top of a lack of seafood DHA, and the previously damaged joints.

The good news is that I think you can reverse your hip issue, but it will take time. The biggest help for mine was huge amounts of reverse osmosis water, daily seafood, ice baths, and squatting several times a day. I saw improvement starting in about a month. Can I ask where you live? If you are in a big city or somewhere with high EMF, grounding your back, which was another big help for me, might not be beneficial. Chiropractic care also helped my spine realign, once I started getting stronger. Avoiding EMF, which is dehydrating, is very important.

Vitamin K2 (mk4) is great. I'm not sure low-inflam carbs exist, unless you live near the equator. If you are in the northern hemisphere, you should probably be reducing carbohydrates in preparation for winter. I have heard that some people who take chondroitin end up with shellfish allergies, which would be very bad, since shellfish would be one of your best friends for getting out of this situation.

I hope this helps, and I hope you stay with us. There is a lot to learn here.

### caroline — 2014-08-05T08:48:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dealing-with-osteoarthritis.11457/post-136823>

Hi and welcome. have you been reading Dr.K's blogs? There are three osteoporosis blogs and many others that connect the dots. Do you have Dr. K's book? The Epi-Paleo RX? That is a must read to get an overview of the first couple of years of the blog.

Please read the last blog OSF #8 and listen to the podcast that Dr. Kruse links to.

There is a lot you need to consider ........

nonchalant has great advice.

### TrickiDicki — 2014-08-05T08:53:52+0300

### Jack Kruse — 2014-08-05T17:16:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dealing-with-osteoarthritis.11457/post-136851>

‘Unhealthy’ dietary fats — not just obesity — may contribute to worsening osteoarthritis, according to researchers from Duke Medicine.
The study, published in the Annals of the Rheumatic Diseases on July 11, 2014, showed that mice consuming a supplement of omega 3 fatty acids had healthier joints than those fed diets high in saturated fats and omega 6 fatty acids.
“Our results suggest that dietary factors play a more significant role than mechanical factors in the link between obesity and osteoarthritis,” said Farshid Guilak, Ph.D., Laszlo Ormandy Professor of Orthopaedic Surgery at Duke and the study’s senior author.
Obesity is one of the primary risk factors for osteoarthritis, although the mechanisms linking these conditions are not fully understood. It has been assumed that increased weight wears the joints out, but this doesn’t explain why arthritis is also found in hands and other joints that are not weight bearing.
[http://ausfoodnews.com.au/2014/08/04/omega-3-fatty-acids-may-lessen-severity-of-osteoarthritis-study.html?utm\_source=feedburner&utm\_medium=email&utm\_campaign=Feed%3A+AustralianFoodNews+%28Australian+Food+News%29](https://www.linkedin.com/redirect?url=http%3A%2F%2Fausfoodnews%2Ecom%2Eau%2F2014%2F08%2F04%2Fomega-3-fatty-acids-may-lessen-severity-of-osteoarthritis-study%2Ehtml%3Futm_source%3Dfeedburner%26utm_medium%3Demail%26utm_campaign%3DFeed%253A%2BAustralianFoodNews%2B%2528Australian%2BFood%2BNews%2529&urlhash=PaT6&_t=tracking_anet)

**[Omega-3 fatty acids may lessen severity of osteoarthritis, study](https://www.linkedin.com/redirect?url=http%3A%2F%2Fausfoodnews%2Ecom%2Eau%2F2014%2F08%2F04%2Fomega-3-fatty-acids-may-lessen-severity-of-osteoarthritis-study%2Ehtml%3Futm_source%3Dfeedburner%26utm_medium%3Demail%26utm_campaign%3DFeed%253A%2BAustralianFoodNews%2B%2528Australian%2BFood%2BNews%2529&urlhash=PaT6) ausfoodnews.com.au**
'Unhealthy' dietary fats — not just obesity — may contribute to worsening osteoarthritis, according to researchers from Duke Medicine.

### nonchalant — 2014-08-06T17:20:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dealing-with-osteoarthritis.11457/post-136986>

I use a similar grounding system when I'm in the country, and my back loves it. I use a sheet of conductive fabric (from LessEMF.com) so that my entire body is grounded. When my back was in worse condition, I would often feel tiny trickles of sensation in my back and hips when laying on it. It was like I could feel the inflammation leaving.  I miss those tiny lightning bolts. But I still occasionally feel them when its been a while since I last grounded.

I don't think Dr. K would suggest a regimen of aspirin.

Do you use ice packs regularly on your hips? I used to sleep on Cryo-Max ice packs. I'd put them underneath the grounding sheet, so I could benefit from the two things at once.

There is discussion in other forums about the 'right' way to use fish oil. Robb Wolf a few years ago was advocating very high fish oil supplementation but reduced his recommendations when it became clear that fish oil, being PUFAs, is highly oxidative and too much can lead to oxidative stress and inflammation. I think JK is right to prefer 'whole real' food sources.

### Da-mo — 2014-08-23T14:42:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dealing-with-osteoarthritis.11457/post-138687>

Just in case no else has covered it - my biggest improvement on osteoarthritis in my knee came after eliminating nightshades from my diet (tomatoes, potatoes etc. . . .) You'll find reference to this in the Epi-Paleo template here: [http://jackkruse.com/brain-gut-6-epi-paleo-rx/](http://jackkruse.com/brain-gut-6-epi-paleo-rx/)

> **_Foods that I would totally eliminate from the Epi-Paleo Rx:_**
> _1\. All grains no matter how they are prepared culturally.
> 2\. All US dairy (including raw US dairy) because of the A1 casein problem and its tie to BCM-7 and its massive link to Hashimoto’s and disease. Hashimoto’s in not a static disease but a dynamic one. This means you must pay attention to your thyroid panels and AB’s as you evolve through your life too. One can use French or New Zealand dairy products if you can source them.
> **3\. All nightshades vegetables if you have chronic elevated inflammation and a low Vitamin D level:** Datura, Mandragora (mandrake), Atropa belladonna (deadly nightshade), Lycium barbarum (wolfberry), Physalis philadelphica (tomatillo) , Physalis peruviana (Cape gooseberry flower), Capsicum (chili pepper, bell pepper), Solanum (potato, tomato, eggplant), Nicotiana (tobacco), and Petunia. With the exception of tobacco (Nicotianoideae) and petunia (Petunioideae), most of the economically important genera are contained in the subfamily Solanoideae. **Read my caveat below about the dietary uses!**
> Many members of the Solanaceae family are used by humans, and are important sources of food, spice and medicine. However, Solanaceae species are often rich in alkaloids whose toxicity to humans and animals ranges from mildly irritating (most) to fatal in small quantities._
> **_What are Solanaceae? If you have an AI, DDD or DJD consider avoiding them:_**
> _A. American-grown soy! It has been hybridized with petunias, a nightshade, to be pesticide resistant (Round-Up)
> B. All potatoes (NOTE: this does not include sweet potatoes because they are from the Marigold family)
> C. Tomatoes (green are worse than any other type if you eat them as I do. The lycopene issue is offset by eating other red fruits of veggies. Watermelon for example blows tomatoes away as a source of lycopene.)
> D. eggplant
> E. sweet and hot peppers (including paprika, cayenne pepper and Tabasco sauce, but not black pepper)
> F. ground cherries
> G. tomatillos and tamarillos
> H. garden huckleberry and naranjillas
> I. pepinos and pimentos
> J. Only the cape gooseberry is a nightshade (Physalis peruviana), Most gooseberries are not in the nightshade family. They are in the genus Ribes and are related to currants.
> K. Blueberries, Strawberries, okra, artichokes, have some solanaceae toxin in them, but are not strict nightshades. If you have arthritis or another inflammatory condition you might want to rethink the ‘dogma’ that surrounds them. (kills me to tell you this too)_
> **_Caveat: Key point to consider and why I use some of these veggies/fruit in my Epi-paleo Rx in my_ [_e-cookbook_](http://www.jackkruse.com/optimized-cooking/)_:_** _Most of the foods listed above have small amounts of the toxins in them. If you are ill with debilitating arthritis, Hashimoto’s or any autoimmune disease, fibromyalgia, biotoxin illness, or just have a high HS CRP normally serious consider limiting these foods. In many countries around the world people have found a way around these toxins by slow cooking their nightshades at very low temperatures for long periods of time and that would keep the vitamins intact while making the fruits easier on the body. This is how I use nightshades in my sauces. Nightshades have something called oxalic acid that depletes the calcium in your body. If you are already using pre and probiotics for your gut health then you know the meaning of the double edged sword. Dairy has a lot of calcium, and if tomatoes take it away from our body , why not consider have them together to create a balanced dietary effect? This is why many Italian dishes are prepared with the combo. As most of you know I am no fan of A1 dairy products for many reasons I laid out in the_ [_Epi-paleo Webinar_](http://www.jackkruse.com/epi-paleo-teleseminar/) _(BCM-7 casein and DPPIV) but the point is that if you learn how to create a balanced environment in your body then you don’t need to avoid the foods you love. This is what I learned about myself by doing quarterly testing on myself over 7 years. When I saw my HS CRP and Vitamin D were unaffected by some of these things I ate them without regret and you should too. That being said if you have an inflammatory condition, like most of my patients have in my clinic, you need to rethink this food group carefully._ [_Take a look at this google document for some more information._](http://docs.google.com/viewer?a=v&q=cache:e1MMPfAyY6gJ:www.donaldmiller.com/Adaptive%2520Innovations%2520in%2520Medicine%2520\(NXPowerLite\).ppt+&hl=en&gl=us&pid=bl&srcid=ADGEEShcsl7BBJtqYuzeHZ7UCDCJR0xqCFyZ_K9h9ib23mxu_PRFwEfHbQwTL6hgf_TvEyIY2Qdryu1hYq8po-RLG2nX1Tgab5AqqwiAs3U52u20MsKCYIM9QWMBBpuQvW33TQS7LC56&sig=AHIEtbSFrtmuo0Wg8NWPOh_nNc9cVfERUg)
> [_Here is a link for a good book on these substances._](http://www.nightshadefree.com/where-have-you-found-nightshades/)
> _Most of my spine patients with DDD and DJD get told this information and it shocks them. Most have never heard about it at all._ [_Here is a link to an arthritis foundation for you to research a bit more about this issue._](http://www.noarthritis.com/)
> _4\. All fruits, WHEN they out of season for your particular geography. In season, they are perfectly fine for you.
> 5\. Optional cut of all fowl. For Optimal health if you can afford to limit fowl do so. All fowl should be cutuntil your healthy with the exception of duck. Personally I eat very little fowl but when I do it is wild duck or quail/pheasant. I use duck fat much more than its meat.
> 6\. All Legumes without exception even for cultural reasons.
> 7\. Any saponincontaining food. Alfalfa sprouts, Yucca, soy, quilla extract (Used to foam drinks like beer) chia seeds
> 8\. Avoid all sweeteners period. Stevia is OK ( I rarely use it now) if it has no maltodextrin or other additives._

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dealing-with-osteoarthritis.11457/post-139205>

I thought I'd give folks an update.
I think the grounding, managing EMF exposure and increased fish oil/oyster/fishy stuff consumption has made the biggest improvement in my health. Maybe also non-fluoridated water but maybe not such a powerful factor. Hard to say. I've also received a delivery of vitamin K2 supplement (derived from nato) which I've started taking. Seems very hard to come by here in Australia...

My hips are feeling so much better than they were just a month ago it's unbelievable. Last weekend I was able to walk a good couple of miles along steep, rough, rocky forest tracks with the dogs and no problems at all. The next day, another lengthy walk. And the next day. It's fantastic how my mobility has returned! Yay!

On the down side, I have definitely become much more sensitive to EMF - wifi and cell phone particularly. I now have my phone on airplane mode for 90%+ of the day and only turn the cell phone on to check for voice messages and SMS. Even then I feel tingles and jumpiness in my hands. The other day I was on a plane and at the end of the flight while taxiing in, everyone turns on their phones. I felt pressure in my head. I suspect I'm now just able to discern these sensations because I'm not zapping myself all day long. I also know that this is not just imaginary - it is a real physical sensation. The impact of wifi in schools, particularly as ipads become the primary teaching tool, is scary.

### TrickiDicki — 2014-09-02T03:23:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dealing-with-osteoarthritis.11457/post-139589>

@[prAna303](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1363/) @[Inger](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1057/) - I agree, having awareness is preferable to being unaware, it just means there's another thing popping to the top of the priority list! Spent last night rewiring the house to reduce wifi needs. The kids are getting nervous (as well they should - LOL!)

Back to arthritis - I have a question on popping, clicking in joints, not just those affected by OA. Common knowledge says that pops or clicks are related to the release of dissolved gases as the pressure in the synovial capsule is reduced. So if one notices clicky joints on one day more than another, this would suggest that the chemical composition of the synovial fluid has changed. Are clicky joints a potential sign of problem such as nutrient flow and/or circulation into the joint capsule?

### Gracelind — 2014-09-02T04:19:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dealing-with-osteoarthritis.11457/post-139595>

> [TrickiDicki said:](/goto/post?id=138662)
>
> Wondering if anyone has opinion of [ozone treatment](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ozone_therapy) for helping with the healing process of arthritic joints once other factors are taken care of? I can't think of a good explanation why ozone treatment should work - if I understand it, ozone would be electrophilic so would remove electrons from the treated area rather than introduce more electrons? Could this act to stimulate the ETC and thereby be beneficial? Maybe the addition of an oxidative agent to the site might stimulate mitochondria?
> The oxidative effect of ozone would seem to be harmful and inflammatory.
> So to my mind, this wouldn't add up to 'useful' unless I've got things mixed up?

As it happens I had an ozone injection in left sacroiliac joint last Wednesday. It took some time to feel the effect but that joint has been pain free since. Granted--only a few days. The muscles around the joint have been tender at times and there's some sciatica because there's something else going on but the ozone stopped a major locus of pain.

Several times I've been present when patients exclaimed to this naturopathic doc about the amazing effect of ozone in arthritic knees. One said she hadn't been able to hike for a decade until after the shot, when she did an 11 mile trail with no bad after effects.

It's great that you're getting improvement from changes you've made on the road to better health! I don't know how the ozone helps, but it's something to keep in your toolkit in case you need it later.

### TrickiDicki — 2015-09-03T14:57:25+0300

Hi guys
It's been a long time since my last post, so here's an update. Thanks to @[PaulG](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1838/) for prompting me!
I had my left hip replaced in January. The operation went off without a hitch, no complications. I had a large swag of supplements with me in hospital and found meditation to be effective before and after surgery. I've continued on my 'protocol' since the op.
What was interesting was that the surgeon remarked how there appeared to be some bone regrowth within the cyst sites! My non-operated hip has completely calmed down and has managed the additional stresses of physiotherapy very well. I don't have pain, I have pretty good mobility and at this stage I'm not scheduling the 2nd operation.

Cheers

\- Richard

# Thread: Increased Bruising/Bleeding - Omega-3 Levels Dangerously High?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/increased-bruising-bleeding-omega-3-levels-dangerously-high.11460/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread

### Winter Mermaid — 2014-08-05T18:43:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/increased-bruising-bleeding-omega-3-levels-dangerously-high.11460/post-136857>

Hello lovely people,

I am on Day 4 of the Leptin Re-set (keeping far under 50 grams of carbs a day and almost certainly in ketosis) and Day 8 of Cold Thermogenesis (just face dunking in icy water). My goal is to lose 20-30 pounds and become a proficient, cold-adapted, open water winter swimmer. I am following the diet strictly and I've been increasing my fish/crustacean intake a LOT. Maybe even overdid it . . . but I feel fantastic.

Yesterday I had my regular acupuncture treatment, and to my shock I developed large purple round bruises around the needle sites (under my navel, feet/calves). I've never been an easy bruiser and have gotten acupuncture for years without bruising.

My conclusion is that my high intake of DHA/EPA (4-5 grams total a day from salmon, sardines, and oysters) has already significantly thinned my blood.

My question is: is this dangerous? Should I worry about if I get into a random accident and need surgery? Will I bleed out? Is there such a thing as too much tissue Omega 3? I'm going to cut back on the fish a little unless I can be reassured this won't kill me- heh. Other than being about 25 pounds overweight (I stress-eat carbs like a crazy person and recently got myself OUT of the stress-inducing terrible office job, have already lost a couple pounds), I am healthy and have no unusual conditions.

Thanks so much and great to meet everyone in this awesome forum .

@[nonchalant](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/191/), I have been a reader/follower of Mark's Daily Apple for years - all the meat and dairy I eat at home is grass-fed and organic. HOWEVER my downfall is being out of the house, busy, stressed- I would buy an entire cake, eat an entire pizza, KFC/McDonald's/Taco bell. I call it the SAD (schizophrenic american diet) because half the time my diet is better than most people's and half the time it is probably worse. I have a naturally high metabolism- my friends always say if they ate how I (sometimes, when I am stressed) eat, they would be 300 pounds overweight. So yes, my diet was high in bad oils, wheat, dairy and meat . . . . sometimes.

I've gone regular paleo/primal several times before. I would feel great for about two days, and then have a crazy binge day from wheat and dairy withdrawal. Awful. I truly think the Omega 3 connection (from real food not supplements) is what is making this different- I have never gone four days of no wheat/no dairy without a binge!!!

I've been doing 5 minutes of sunbathing at solar noon for the last four days too, which might be helping too. I am super pale and can't tan, so 5 minutes is great .

SO if you're eating food with little of it in it and you're in a high stressed environment you can see why DHA deficiency shows up.......the result is a broken mitochondria. ### Josh — 2014-08-05T23:19:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/increased-bruising-bleeding-omega-3-levels-dangerously-high.11460/post-136896>

[http://www.nutritionj.com/content/13/1/31](http://www.nutritionj.com/content/13/1/31)

> **Background**
> The American Heart Association’s Strategic Impact Goal Through 2020 and Beyond recommends ≥ two 3.5-oz fish servings per week (preferably oily fish) partly to increase intake of omega-3 fatty acids eicosapentaenoic acid (EPA) and docosahexaenoic acid (DHA). We examined the intake of total fish, fish high in omega-3 fatty acids, α-linolenic acid, EPA, and DHA in U.S. adults (19 + years) using data from the National Health and Nutrition Examination Survey, 2003–2008.
>
> **Methods**
> Usual intakes from foods alone and from foods plus dietary supplements were determined using the methods from the National Cancer Institute.
>
> **Results**
> Mean usual intake of total fish and fish high in omega-3 fatty acids was 0.61 ± 0.03 and 0.15 ± 0.03 oz/day, 0.43 and 0.07 respectively. Total fish and fish high in omega-3 fatty acids median intake was 0.43 and 0.07 oz/day, respectively. Intake from foods alone for ALA, EPA and DHA was 1.5 ± 0.01 g/d, 23 ± 7 mg/d and 63 ± 2 mg/d, respectively. ALA, EPA and DHA from food only median intakes were 1.4 g/d, 18 mg/d and 50 mg/d, respectively. Intake of ALA, EPA and DHA from foods and dietary supplements was 1.6 ± 0.04 g/d, 41 ± 4 mg/d and 72 ± 4 mg/d, respectively. While intakes of fish high in omega-3 fatty acids were higher in older adults (0.13 ± 0.01 oz/d for those 19–50 yrs and 0.19 ± 0.02 oz/d for those 51+ year; p < 0.01) and in males as compared to females (0.18 ± 0.02 vs 0.13 ± 0.01 oz/d, respectively; p < 0.05), few consumed recommended levels. Males also had higher (p < 0.05) intake of EPA and DHA from foods and dietary supplements relative to females (44 ± 6 vs 39 ± 4 and 90 ± 7 vs 59 ± 4 mg/d, respectively) and older adults had higher intakes of EPA, but not DHA compared to younger adults (EPA: 34 ± 3 vs 58 ± 9, p < 0.05; DHA: 68 ± 4 vs 81 ± 6, p < 0.05).
>
> **Conclusions**
> As omega-3 fatty acids are deemed important from authoritative bodies, supplementation in addition to food sources may need to be considered to help U.S. adults meet recommendations.

### caroline — 2014-08-06T09:13:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/increased-bruising-bleeding-omega-3-levels-dangerously-high.11460/post-136954>

Have you watched the podcasts .... a great way to get fast and easy info.

you have pretty well nailed it ...but you are in EMF hell ...as you already know!

this is what your day should look like....

rise and go outside and look at the sun ...barefeet and little clothes

BAB within 30 minutes of waking .... make sure to have at least 50gr. of protein - tons of seafood!

R/O water

get as much sunshine and grounding as you can

limit blue light and non native EMF ...hugely important!

get all electronics out of your bedroom ...no iphone, ipad, TV. clock radio etc. Can you cut the breaker to your bedroom? do you have a smart meter?

can you go camping on the weekends? sleep under the stars? swim in a stream or lake?

oxy is very important to activate the great pharmacy in your brain ...so laughing, hugging, sex..... the more often the better - for all of the above!

keep reading and asking questions....

### ssj3 — 2014-08-06T11:15:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-revolution-in-electronics-in-the-making.11462/post-136959>

I have been following the graphene developments from an investment view... here is an excerpt from an article with details similar to the youtube video.

> Excerpt from Graphene — Coming to a Newspaper Near You ,Jason Stevenson, Resources Analyst
>
> Pioneers are building products that need more resources. One of these involves one of the greatest scientific discoveries of our lifetime —graphene.
>
> There’s no other way to say it, this is ground breaking tech.
>
> Andre Geim and Konstantin Novoselov made the discovery back in 2010. If you haven’t heard about it, keep reading. Graphene could be the most important tech of the 21st century.
>
> So what is it?
>
> Graphene is lighter than paper and stronger than steel. At the same time, it’s flexible and more conductive than copper. It’s possible in the future that you could be reading a newspaper made from graphene.
>
> One day, Geim and Novoselov were playing around with graphite flakes and sticky Scotch tape. Similar to how children play with their toys. If you didn’t know, flake graphite is simply a strip of graphite.
>
> The team were examining the electrical conduciveness of graphite.
>
> They used the sticky tape to peel off a layer (flake) of graphite from its block. They peeled one layer at a time. Eventually they managed to shave the block down to a one atom thick flake — the thinnest of all possible layers.
>
> A playful game ended up turning into a Nobel Prize. It turned out that this one atom flake was about 100 times stronger than steel by weight. When made into a hammock, it would be strong enough to hold a four kilogram cat but thinner than a human hair.
>
> Graphene is only one atom thick, so it’s clear and flexible. The potential uses for graphene appear almost limitless.
>
> But there’s one major challenge — commercialisation.
>
> Graphene has the power to change economics. It has the power to change the world. However, it’s still in its early days. This means that there’s a lot of work to do to transform graphene into a leading product.
>
> The aim is to manufacture graphene on a commercial scale. But there are few promising practices that exist. Experts have tried splitting graphite layers by using plasma and chemicals. However, this tends to produce lower grade graphene.
>
> This struggle can only mean one thing…graphite will likely become a valuable product of the future. It’s also a truly scarce resource.
>
> And don’t forget that it’s highly conductive. Some believe it could rival copper in the future. Flexible electronics, solar cells and batteries could all use graphene.
>
> Let’s look at batteries a little closer. The world wants to reduce carbon emissions. This means that batteries will be more important to the future than ever before.
>
> Welcome to the ‘lithium air battery’ using graphene. It has five times the storage capacity of a ‘lithium-ion battery’, which uses graphite. This is truly tech that can change the world.
>
> Tesla Motors \[NASDAQ:TSLA\] may even opt for the ‘lithium-air battery’ one day. Tesla uses lithium-ion batteries to power its electric vehicles. Lithium-ion batteries are also widely used in mobile phones and laptops.
>
> But lithium-ion batteries have a flaw. Power. Tesla lithium-ion batteries can only support 426 kilometres on one full charge. I mean, this isn’t bad, but it isn’t great. It’s likely that graphene powered batteries are the next generation. They are light in weight and high in energy. And lithium air batteries will likely be cheaper to build.
>
> But lithium-air batteries need to become marketable. If applied to electric cars, they have the potential to go from Melbourne to Sydney on a single charge. This is a distance greater than 800 kilometres.

# Thread: What is the brain injury?

### Aerose91 — 2014-08-06T03:59:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-brain-injury.11463/post-136930>

I apologize ahead of time if I missed it in the blogs but Dr Kruse says that injury to the PVN is the source of adrenal fatigue. I'm all for this but I am still foggy on what causes the initial insult to the PVN. Why can it not accurately respond to outside stresses anymore?

I know most doctors say that stress and overexertion etc. causes AF but I don't think I buy that. I was surrounded by people who lived in the same city as me, had the same job/sleep patterns as me, ate worse than me and partied waaaaay more than me and they are perfectly fine. Yet I have, according to all my doctors, "the worst case of adrenal fatigue I've ever seen". I'm talking frequent fainting, shower once a month, too dizzy to stand, so nauseous I spend my days over a toilet etc. And that was just a predecessor to my M.E.

There's obviously something broken in me that isn't getting fixed. Just 1 day of too much expenditure and it will set me back months. I understand the leptin reset but can't do it because it crashes me into oblivion- too much stress. How did the PVN get injured in the first place?

### nonchalant — 2014-08-06T04:33:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-brain-injury.11463/post-136931>

Blue light toxicity, poor sleep, and loss of proper circadian signaling damages the PVN.
Other people near you can exhibit cellular stress in another fashion, such as diabetes, cancer, heart disease, depression, etc.

### Aerose91 — 2014-08-06T05:49:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-brain-injury.11463/post-136937>

Those make sense. But then why does expending energy in any form (physical, cognitive, stress) cause me to crash into oblivion? These are crashes that take 6 months + to crawl out of. Wouldn't the same insult be needed to cause further injury?

### nonchalant — 2014-08-06T06:43:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-brain-injury.11463/post-136939>

The PVN is involved in the connection of the central nervous system to the endocrine & immune systems. If this connection is off, your body cannot function properly. The hormones it normally uses are not available without direction. Apparently it is a vicious cycle, where the PVN isn't receiving the signaling it needs to make sense of the world. It almost sounds like you are suffering from seasickness.

Aerose, are you doing any CT? Jack recommends face dunks as a way to signal the confused PVN. It is a backdoor method to reach the PVN when the normal environmental signals are not being received.
[http://jackkruse.com/brain-gut-16-adrenal-fatigue-rx/](http://jackkruse.com/brain-gut-16-adrenal-fatigue-rx/)
