# Thread: Jeff's Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeffs-optimal-journal.11464/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 46
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jeff Power — 2014-08-06T05:49:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeffs-optimal-journal.11464/post-136936>

I'm working with a Naturopathic Medical Doctor to identify the root cause of some hormonal imbalances that I am experiencing. After reading the Adrenal Fatigue Rx, I feel like I am a case study for Dr. Kruse's explanation. Read more below and you will see why....

Background:
I'm 31 years old. I've been athletic my whole life, grew up eating organic as a child, and played sports my whole life. I went to college, drank a lot, partied a lot and worked out at the gym like a mad man. I took many work out supplements that I now regret, but at the time was great! (i.e. testosterone, tren, deca, winstrol, andro, etc). Also to note is that I had Lymes Disease when I was 16. My mother put me on a year long wholelistic program and I have never had a problem since. I now run a successful business while completing a juris doctorate in the evenings. I've been in school for the past 3 years (an extremely stressful and demanding schedule to say the least, basically going 12 to 16 hours a day 4 days a week). About a year ago, I shifted to a modified paleo high healthy fat and low carb diet. I cut out gluten, processed foods and sugar about 6 months ago. I also started to begin my day with a big cup of bulletproof coffee (which I love) - butter, coffee, MCT oil, collagen protein and aniracetam powder.

Now:
After completing two rounds of [www.WellnessFx.com](http://www.WellnessFx.com) testing and tracking, I decided to go to see a Naturopathic Medical Doctor to get a deeper look at my hormones. The following are issues we found and are now trying to correct:

1.  Low Test serum: 534 ng/dL
2.  Low Free Test: 11.0 pg/mL
3.  Low Pregnenolone: 32 ng/dL
4.  Low Cortisol Serum LCMS (8 AM Test): 8.6
5.  Low Free Cortisol (8 AM Test): .028
6.  Lower end DHEA-Sulfate: 231.9 ug/dL
7.  Lower end IGF-1: 148
8.  Low FSH: 2.3 mIU/mL
9.  Low B12: 487
10.  Low end WBC count: 3.5 x10e3/uL

All my other labs were good to great.

1.  Healthy Vitamin D: 94, Zinc, Magnesium, Iron
2.  Low Inflammation: C Reactive Protein: 0.18
3.  Everything else was good, cholesterol good, kidney health, liver health, no candida, prostate good, etc.

Diagnosis:

My naturopathic doctor works with a lot of pro athletes. He sees this as a common profile among men, especially those that have head trauma - rattling the pituitary gland and other brain connections. I played soccer my whole life and still do (constant knocks to the head).

Treatment:

1.  He started me on HCG shots to naturally increase my bodies ability to produce testosterone (compared to test replacement therapy)
2.  Pregnenolone pills at 50mg per day.
3.  An adrenal complex supplement (bovine adrenal, ashwaganda, etc.)
4.  Ordered me to fix my b12 levels and up my magnesium intake.
5.  Ordered me to work getting better sleep and finding ways to relax as being a priority.

I am going back in next week to take blood and dig in on some additional areas he wants to explore.

What do you think about this treatment plan? Am I on the right track? Should I be considering the Adrenal Protocol or other protocol. Or should I wait and see how things are progressing? Any help or feedback is appreciated.

PS
I love the work Dr. Jack Kruse is doing. I'm so grateful for the hard work he is putting into his research! Thank you.

### caroline — 2014-08-06T08:46:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeffs-optimal-journal.11464/post-136951>

Are you doing the leptin reset - using an epi paleo template?

You need to reset the Rolex in your brain ..... Your work and study program is crazy!

what is your non native EMF situation?

### Lahelada — 2014-08-06T16:33:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeffs-optimal-journal.11464/post-136976>

Hello and welcome.
I cant comment on your docs RX with the exception of saying that if you can get something jn jts natural package do so. Your vit D levels are good so I am guessing that Emf has not affected you yet. In order to recover I would ringfence though and act as if you were i.e mitigate exposure. Now for what you will probably not like to hear but exercise is bad for you. Yes,not per se but for you. You are effectively overspending energy and Masterflow has reduced your credit limit with a view to cutting you off in the future,date unknown. Start saving and pay heed to circadian rythms. Sleep early,awake early,have a big breakfast,do not eat anything 4 hours before bed. Become boring at least for a while. Get out into nature as much as you can. Increase your Oxytocin production. You will need to find yourself a list to see which activity suits you best...
Get cold.Read up on cold thermogenesis.
Oh,I forgot to say make time to observe sunrise. It resets your bio c lock.
Too much allat once ? Start with what js the most lowhanging frujt for you and work yourself up the tree.
Best of luck.

### Jeff Power — 2014-08-06T17:00:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeffs-optimal-journal.11464/post-136981>

As for EMF, I live in a condo building and work in a wifi filled office all day. I'm sure I'm getting a lot of non-native EMF. I love technology, gadgets and living in a city so those I'm struggling with. I did recently start to use a red LED light in the evenings and also set on a timer in morning to automatically turn on to help with my bio clock and waking up. Not working out might kill me faster but I'm actually considering it for some period of time. What do you think about sticking to Yoga for a while? Also, I set up a work out room in my office for my staff to use, so I have been following a simple daily plan where everytime I go to the bathroom, I walk back into my office and do 15 push ups. It is not very strenuous but it gets the blood flowing.

### Lahelada — 2014-08-06T18:41:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeffs-optimal-journal.11464/post-136989>

### Jeff Power — 2015-11-11T17:17:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeffs-optimal-journal.11464/post-181660>

It's been a while since I posted on here so I thought I would give an update a year later. Up until recently I didn't understand how important light was to solving my personal thunderstorm. To reiterate, I am in good shape, look and feel mostly good but my labs are not optimal and I am working on that. I am trying to get to optimal focusing on my light issues (i.e. too much blue light and not enough IR/UV).

Below are some of my labs from about three months ago before getting serious about light. I will be getting labs again in the next two weeks.

**Main labs I'm trying to get to optimal:**
T: 593
Free T: 12.1
Preg: 31
Dihydrotest: 49
DHEA-S: 232
IGF-1: 161
BUN/Creatine: 16/0.9
Morning Cortisol Serum: 9.5
CBG: 3.1
Free Cort Serum: 0.28
Vitamin D - 59 (without supplementation, was as high as 94 with supps)

**Others:**
Hs-CRP - 0.3
Leptin - 1.9
Adiponectin: 8.0
Mg: 5.5
Zinc: 1332
T4: 7.5
A1c: 4.9
Free T3: 3.2
TSH: 1.12
Potassium: 3.9
Total Cholesterol: 245
LDL: 159
HDL: 77
Triglercide: 83
sdLDL: 25
WBC: 4.0
RBC: 5.14

(other labs available and are in good shape per my NMD)

**Current Strategy (started about 30-60 days ago):
1\. Work on sleep:** Blue blockers at night in home, limit blue light, wear blue tech glasses when in law school twice per week at night until 9 PM in toxic environment, 10g magnetico, take mg and dhea before sleep, black out room.
**2\. Get more light during work week:** Reptile bulbs and IR bulb in morning for 30m-1hr laying bed, morning direct sunlight on retina outdoors, spend at least 1 hour in direct sunshine at optimal UVB time based on dminder app, weight training in direct sun twice per week, black lights and IR light in my office, ban on fluorescent lights, use of blue tech in office.
**3\. Get more direct light on weekends:** spend as much time outdoors in direct sun as possible on weekends.
**4\. Supplements:** HCG 80mg every other day, 50mg dhea, 100mg preg, mg theronate, mg or, mg mal, d-ribose, tourine.
**5\. Diet:** epi-paleo, high fat, seafood meal at least once every day, big breakfast with coconut oil and butter coffee everyday.
**6\. CT:** not currently doing CT (working on a way to start doing this again).
**7\. Water:** drinking natural spring water shipped from Sedona, as much as I can each day throughout the day.
**8\. Education:** Recently joined gold membership, I have been working my way through all of the webinars and Q&A's since the beginning. This has been quite helpful. It starting to all make sense to me I think...

Any suggestions are appreciated. I will report back with results. My plan is to see if it is possible given my current location (Scottsdale, AZ) to get to optimal. If not, then I will have to start to think hard about my actual location. I live on the 5th floor of a condo and work on the 2nd floor of an office building in a significantly urban nn-EMF area. Time will tell...I have a feeling I know the answer based on learning.

adrenal fatigue links to a lack of UV and IR light via our surface receptors. Adrenal fatigue links to the PVN.Light information is captured exclusively by the eyes using specialized retinal photoreceptors and transduced directly to the SCN via a dedicated neural pathway, the retinohypothalamic tract (RHT). Each day the light-dark cycle resets the internal clock, which in turn synchronizes the physiology and behavior controlled by the clock.

The major biochemical correlate of the lightdark cycle is provided by the pineal melatonin rhythm. Under normal light-dark conditions, melatonin is produced only during the night, by using sunlight captured in biogenic amine during bright sunlight during the day to power the effect. Moreover it provides an internal representation of the environmental photoperiod, specifically night length.
This pathway also helps yoke the Vitamin A cycle in the photoreceptors to the environment in this way. These two cycles become correlated novelties that allow the brain to predict the future by trend setting photo chemical cycles. The trend is predictable if dopamine is also also being created. This is why dopamine and melatonin are correlated by being biogenic amines. One should never be uncorrelated without the other unless the light stimulus is very altered in some way tied to an unbalanced spectrum. The synthesis and timing of melatonin production requires an afferent full spectrum light signal from the SCN which projects to the pineal gland via the paraventricular nucleus and the superior cervical ganglion. This is why BAT is found over the posterior neck region where this ganglia exists. Light becomes able to burn BAT protons to liberate heat and energy for sleep energy to run the non linear optical programs in the brain of regeneration when the DC electric is designed to be absent when day light is absent. This helps explain why light exposure during the night also inhibits melatonin production acutely and provides an indirect assessment of light input to the SCN via the RHT. Light is a correlated novelty to the DC electric current in wakefulness as Becker found in all of hiw work on salamanders and mammals. This finding has been largely ignored by biology to human peril in our modern world.

> Kris, Wow those are some really interesting results.
>
> So are you going to continue on high carb in summer and then low carb in the winter moving forward?
> Where do you live?

Interesting indeed! Actually, I don't think I'll ever go really high carb, and the reason being, is even if I get decent sun in the summer, I still feel like I'm using cold as my dominant stimulus. Everyday I am guaranteed a couple of cold showers, and I drink ice cold water all day long, that I feel like my SCN is entrained to cold right now, even in summer. It just seems this pathway works regardless of the season, and I crave fats like crazy in the cold. The only time carbs appeal to me now is if they are smothered in butter, coconut oil or sour cream.

That being said, I definitely eat more carbs now that it's summer, but in general, I'm usually around 100g of carbs, and my diet is pretty consistent year round.

I live just outside of Ottawa up in Canada at the 45th latitude.

# Thread: I'm in love with my Schumann resonance generator

### WereBear — 2014-08-07T03:04:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-in-love-with-my-schumann-resonance-generator.11470/post-137049>

It's been two weeks since installing the [Total EMF Shield](http://www.amazon.com/Total-EMF-Shield-Business-Protection/dp/B00BQJ7BJC) I read about on the forum and my sleep has _increased_ by two hours in the morning.

Now, I've put my iPod touch on Airplane mode 98% of the time, put a EMF shield on the bottom of my laptop and don't put it on my lap anymore, take my lunch hour in a nearby park, and use either speakerphone or my air-type earbuds to stay away from my cellphone, which I don't even use that much.

It's a month in and I've been eating right, starting my shower with ALL cold for at least two minutes, switching to my salt lamp at sundown, etc. I'm sure all that has helped.

But my husband's sleep has also improved, and he hasn't changed a thing!

I wasn't skeptical, or I wouldn't have invested in these steps. But I admit... I didn't expect such fast results, or so much of them.

Another wonderful part is hearing my husband telling his father about what we've been doing, and saying, "The science is there." He's a very important convert... though he's not as far down the road as I am, he is very happy the efforts I'm making are paying off.

The key to the Total Shield’s effectiveness is the presentation of the following four characteristics of the earth-resonant field.

### WereBear — 2014-08-08T15:35:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-in-love-with-my-schumann-resonance-generator.11470/post-137290>

I'm just reporting what is happening. I don't have the knowledge to explain it further. If it's a placebo effect, it's quite the powerful one, as it worked on my husband for five days before he knew it was in the house.

Yes, I did a kind of science experiment on him, smuggling it in and not saying anything. At the end of five days, (I'm not in the habit of keeping secrets from him so that's as long as I lasted) I showed him how the nasty bug bite on my back had vanished in three days, instead of the over-a-week the last one hung in there, and asked him how his sleep had been. And I knew what his answer would be because I had _observed_ it getting longer!

It's difficult to find unbiased reviews of such items online. And this is only n = 2, after all. And it's only been two weeks. I gambled and I think I won.

Or, check that the seller has a return policy of some kind, and be sure to use a credit card.

> Total Shield is composed of two separate electronic generators. One generator is a detector device that identifies the frequencies of the geomagnetic disturbances and grid lines from the environment and broadcasts them back out through a Tesla coil, which cancels out the disturbance. ### Jack Kruse — 2014-08-12T05:08:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-in-love-with-my-schumann-resonance-generator.11470/post-137625>

Colder temperatures increase the strength of a magnetic field. Why? Cold can have an effect on magnetism, because cold can have an effect of how fast or slow electrons move. This links the sun’s power to the magnetic field of the Earth. It also links the **_Schumann resonance_** strength to the sun. When the sun is shining the Schumann frequency is strongest. When the sun is shining the Earth’s magnetic field is weakest. This implies that the Schumann resonance strength varies inversely with the magnetic field’s strength. _**At night time,**_ **_the magnetic field is stronger on Earth._ So when you use a fake generator better be well thought out.**

# Thread: The cycle of life
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-cycle-of-life.11471/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 23
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### QuantumRadical — 2014-08-07T03:07:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-cycle-of-life.11471/post-137051>

Well, my father has finally passed away. He was a very unhealthy 84. He had been on a downhill ride since he had a blood clot two years ago. No, that's wrong, he had been on a downhill slide for a couple of decades, if not longer.

He was the perfect example of the drugged generation. He was on so many drugs before and after the blood clot that it seems a miracle that he didn't die of "medically prescribed drugs" long before a couple of days ago. And, what is more disheartening, is that he believed that the drugs were good for him because they were prescribed by his doctors. My mother is in the same boat, but that is a whole other story, for another time.

My father was a physical therapist and understood the value of human-on-human touch. He was also a fine athlete--he even had a chance to try out for the St. Louis Cardinal baseball team back in the day.

But back to the main story. He had an operation two years ago to clear his blood clot in his leg. Two weeks later, and not using his walker, he fell and broke his hip. I came home to help him through a couple of weeks of rehabilitation, but the drive to fix himself was just not there. And so, the family has watched him gradually just waste away. I believe that the drugs prescribed him affected his will to do anything, made him lethargic, made him lack any ambition to get well, or get in shape, or make any changes in his behavior that would have been a positive contribution to his quality and length of life. The drugs affected his will to live.

I routinely have refused drugs from doctors for a very long time. My wisdom comes from watching their affect on my parents. If I had followed my doctors' advice over the years, I would be taking statins regularly, probably blood thinners, and no telling what other concoction of toxic drugs.

Even today I see TV commercials often and routinely talking about various drugs that will miraculously improve your health, yet, the majority of the commercial is a guy talking as fast as possible about the possible side effects. That ought to tell you right away to just run away from whatever they are trying to sell you.

Anyway, please don't express any condolences on this forum. This is a great community and I know we are all sympathetic and empathetic of each others' lives and issues. I believe, and scientifically know, that Dr. Jack Kruse is onto something good and great. I also believe that 50 years from now, the era of the DRUG will be described as just as barbaric as bloodletting was in its time.

What I do know is that my Dad is not gone. His quantum coherence no longer exists in his corporeal form but affects all fields everywhere all the time throughout the universe.

Randy

### Jack Kruse — 2014-08-07T03:21:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-cycle-of-life.11471/post-137057>

Randy I am sorry to hear it, but here is the good news. Your Dad could not see it........so he did not live the truth to give him more time. You see it.
The truth is hidden in plain sight for us all.........and our desires blind us to it. You, my friend will likely affect many people with your insight and foresight. And that is an accomplishment your Dad had a big role in.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-cycle-of-life.11471/post-137124>

Randy

I struggle with this with my own parents. I can't get my mom off statins, after she had a CVD event when traveling in Australia 2 yrs ago. I've tried to educate her and my dad and they just can't see the forest for the trees. about a year ago I let them go and stopped talking to them about how they live, and the foods they eat and I just focus on loving them and spending time with them when we visit each other. They shaped me into who I am, and helped inspire my desire for knowledge. As they age, I realize they will continue to decline though they're holding on with great vitality and vigor at this point, though I don't anticipate they'll live as long as their parents or grandparents.

We live, and love those in our lives with their flaws and their blinders. What's interesting is that the people you think will be the most open to change aren't, and those who are open to change, are the one's you never expected to embrace it.

Godspeed to your dad.

This is very thin connective tissue that act like the SQUID in the brain to deliver the photons to the correct area of the bone. This light signal is rectified to a mechanical signal in periosteum to allow bone to resorb or lay new mineral where the stresses of gravity dictate it should be. Because these photons were in massive abundance from our star, and carried the MOST information at a very low entropy cost, about the photons located in our solar system. All matter is at its core is condensed matter, and photons from the sunlight change it. That is the fundamental story of evolution few see. ### Jack Kruse — 2014-08-07T06:17:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-cold-primordial-to-life-and-not-hormetic.11472/post-137083>

"**Microsystems for Control of Light-Matter Interaction at the Nanoscale**," Il Woong Jung, hosted by Daniel Lopez

Abstract: It has been shown that electromagnetic fields incident upon metal particles can be confined over dimensions on the order of or smaller than the wavelength of light. This strong light-matter interaction leads to an enhanced optical near-field at metallic interfaces or in small metallic nanostructures. These highly enhanced fields can be used to probe materials with subwavelength resolution (near-field scanning optical microscopy) and allow single-molecule surface-enhanced Raman spectroscopy detection. The ability to control these interactions (i.e., tune the plasmonic resonance) is highly desirable for developing ultrasensitive Raman and fluorescence detectors, highly efficient optical antennas, tunable single-emitters, nano-patterning, and novel nanophotonic devices.

In contrast to the strong light-matter interaction observed for metallic structures, photonic crystals are periodic dielectric structures that support many weak interactions to combine coherently to a strong interaction. By controlling the structure and geometrical dimensions, photonic crystal slabs can support guided resonances that couple to external radiation in ways that profoundly change its optical properties. By using nanofabrication tools such as focused ion beam, these nanophotonic elements can be integrated with micromechanical devices for added functionality to control the electromagnetic radiation.

In this talk, I will give an introduction to photonic crystal integrated microsystems and then present current research efforts focused on using micromechanical devices to actively control the optical properties of plasmonic nanostructures. In addition, just as microsystems have advanced the capability to manipulate light at the visible to infrared wavelengths, they can vary the focus, scan, modulate, and dynamically adjust the beam profile for X-rays, thereby enhancing the capability to probe materials at the nanoscale.

I hope y'all are getting my hints.

# Thread: Hospitalized with Ketoacidosis. Again (& Again).
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hospitalized-with-ketoacidosis-again-again.11476/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### FreeRangeKiwi — 2014-08-07T11:04:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hospitalized-with-ketoacidosis-again-again.11476/post-137098>

My wife, who is breastfeeding, has been paleo for around 4 years. Baby number 3 is 10 months old and (much to my ire ) she has not been habitually keto in 19 months. She leptin reset three years back and has dabbled in CT. She has always had an very athletic figure, although gained around 10kg in her mid 20s (now 31).

Currently, she is apparently healthy and eating in a primal manner, not restricting carbs to a great extent... ~100-150g/day, breastfeeding, but sedentary.

She drinks RO water, eats bone broth daily, puts a teaspoon of ev coconut oil or MCT on things now and then, eats fish several times per week (but seldom shellfish), and mainly sticks to skeletal cuts. Not perfect epipaleo; she leaves the household freakshow up to me and the baby.

Still, I would not have expected that she get admitted again for ketoacidosis, ketones at 7.26. The first time, a bug did the rounds (or course it didn't get me or bubs ;-) ). The boys stopped vomiting after 4 hours. She couldn't stop after 4 days, 5kg down. Fair enough. Ketoacidosis conceivable. A few months later, Noravirus did the rounds (missed me and the bubs, hit all the kids' cousins). 5 days of vomiting, 6kg down. Ok, still a valid excuse.

But this time, it was spontaneous. Ham, eggs, vegetables, parmesan + chocolate, rooibos + coconut cream for breakfast. Lunch was wild line caught tuna, avocado, some funky blue cheese, almonds, chocolate. Vlc, but nothing strenuous.

Now she's a bit gun-shy about going near ketosis. Fair enough, she could barely walk.

The things that I wonder about are:
\*each episode she developed pussy pimples on chest and neck
\*mould in the blue cheese (leading to a cytokine increase much like in the first two episodes > inhibiting ketosis in the face of ketogenesis)
\*possible hormonal issues that make her a superpuker (seldom can stop. hyperemesis G with first two pregs)
\*increased use of phone/switch to an old iphone 3g from a samsung mini
\*demands of breastfeeding
\*the fact that her typical postpriandal blood glucose has always been around 2.2 mmol (does she have crazy high levels of ketones naturally?)
\*lack of physical activity keeping her from metabolizing the ample ketones she apparently produces
\*insufficient DHA (the old, paleo vs epipaleo)

Am ordering a ketonix sport to keep tabs on things, but for now, we are wondering which tree to bark up. Anything to stop her subtle mother from dropping off more dishes of rice, potatoes, "gluten-free" baking and softdrink.

Thoughts?

How close together are your kids?

How is the health of her mother and grandmother?

what is your non native EMF exposure?

### Jude — 2014-08-07T13:20:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hospitalized-with-ketoacidosis-again-again.11476/post-137118>

Perhaps she needs a good blood work up. Sounds like you're familiar with the protocols?

### Shijin13 — 2014-08-07T15:12:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hospitalized-with-ketoacidosis-again-again.11476/post-137120>

If she's hitting Ketoacidosis my first concern is she T1.5D? Autoimmune Type 1 that onsets as an adult due to the immune system attacking the pancreas. I'd be pushing for a full Diabetes panel, to include the following tests: fasting glucose, HbA1C, Estimated Average glucose, Fructosamine, Glycation gap, Postparandial glucose index,, insulin, proinsulin, C-peptide, Proinsulin:C-peptide ratio, anti GAD and Insulin Resistance Score, and an autoimmune panel that looks at T1D markers at a minimum.

Breastfeeding will not drive the Ketoacidosis. Breastmilk production increase on a Ketodiet, as does the quality of the milk. I'd be looking at the autoimmune issue and T1.5D testing to rule it out. If you're doing ketosis right and you don't have beta cell issues you should be able to function w/o hitting ketoacidosis. the fact that she's paleo and hitting ketoacidosis says to me she's got issues w/her Beta Cells which produce insulin in her pancreas.

There are T1D and T1.5D who are actively controlling their autoimmunity with Keto diets and some exogenous insulin support and their are others who no longer need insulin.

You wife needs to start noshing oysters and sardines EVERY DAY. and she needs to start getting cold, in addition to getting your GP/PCP to run the labs to rule out T1D or T1.5D

### Jack Kruse — 2014-08-07T15:21:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hospitalized-with-ketoacidosis-again-again.11476/post-137123>

ketoacidosis is not ketosis........two totally different issues. Gretch is correct. Ketoacidosis is sign of diabetes of some type. Type 1 or 1.5 most common.

### Skimo — 2014-08-07T15:29:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hospitalized-with-ketoacidosis-again-again.11476/post-137126>

How was her glucose level during those episodes?
I have just read in keto clarity that ketoacidosis occurs with ketones above 20 and a high glucose in T1D patients and some rare T2D insulino-dependant ones.

Cows can get into ketoacidosis after calfing at the beginning of lactation, but they have a totally different fatty acids metabolism than us...

### FreeRangeKiwi — 2014-08-08T02:52:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hospitalized-with-ketoacidosis-again-again.11476/post-137225>

Thanks guys, you rock. Regarding lyme, it is not usually seen in NZ unless brought in. We don't farm. Wifi banned in house, phones in flight mode mostly, one neighbourhood wifi modem in range, sleep grounded, kids 1, 5, 7, Grandmothers 92, 91 (still farming). At a guess, mother (62) displaying signs of low preg + hysterectomy + gallbladder + sleep + teeth issues.

Throughout, BG ranged from 4 to 7 depending on feeding. Hba1c was 4.7% / 28mmol/mol. However, her sister has T1D, (and incidentally, coeliac in other family members, my bet is on mother too).

Aware that ketosis is not ketoacidosis, and am concious that it is nigh on impossible to achieve the latter nutritionally. Will chat to the endocrine doc and see about those labs Gretch. He's stumped, and feels she doesn't have other indicators of diabetes other than KA. Might have to push him to dig deeper though.

What about hydration? I should have pointed out that, in the first two episodes, dehydration and hyperkalaemia (total body) was apparent. Following the simplest line of thought, is it possible that she has just not replenished intracellular K (esp. if combined with possible low preg)?

### Shijin13 — 2014-08-09T01:22:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hospitalized-with-ketoacidosis-again-again.11476/post-137329>

not with KA. Endo or GP needs to dig deep and even consider stepping out side his box. the fact that DW sister has T1D - tell mes the Endo missed this on the first go around. He should have run those beta cell labs immediately.

# Thread: Project: Quantifying Health, Performance & Longevity
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/project-quantifying-health-performance-longevity.11478/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### biohacked — 2014-08-07T17:40:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/project-quantifying-health-performance-longevity.11478/post-137154>

**_Note: Post I made on another forum, putting here in case others interested._**

For a couple of months I've been working on a project to explore and find the most useful biometrics for health, performance and longevity. These include lab tests, data from tracking devices and anything else we can track on our bodies.

I've already interviewed experts in areas such as **HRV for work out optimization**, **oxidative stress vs antioxidant impact**, **brain optimizations via methylation, methylation in general and micro-nutrients** and **mitochondria** and am lining up many others.

These recordings will be going up in a podcast named "[The Quantified Body](http://thequantifiedbody.net/)" on August 15th. From then on I'll get an episode out every week on new biomarker related topics.

You can check it out here: [http://thequantifiedbody.net](http://thequantifiedbody.net)

**My story:** Data has been extremely useful for me managing the recovery process from my own chronic health issue - life changing in fact. But I've spent a lot more money than I needed to, if I'd know what I know now about labs, proxy biomarkers etc. So the goal of this podcast is to spread the word about the most useful biomarkers, what reliably changes the markers, and the topic of leveraging quantification of our bodies to improve our lives.

The more I've dug, the more I've realized how useful biomarkers truly are is not straightforward: cost, accuracy, practicality, research study quality backing their use, benchmarks available? healthy control benchmarks? optimum performance benchmarks? stability? access for consumers? access for physicians? risks of data corruption e.g. some blood labs have this issue if treated wrongly... etc. etc..

I have stacks of topics I'm interested in exploring in this area and people I'm interested in getting on the show because I think they have important things to say on the topic.

But I'd love to know **what you are interested in hearing about on biomarkers and their use** - I'd like this to become a really useful resource for you, for biohackers.. for citizen scientists etc who do a lot of this stuff already, but probably have questions and ideas about areas you'd like to know more about.

I've put up a 2 Question survey here for you to add topics and people for me to put on the list to chase down and explore. Literally 30 seconds to fill in.

[http://thequantified...ics-survey.html](http://thequantifiedbody.net/topics-survey.html)

I'll post a summary of the survey results in this thread once I've got a decent sample - as I think that could already be useful.

Also welcome any other ideas you have about this project. It's a learning curve for me, but already been very rewarding just with the first 4 episodes recorded - I'm sure you're going to get a lot out of them - especially the practical details.

### nonchalant — 2014-08-08T03:28:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/project-quantifying-health-performance-longevity.11478/post-137234>

My most important biomarker is EMF sensitivity. I have had a tickle in my throat (when I was taking iodine) around EMF, and also tinnitus, currently, as well as feeling a slight trickle of electricity around some electronics. Someone else got a rapid heartbeat, and some get a heat sensation on the skin.

Other biomarkers for me are tolerance of heat and cold, hunger, eyesight (blurriness), hearing acuity, pain tolerance, condition of skin. Someone else, if they get a cut, check their blood color and viscosity. Jack checks his night vision. For thyroid issues, the outer third of the eyebrow is an indication, as well as the fingernail lunulas.

### biohacked — 2014-08-08T07:22:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/project-quantifying-health-performance-longevity.11478/post-137265>

Thanks for the positive energy @[PaulG](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1838/) - I'm looking forward to launching it next Friday.

The interviews have been great so far and I feel it will get much better as I find my groove with it.

My story in brief is CFS/ Biotoxin Illness - if you're interested in it specifically I've written about it and put video of my presentation at last year's Quantified Self here:
[http://biohacked.net/treatment-strategy-for-me-chronic-fatigue-syndrome/](http://biohacked.net/treatment-strategy-for-me-chronic-fatigue-syndrome/)

### biohacked — 2014-08-08T07:26:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/project-quantifying-health-performance-longevity.11478/post-137266>

@[nonchalant](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/191/) I would describe those as symptoms and your health condition, rather than biomarkers.

I'll explain.

Biomarkers are specific quantified metrics like lab tests and measurements through tracking devices. The interest in biomarkers and quantification is that it is an objective look at the situation, whereas using symptoms tends to be more subjective. Symptoms are still useful but not as reliable for taking decisions and judging your status (health condition, performance etc.).

Hope that's helpful for you.

### biohacked — 2014-08-13T16:11:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/project-quantifying-health-performance-longevity.11478/post-137748>

As promised I'm posting the results of the survey below - in bold are the interviews that have already been done and will be published as episodes the first few weeks.

If any of these topics are of particular interest to you, keep the feedback coming and I'll put more effort into covering the areas most in demand. The interviews so far have been much better than I'd even hoped for - I'm truly humbled by the sharing of outstanding practical information on this topic and how engaged the guests have been.

**What Topics Would You Like to See Discussed on the Show?**

Tracking devices and their value/accuracy
**Best HRV monitor devices \[1st Episode covers this topic, coming up this weekend\]**
Blood tests - measuring and improving
**Increasing mitochondria function \[One of first episodes covers this, more to come in 2nd month\]**
Cognitive performance
**Methylation \[Several of first episodes cover this\]**
Detoxification
Athletic performance
Improving dopamine release in regards to athletic performanceOxygen Saturation Testing
Electrical muscle stimulation - measuring strengthening and healing
Brain fog/ anxiety
Nootropics
NeurofeedbackEffects of WIFI in schools - can it be quantified?
Sleep related Biomarkers and biohacking
**How accurate are lab tests and how are reference ranges determined and how have they changed in the past 50 years \[is an ongoing theme we cover in each episode in relation to biomarkers\]**
Use of natural medicine
**What biomarkers are there for various diseases such as Cancer? \[covered in part in episode on oxidative stress markers\]**
What biomarkers are there for various injuries such as head trauma?
Biomarkers for depression and adhd

**Who Would You Like Us to Get on the Show? And What Topics Would You Like Them to Talk About?**

**Dr. Ben Lynch (Methylation, Epigenetics) - \[already interviewed, in upcoming episode\]
William J Walsh (Methylation) - \[already interviewed, in upcoming episode\]**
Dr Amy Yasko (Methylation, Lyme, Genetics, Epigenetics, Helicobacter Pylori)
Chris Kresser (health in general, diet)
Dr. Jeffrey Bland
Steve Maxwell (Ayurvedic medicine)
Round table discussion - Ben Greenfield and Dave Asprey OR Dave Asprey and Jack Kruse \[3 way informal conversation of biomarkers\]

# Thread: So long for now….
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-long-for-now%E2%80%A6.11479/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### SeaHorse — 2014-08-07T22:25:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-long-for-now%E2%80%A6.11479/post-137194>

I just finished rereading Jack's post for newbies "Why newbies are important…." and felt it was time to put into action something I've been considering for a long time….redirecting my energy away from the computer and the forum. It has played such a pivotal role for me these past three years and now it's come down to the crux….I'm addicted to the site and keeping up with all the new information offered, life stories, Jack's blogs, hilarious exchanges etc. etc. It's all so amazing and has changed how I think and act profoundly and yet I sense very strongly that how much time I spend here is becoming a detriment. The compulsion to read everything has been feeling like the chemistry of addiction and that's something I know well and want to address. It is also why I'm making this a public declaration…you know…12 steps and all that.

If I am honest and look at my environment what I see is a number of things that I can still change and improve. I don't need to be in front of a computer for my job and yet I'm here in front of the screen for hours at a time. I'm heading back to the basic principals that we've all been presented with. More time in nature, less emf, lots of sun while it lasts, good water and as much cold as I can imagine. I have the opportunity to have these things right in my home and I don't want to miss it.

So, without getting sappy, I'm sending all of you a hug and an enormous amount of gratitude. JK, the biggest electron collector and donor of them all….thank you! This is my N=1 for the next few months…..I'll be keeping up with blogs and webinars, but aside from that I'm going to be living my life "in the flesh," ….let the experiment begin!!

xo Sarah

# Thread: Winter Mermaid's Adventures in Biohacking
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/winter-mermaids-adventures-in-biohacking.11480/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 208
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Winter Mermaid — 2014-08-08T03:11:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/winter-mermaids-adventures-in-biohacking.11480/post-137228>

I am excited to start what is going to be a long journey.

The following are the genetic/epigenetic factors that make me interested in doing the Leptin Reset and Cold Thermogenesis:

Paternal grandfather died of a heart attack. Maternal grandfather had a heart attack in his 50's and then developed T1 diabetes. Maternal grandmother has severe osteoporosis, paternal grandmother had breast cancer, got treatment, and then died of Alzheimer's. Maternal uncle, despite being a doctor and avid jogger, got high cholesterol and high blood pressure in his 40's.

My father has always been 30 to 50 pounds overweight no matter what he tries, with the exception of the paleo diet which caused him to lose all the weight. However he is addicted to carbs, went off the diet, and gained all the weight back promptly.

My mother is hypothyroid and suffered from a wide range of mysterious symptoms since giving birth to my younger brother: gut problems, random pain, migraines, depression- leading to multiple hospitalizations with no diagnosis. She went to a ton of doctors and none of them could figure it out. A few years ago she was finally diagnosed with limited scleroderma, an autoimmune disease. She believes this was caused by extreme stress in her 20's and a childhood of highly processed, non-nutritious food. She also realizes now that she was severely deficient in DHA.

I believe that may have contributed to me having a full year of colic when I was born. I literally didn't sleep more than 30 minutes at a time for my entire first year of life and had frequent screaming fits. I was breast fed for much of that year but again, her diet was quite poor.

From the ages of to 6 to 22, kindergarten to graduating college, I was continually stressed and miserable due to being forced to attend school. I wanted to read and learn on my own, and was very different than other kids. Had I been a child these days I would have immediately been diagnosed ADHD, which I actually was in college though I refused to take any drugs for it. I was relentlessly bullied from age 6 until age 16 or so and had very few friends, spent my time rebellious and frustrated and angry. I chose to go to college after much arm twisting from parents and hated every minute of it. At age 23 I got into grad school and loved life and learning for the first time EVER.

I had a hideous period of time in my mid twenties where I had jaw surgery to correct congenital palate deformities and terrible teeth. I had a bone graft from my lower jaw to my upper and it really did a number on me. Glad I did it, but very taxing physically and emotionally.

I was a very fit teen, but began gaining weight due to stress which would trigger crazy carb and fried food binges. I am 5 foot 6 inches and my ideal weight is around 130 pounds. Before beginning the leptin reset I was 165, my highest weight ever.

Despite all these epigenetic stressors I have the following things going for me: I have an iron stomach inherited from my father. Despite tons of antibiotics as a child and teen/young adult \[UTIs, soooo many\], as well as a horrible processed food diet, I have never had any digestive trouble whatsoever. I can binge eat LITERALLY a whole pizza, a whole cake, a milkshake, and a full plate of nachos in one meal and not have any intestinal disruption or distress. I missed my calling as a competitive eater, so it is rather obvious that I can benefit from the Leptin Reset.

I also seem to have a fast metabolism despite my eating. In the past I have had the eating habits of the obese but never got more than moderately overweight. Because of my medium large frame, and because I gain weight evenly all over, I have never looked very overweight even when I am. It all goes to my chest and butt, waist stays slimmer even at my fattest.

I also have always had great sleep, with a strong circadian rhythym as soon as I force myself to put down the book or computer and go to bed. I fall asleep quickly, stay asleep, and always get eight hours. I have arranged my working life so that I never need to wake up early and have a totally dark bedroom with blackout curtains.

My libido has always been quite high, leading me to wonder whether I have higher than average testosterone. In some respects my style of thinking and relating is quite masculine, but I look very feminine \[excess estrogen converting into testosterone? I don't know my biochemistry well enough\].

I also have work that I am absolutely passionate about, and at age 28 I am finally enjoying life to the fullest and want to A\] get in great shape for now and B\] avoid the fate of most people in my family in terms of neolithic disease. I am almost 29 and this is the age my mother was diagnosed with hypothyroid.

Luckily, I have total flexibility with my schedule to design an optimal life. I start a new job in September and I want to spend August doing the basic Leptin Reset so I can figure out how to design my schedule. I see people by the hour appointment so if I want to eventually exercise at 2 pm everyday that will be possible.

I am so grateful to have found this community around Dr. Kruse's work. It puts together a lot of pieces that I wanted to connect but could not quite understand, especially about the immense impact of neolithic disease on my family. I am very lucky in a lot of ways and I count being born at a time to take advantage of the paleo and epipaleo movements as one of them.

My ultimate goal, as I stated in my first thread, is to become a fat adapted, cold adapted winter swimmer. I want to learn how to do it well and safely for the rest of my life, not as a highly competitive chronic cardio type deal. I love swimming as a meditation and I am currently improving my technique with the Total Immersion videos, which I recommend to anyone who likes to swim for exercise. I live close to Walden Pond in Concord, MA, and there is a community of all weather swimmers that I would like to join.

It will be very helpful to chronicle my efforts here since real life support is lacking. I have wonderful friends, family and boyfriend but they do not like these protocols and think it is too extreme, though they give me lots of oxytocin otherwise \[exclamation point\].
PS. The exclamation point on my keyboard is broken so I seem very stoic. I usually use a lot of exclamation points because I am very excitable.

### Winter Mermaid — 2014-08-08T04:31:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/winter-mermaids-adventures-in-biohacking.11480/post-137241>

Day 1: Felt great physically but couldn't get motivated to do one bit of any kind of work or mental heavy lifting, or chores. Face dunking resulted in lovely feelings of heat in my whole body afterwards.

Day 2: The hardest, felt very weird and out of sorts, but no cravings just very low energy despite high fat and high fish intake.Day 1 of spot icing with compression shirt - well tolerated, got skin down to 55 degrees, cherry red with no urticaria.However at the ten minute mark I became nauseous and felt strange, truly a sensation I have never experienced. The feeling abated within an hour of the CT session.

Day 3:Woke up feeling better, ketone sticks showed I was in mild to moderate ketosis. Had lots of trouble stuffing down 50 grams of protein in the morning as my hunger had dropped. Had a bit more energy throughout the day. Gave my stomach a break and tried my homemade Ice Bra: an old sports bra soaked in water and frozen in the freezer. Wore it over my compression shirt and got the skin down to 55 degrees. I carry much of my extra weight here and wanted to see how my body tolerated this.

Day 4:Woke up with energy, forced down breakfast, was able to resume normal activities. Scale shows I have lost five pounds since Day 1 \[exclamation points, multiple\]. Down a bra size as well. Unbelievable.

Day 5 : \[today\]: I write for a living half the time and frequently get sore wrists and arms from lots of typing. I noticed I typed for hours and had no pain - must have been inflammation \[exclamation points\]. I can go all day without dairy but at night I feel like I "need" butter. Makes me feel sleepy and relaxed.

Overall feel sleeker and better than I have in years.Steadily more energy every day as my body gets used to using ketones. I have been eating bits of seasonal fruit and vegetables to see how many carbs will keep me still in ketosis. I am afraid to be deeply in ketosis and have been aiming for mild according to the test strips. Have had a bad kidney infection in the past \[UTI traveling up before I realized\], so leery of doing anything that would increase stress on the kidneys. Getting Dr.K's opinion on that matter is worth abandoning my anonymity \[exclamation point\].

I am trying to avoid dairy in general, though my Slavic heritage would suggest I'm well adapted to it. I am so caffeine sensitive that even decaf coffee gives me a high and then a depressed crash. Mania and then restless nonproductivity and, weirdly, SLEEPINESS. Too much coffee makes me tired. ### Winter Mermaid — 2014-08-09T14:44:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/winter-mermaids-adventures-in-biohacking.11480/post-137362>

I think last night may qualify for falling off the wagon - went out with a friend (who is also doing low carb and is flirting with trying epi-paleo) to an upscale place and got delicious headcheese, wurst, four kinds of raw cheeses, and pickles. High quality ingredients and made in-house, but more processed than I have been used to eating this last week. I also had five candied nuts, a tiny bit of sweet jam/relish, and apple slices. According to the ketostix I was still in ketosis immediately after and a few hours after that meal, so total carbs must have been under 50 or so.

Within an hour of getting home, I got a very painful single hive on my arm that appeared suddenly with a piercing sting- so bad I thought maybe a spider bit me. My whole arm was on fire. I iced it, and I think it is likely I had some kind of reaction to all the cheese or possibly to some ingredient used in the many fermented meat products I ate. Or perhaps it was simply too much protein. Not sure. I very rarely have allergic reactions to anything and I've never had an identifiable allergy. I should add that totally by accident yesterday was the first day I had NO DHA in my diet- I ran out of fish and didn't get more until later, and by then my friend wanted to go to dinner. I wonder if that has anything to do with it!

This is a minor setback (woke up feeling better but definitely not as slim as every previous morning of the re-set). Eating wheat would be a total loss and instantly bloats me 5-7 pounds of water weight overnight.

From now on, if I eat out, I am going to keep it real simple: fish/seafood and vegetables cooked in butter, or a steak and vegetables cooked in butter. At least on the first stage of the re-set.

> In my own world, yes to your comments... You get science from Jack and perhaps plain and simple words from me but we are on the same boat. The allergic reaction can be because you are in a constant "out of energy" mode but also a warning signal to get the hell out of here (or there). Listen to it, like it and do things to change it, and with that i do not mean yet another pill like most normal humans do.
>
> When i get stuck in everything from EMF hell to simply humans doing stupid things i first sense a lost of brilliance, i loose the details in life, the small things that makes me purr lika a cat. In a sudden everythings feels, pale... This is where i often just leave no matter what, always heading out in the wilds.
>
> Just reading a little about you above i think you have a very special ability that is just waiting to get the proper amount of energy. I sense a special mindset, often i do when people had a special childhood, like you did. The time to come for sure has some really though tests to put upon us living and for you, you have been trained in that from early years. If you manage to stay here even if it sounds goofy you will end up in some living creature for sure.
>
> You will be looking weird to many but i think you cope with that.
>

I feel like I found Narnia through the coats in the wardrobe. A real place, but people may not believe it exists until they see it for themselves.

I resonate with what you are saying, for sure. I am learning how to take care of myself N=1, not how anyone else thinks I should.

Today for breakfast I had a can of salmon, and spring water. Five hours later for lunch I had a can of sardines, a ripe peach, and spring water. Having the fish for those two meals immediately put me in a very different place. I've eaten regular paleo before and it never left me feeling this clear. Tonight I stayed up for a friend's birthday party but I chose to spend those electrons on my dear friend .

P.S. to the doc: I made the yam lasagna with beef and ricotta tonight and everyone raved about it, no leftovers! I am impressed. I'll cook my way through it.

Starting to realize that the reason I stopped reading MDA is because I felt the science was meh, it had become so mainstream and kind of dull, too focused on diet. I still love Mark and think the site does great things but I wondered why I didn't read it anymore . . . .

I was ready for the Narnia of quantum biological theories!

### Winter Mermaid — 2014-08-11T16:04:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/winter-mermaids-adventures-in-biohacking.11480/post-137554>

Way too much cheese and cream this weekend. Had some homemade flan at a party yesterday, I scraped all the caramel off (so delicious, also first sugar I have had in a week). Stayed in ketosis the entire weekend and this morning despite this, though it went to "mild" on the sticks. Though I have not been exercising this week, I did go kayaking for an hour on a very calm Charles River- some people had sore shoulders and arms this morning but I didn't- wonder if that is a side effect of ketosis. I usually have intense soreness after doing a new type of movement.

The rest of the party was in a quiet park in the city, we all ran around barefoot playing frisbee and flying kites in the sun for hours. I felt great- then went home and spent time in my living room after dark with roommates watching our gigantic TV and using my laptop (which I don't keep on my lap). Got a headache within an hour . . . . this EMF thing . . . hmm . . . . before going to bed I turned off the power strips connecting all the TV/cable/internet stuff. I can't kill the circuit without talking to roommates first.

My blue blockers are on the way.

### Winter Mermaid — 2014-08-12T14:09:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/winter-mermaids-adventures-in-biohacking.11480/post-137654>

Hi Caroline! I've been so busy with work it's been hard to keep up on the forums.

Trying to work on my EMF versus darkness: I have blackout curtains that I put on at night, otherwise there is enough blue light to read from because of surrounding houses and streetlights. These are heavy curtains and no air can get through, so I have a unit air conditioner in one of the three bedroom windows. However, trying to limit its use - so left one curtain/window open and used a sleep mask last night. Sleep mask came off in the night somehow and I woke up too early (6 am, usually with the curtains i wake at 7 am). Ugh.

### Winter Mermaid — 2014-08-12T14:16:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/winter-mermaids-adventures-in-biohacking.11480/post-137657>

Continuing to drop weight, actually a bit too quickly for my liking (I worry about skin/tissue elasticity maintaining itself). Only spring water, seafood twice a day, random bits of fermented vegetables.

My skin is clearing up dramatically. Used to get hundreds of mildly clogged pores on my face (not acne exactly) and they are disappearing. Feeling hot after meals and CT is humming along- still get nauseous every time I ice my torso. It seems to be specifically the spot below my navel, which the Chinese call the dan tien. It's a major energy center in the body and I wonder if it doesn't like being cold . . . trying to ice around it.

### Winter Mermaid — 2014-08-12T14:53:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/winter-mermaids-adventures-in-biohacking.11480/post-137659>

Oh AND I went on a recreational swim (nothing strenuous) at Walden at noon, and put zinc sunscreen everywhere but my back - I wanted to do an experiment. I usually burn red within 10 minutes but I have read on this forum that people tan better when their DHA is high.

No burn- a light golden tan! First time in my life. I was out there for one hour!

P.S. To the ladies \[mostly the pale white ones since we seem to age the most visibly\]: do you worry about photoaging and wrinkling of your skin with all of this sun exposure? Or is that mitigated by healthy diet and DHA levels? Even with a better ability to tan I would rather keep my face/neck/decolletage/hands out of the sun- I wear driving gloves in the car .

One thing I stopped doing long before the Leptin Re-Set was using chemical sunscreen, or any sunscreen except zinc- and that only if I was going to be outdoors for a long time in little clothing. I have a parasol, long cotton gloves, and shrugs that I wear instead. I've been doing 5 minutes outside in little clothing at solar noon on purpose since starting the re-set.

### Inger — 2014-08-12T16:05:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/winter-mermaids-adventures-in-biohacking.11480/post-137666>

well... i know i am going to get super wrinkly as I age, because i spend so much time in the sun! But I am not worried one bit. I think wrinkles look cute if they are the happy kind of wrinkles. They give an aging face character.... i was never a fan of wrinkelless skin as long as it comes because of being out in the sun - not from tobacco or worrying etc.
We just need to smile a lot and the wrinkles are going to be beautiful I am sure

### Inger — 2014-08-12T16:07:02+0300

You need to start tanning in winter/early spring whole body, then you will not burn as your skin adapts to the sun as its power increases. That is how I do, that is also what nature does, the sun power increases very slowly!

### Winter Mermaid — 2014-08-12T22:17:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/winter-mermaids-adventures-in-biohacking.11480/post-137686>

Ok, I think my Leptin is well on its way to being re-set.

I tried a bite of a banana today and I was shocked at how sickly-sweet it tasted! I could not eat more than the one bite, it was like eating candy. I promise you that on my epi-SAD \[I think that's a good name for half-paleo half-SAD eating\] diet, a banana did not seem like dessert unless it was fried in butter and drowned in maple syrup and cream.

I also had a chocolate craving after lunch so I bought a bar of dark stuff. I took one big bite, and after I swallowed it was like a sign went off in my gut that said "done"! I did not want anymore, before I could easily eat two of these. As an experiment I took one more bite, and chewing and swallowing it was a sharp decrease in pleasure from the first bite, shading into "unpleasantly close to too full."

This does not happen without the seafood/DHA. I could eat ten pounds of bacon and it wouldn't negate my desire for sugar.

You are doing great!

Love how sleepy CT makes me. Very relaxing.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-08-14T16:11:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/winter-mermaids-adventures-in-biohacking.11480/post-137828>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=137803)
>
> journals are where innovation is born.

Ideas are, almost inevitably, networks of other ideas. We take the ideas we've inherited or stumbled across, and we jigger them together into some new shape. When an idea takes a new shape or size the idea’s thermodynamics change and from this action a new idea or concept is born.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-08-14T16:17:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/winter-mermaids-adventures-in-biohacking.11480/post-137833>

In crawfish, blood cells are attracted to the niche, a nursery of baby neurons at the base of the brain. Each cell splits into two daughter cells, precursors to full neurons, both of which migrate out of the niche. Those head to two clumps of nerves in the brain called clusters 9 and 10.

[http://www.cell.com/developmental-cell/abstract/S1534-5807(14)00405-5](http://www.cell.com/developmental-cell/abstract/S1534-5807\(14\)00405-5)

### nonchalant — 2014-08-14T16:40:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/winter-mermaids-adventures-in-biohacking.11480/post-137836>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=137828)
>
> Ideas are, almost inevitably, networks of other ideas. We take the ideas we've inherited or stumbled across, and we jigger them together into some new shape. When an idea takes a new shape or size the idea’s thermodynamics change and from this action a new idea or concept is born.

When we come up with a new concept, a great idea that works well for us, I look back, and see that the idea was built on a previous idea that we had, that was only a little bit innovative. It solved a small problem. Who knew that when we came up with the small idea, that it would spawn greatness in just a year or two.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-08-14T21:20:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/winter-mermaids-adventures-in-biohacking.11480/post-137861>

OK..............I said this in CT 4:
“ I saw a man becoming transformed, by a bunch of Monks who lived in the Himalaya’s. It was said most of them lived to be over **_100 years old on little food, in the cold, and were incredibly productive. I wondered to myself, could this really occur in life_** or was it just part of the story?”

The more I read, the more the book brought me back to my days at the museum as a young boy. I began to think about it more.

I progressed on to the scientific papers I was sent. They main topics discussed were cytokines, leptin, cell membrane chemistry, and obesity. After a very short while things clicked. I stopped the majority of what I was doing and went to the medical school library. I was like a great white shark in a group of seals for 8 months.

Now go re read CT 5 with new eyes since now you have quantum changes to your neurons.

### Winter Mermaid — 2014-08-14T22:48:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/winter-mermaids-adventures-in-biohacking.11480/post-137862>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=137861)
>
> OK..............I said this in CT 4:
> “ I saw a man becoming transformed, by a bunch of Monks who lived in the Himalaya’s. It was said most of them lived to be over **_100 years old on little food, in the cold, and were incredibly productive. I wondered to myself, could this really occur in life_** or was it just part of the story?”
>
> The more I read, the more the book brought me back to my days at the museum as a young boy. I began to think about it more.
>
> I progressed on to the scientific papers I was sent. They main topics discussed were cytokines, leptin, cell membrane chemistry, and obesity. After a very short while things clicked. I stopped the majority of what I was doing and went to the medical school library. I was like a great white shark in a group of seals for 8 months.
>
> Now go re read CT 5 with new eyes since now you have quantum changes to your neurons.

I have now read all the things you suggested at the beginning of this journal- and CT 5.

It's amazing what a low standard of health and well-being we've come to accept as normal in the richest country on earth.

This especially interests me because I am a psychotherapist and I treat people with mental illness- almost all of whom have co-morbid conditions: obesity/anorexia, allergies, autoimmune issues, tons of digestive issues. I also see the total destruction wrought on people's bodies from anti-psychotic drugs (big belly, shortly thereafter diabetes and heart problems), and loss of libido from SSRIs. Ultimately what I would like to do is open a clinic where someone can get a complete medical/nutritional workup in addition to psychotherapy (which is still important because people tend to have an emotional attachment to being ill, as well as other issues not completely addressed by getting a healthy body).

Like Dr. Kruse, I see myself as experimenting on myself so that I can some day help them, I at least need some preliminary data and first-hand knowledge. For a long time now I have not believed that talking alone will help most people. I have big plans, and if I want to accomplish them I must be much healthier than I am.

During my training we are required to be in therapy, and I found that I instinctively could not trust a therapist who did not seem a lot healthier than me on every level. What could I possibly learn from them? I know many therapists who are overweight or suffer from other familiar health problems. I believe it is any healthcare worker's duty to be as healthy as possible to set an example. You cannot take a patient further than you yourself have gone, at least not in my field.

> It's amazing what a low standard of health and well-being we've come to accept as normal in the richest country on earth.

> This especially interests me because I am a psychotherapist and I treat people with mental illness- almost all of whom have co-morbid conditions: obesity/anorexia, allergies, autoimmune issues, tons of digestive issues. I also see the total destruction wrought on people's bodies from anti-psychotic drugs (big belly, shortly thereafter diabetes and heart problems), and loss of libido from SSRIs. Ultimately what I would like to do is open a clinic where someone can get a complete medical/nutritional workup in addition to psychotherapy (which is still important because people tend to have an emotional attachment to being ill, as well as other issues not completely addressed by getting a healthy body).
>
> Like Dr. Kruse, I see myself as experimenting on myself so that I can some day help them, I at least need some preliminary data and first-hand knowledge. For a long time now I have not believed that talking alone will help most people. I have big plans, and if I want to accomplish them I must be much healthier than I am.
>
> During my training we are required to be in therapy, and I found that I instinctively could not trust a therapist who did not seem a lot healthier than me on every level. What could I possibly learn from them? I know many therapists who are overweight or suffer from other familiar health problems. I believe it is any healthcare worker's duty to be as healthy as possible to set an example. You cannot take a patient further than you yourself have gone, at least not in my field.

Talk therapy makes mental illness worse..........DHA is the key and getting them out of their EMF oven.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-08-15T23:55:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/winter-mermaids-adventures-in-biohacking.11480/post-137969>

^^^^^Robin Williams.

### Winter Mermaid — 2014-08-16T01:55:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/winter-mermaids-adventures-in-biohacking.11480/post-137980>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=137968)
>
> Talk therapy makes mental illness worse..........DHA is the key and getting them out of their EMF oven.

I agree with your assessment with regard to 95% of practitioners (and I am being generous). There are many bad therapists, and any therapist that sends you towards psychiatric drugs is committing a well-intentioned act of harm.

I confess, I am not really a therapist. I don't do Cognitive Behavioral therapy or work on "coping mechanisms" which are useless without basic health of the body first. I was trained psychoanalytically, to work with deep drives and instincts. I also have personal experience with body psychotherapy, the entire point of which is to get the patient in touch with his energy. I use these techniques with patients and have found them to be very effective.

To give you just one example: I worked with a depressed, eating disordered vegan (not very surprising to the people on this board). This person restricted their eating, was suicidal, and also very into a strict form of religion. Over time, as they were able to trust me and to explore the reasoning behind this idealogy, we discovered that this person's veganism stemmed from a belief that their aggression (instinct to hunt/kill) was so destructive that it had to be rejected completely. Once they were able to work through these thoughts and feelings they were willing to listen to my advice to start eating clams and mussels (among many other things, including a full physical workup)! This was way before I knew about your site, but animal rights philosophers have said it is ethical to eat them since they can't feel pain, and even then I knew the importance of Omega 3 to depression from Dr. Barry Sears and his work. My patient became healthier. That is how I seek to work with people.

Many times people must work through emotional issues before they can seek help. I agree that the "help" they then seek is usually terrible (psychiatric drugs and bad nutrition and over-exercise). However, a therapist who is clued in to both primal instincts and proper research can be helpful.

Thanks guys! I needed the encouragement. Caroline, I read the post-reset FAQ and it's very helpful.

In general my body responds quickly- both positively and negatively. I've now completed three weeks of the reset and I'm going to keep going at least one more week, but I do feel I'm approaching the switch.

Last night I had dinner at a very fancy seafood place and had a delicious salmon tartare for dinner (Inger, it was RAW and I thought of you!). I also had some butterscotch pudding, first sugar I have had on the reset besides fruit and I wondered if I was setting myself back. ### Winter Mermaid — 2014-08-25T14:59:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/winter-mermaids-adventures-in-biohacking.11480/post-138836>

Also, I've noticed a weird phenomenon: Every few days, an area of my body will feel "soft," softer than usual, as though the fat beneath the skin is made of jello. I first noticed it on my arms - it was so strange! My arm felt like a squashy sausage and the skin and fat felt loose, which I assure you is not a normal condition even when I'm overweight.

After I go to bed and sleep 8 hours, I wake up and this area of loose skin and fat has become taut and leaner! The fat on my arms was slightly, but noticeably reduced. I felt the same thing on my stomach, thighs, etc, it's a different area every few days. I'm wondering if this could possibly be the cold thermogenesis killing fat cells? I assumed it could not work that quickly.

Notes from my modifications, trying to eat bigger breakfasts:

I found a diner around the corner from me that cooks everything in clarified butter, not gross canola oil like most places! I was overjoyed, ordered a brisket-cheddar omelet and added some butter to it, had a few thin slices of butter-fried potatoes since I read that most of your carbs should be in the breakfast meal. Brought some secret coconut oil in my purse and added it to decaf coffee with lots of cream. Felt so full, then immediately became very hot. Coconut oil seems to always make me hot.

Now waiting for my outdoor cold tub to fill. I bought a large storage container for my backyard and I've been using that- it's the perfect size and was only $25 from Home Depot. No room in my freezer for tons of ice, I buy it in bags around the corner at the bodega (there are some advantages to living in a city). Bought a cheap hose and I fill it from my kitchen sink. Can't wait to get in 30 minutes in 55 degrees.

### Winter Mermaid — 2014-08-26T23:21:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/winter-mermaids-adventures-in-biohacking.11480/post-138969>

Dr. Kruse,

I just spent a long time on the phone with the inventor himself! What a great guy, he answered my questions for over 30 minutes. Seems quite genuine and low-key, not a salesman type. He tried to talk me down to 10 gauss so it wouldn't be so expensive but after asking for a brief family/health history (and where I lived) he did recommend the 20 gauss. He offered to let me pay in 10 installments, which is affordable to me with the Kruse Forum discount. He said several studies are coming down the pike showing the benefits of various magnetic applications and also said that I should be taking sun chlorella when I eat fish to bind the methyl mercury. He also said effects of mercury are exaggerated with stress, ie high EMF - a vicious cycle.

Because I was diagnosed with ADHD, he said I might have a lot of detox symptoms using the 20 gauss and suggested I take DMFA for the first two months of sleeping on it. Gave me the number to get it from Canada and everything. He said anyone with autism or ADHD should do the same.

It's shipping this week. I really need my brain working in top form, so I'm willing to make the investment. And if for some reason after a trial of a few months I don't think it's doing anything I will gladly sell it at a discount to a member of this forum (something tells me I won't be doing that).

P.S. Marie you were so lucky to find it on CL! I searched and didn't find one anywhere. I do not mind buying straight from the inventor anyway and support his work if there is no way to get it for cheap.

I am a bit concerned that 4 feet around my bed is now unusable because you shouldn't hang out in the return field but I'm willing to hang out in the living room if that's what it takes .

This might be the craziest thing I've ever done but I'm all for experimenting.

### Winter Mermaid — 2014-08-28T16:12:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/winter-mermaids-adventures-in-biohacking.11480/post-139147>

Well gee, I guess I should have actually read to the end of that thread first! He does say his opinion:

Time to straighten out a big misconception. Bonalie mentions chlorella for detox? Why? it is an algae that has DHA. There is no real detox with a magnetico.
I really do not believe this at all. My bet is people get unusual reactions when they have a substantial deficit of DHA in their cells and this causes some thermodynamics fluctuations. If you continue to add DHA it goes away with time. Why? When you use a static unipolar magnetic field you are actively replacing "bad PUFA's" with the "correct PUFA" that allows for optimal toplogic control of energy, information, spin, and opto-phontonics in a cell. Moreover, most of you are blaming DHA or the pad, when in reality it is the breakdown metabolites of the bad PUFA's from lipid bilayer turnover. DHA is replacing these nasty actors.

In the context of a [nuclear magnetic resonance](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nuclear_magnetic_resonance) experiment you are all living in today on spaceship Earth, the Rabi frequency is the nutation frequency of a sample's net nuclear magnetization vector about a radio-frequency field. This distinction is important because life is lived in a conceptual nuclear magnetic resonance experiment today because of the variant non native EMF of modern technology. I must make a distinction here between the Rabi frequency and the static magnetic unipolar field of the magnetico sleep pad. The pad offers a **_subtle control of spintronics_** in a world gone wild because it deals mostly with the Larmor frequency.

The Rabi requency in my view is the more important action because is deals with atomic transitions in a given light field. Since our brain uses an electron as conduit to get to the photon power by using mitochondrial cytochromes and our proteins to harvest this power, the Rabi frequency is the more important frequency than the Larmor frequency. Even Bonalie is clueless about this. You should not be, because the optics dictate this.......not the electronics of precession.

Our brain is designed to recapitulate a red giant thermodynamically using optophotonics. So far the magnetico is the only tool we have to deal with spintronics of an electron in a highly energized environment. Is it perfect? No, but it is the best we have today. In this way it can be thought of Newton’s laws of physics in 1890. Good enough for 99.9% of motions in the cosmos, but it could not explain Mercury’s perihelion. So the magnetico will help alot........but it is not perfect. This is where Bonalie and I differ big time in the application of his pad. The Rabi frequency is distinct from the [Larmor frequency](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Larmor_precession), which characterizes the precession of an electron via transverse nuclear magnetization about a static magnetic field.

In OSF 5, I explain in detail that DHA is designed to break down to the most anti inflammatory metabolites called resolvins and protectins. The same break down products are not found with other" bad PUFA's" that have substituted for DHA in your cells, hence their metabolites are very inflammatory........and they can cause these unusual reactions that people often blame on seafood. Rarely is it the DHA or the magnetico. The magnetico helps you replace bad PUFA's and this "detox response is good not bad thing. The longe it lasts the more bad PUFA's you original had. Here you can learn about how far away your redox really is. Most paleo people, alternative medicine, and paleo leaders have made this mistake in their writings on forums with the respect to DHA and excessive omega 6 or 9 replacments. Peskin and KAne have also tripped over this in my view.

\*\*\*\* Still going to join tomorrow .\*\*\*\*
