Hi there! Thanks for the kind words. This is not an easy process!

I will take this opportunity to say that I am having awful carb cravings today. Fantasizing vividly about eating layer cake and pizza.

Guess what is different about the last 24 hours? I was dumb last night, went to bed really late after watching TV with roommates, and was too tired to put up my blackout curtains. Slept 8 hours but in a room full of ambient blue light from street light and neighbors.

Wow, what a differences. Light and carb cravings seem to go together directly for me. The thought that it will takes years of this diet to completely replace bad fatty acids with DHA feels discouraging right now, because that means every foray into pizza/cakeville will set me back.

Also think that non-butter dairy is not great for me - I have no gut symptoms but too much cream makes me feel soft around the middle. Subtle but there.

I joined the Gold today to give myself some motivation!

### Winter Mermaid — 2014-08-30T15:18:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/winter-mermaids-adventures-in-biohacking.11480/post-139390>

I feel like my Gold membership is already worth it just for the Women and Fat Loss Webinar, which I listened to last night. I say listened because I turned off the screen to my computer and hung out on my floor doing spot icing CT on my stomach the whole time I listened to it! I've been doing the baths, and now the spot icing feels so easy! Before it was uncomfortable and nauseating, now my belly gets ice cold and I don't feel any discomfort.

So here's what happened: I was so miserable-feeling I did decide to eat some pizza. I set off to walk to the store (around 5:30 pm), and found that as I walked vigorously my craving really diminished. Still, I was determined: I ordered slices and a big chocolate chip cookie to eat while I waited. After three bites of cookie my gut (NOT my brain or my mouth) said: "meh." It stopped being compelling, so I threw it away. When I got my pizza the same thing happened: a few big luscious bites, then "meh." I can't recall ever having left pizza or a cookie unfinished.

As I was eating I had a pretty big revelation and put things together. "It's not about the food." I kept repeating that in my head- I think I finally really get it. Eating these few bites didn't matter- it was WHY I felt compelled to eat them that did. Here were the reasons:

1\. It's time to add some mild-to-moderate exercise back in, in the proper window of the afternoon. The walk made me feel great and had a positive impact on my leptin levels. If I had gotten that pizza delivered I bet I would have eaten a lot more. I picked a far-away shop just to give myself a vigorous walk!

2\. I have not been careful about blue light at night for a few days. I forgot blackout curtains and watched TV until midnight.

3\. Lack of CT- after four solid days in a row of ice baths I got "too busy" and skipped it.

It's not about the food. I walked home and put on the Women's webinar and boy, the Doc is way more coherent speaking than writing (his forum writing is rather beatnik poet, and I say that as a fan of beatnik poetry). I absorbed so much info and it made so much sense. I AM much more sensitive to stress and any environmental changes than any men in my life, and every time I've gained weight has been because of stress making my leptin levels go crazy. Even during low stress times, if I have too much light exposure, wrong/not enough exercise, and lack of CT I WILL get these crazy cravings.

So I dug out my lightbulbs from lowbluelights and put them in two lamps. I never had blue light exposure after sunset and put in my blackout curtains right away. I turned off all my devices and read in bed for hours until I was tired. Honestly could have gone to sleep any time after 9 pm, but I feel quite lonely when I'm the only one awake at 5 am. So read until 11 pm, went to sleep and woke up at 7:30 am.

What a difference. Despite eating some crap yesterday, I feel better than I have in days. The night spot CT and ABSOLUTELY NO BLUE LIGHT brought my inflammation levels down and I feel completely well rested. The amazing thing is that even with blue light and stress and bad food I always can get 8 hours, but it will not be a fully restorative 8 hours.

I am going to keep working my way through the webinars. I am impressed with how much info I can assimilate that way, and now I have the perfect thing to listen to while CTing. Love the ice baths but if I don't have time, a simple ice pack on the belly and upper back are so easy- no excuses. I will do it every night unless my body tells me not to. And NO BLUE LIGHT. I will be strict with it.

### Winter Mermaid — 2014-08-30T15:31:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/winter-mermaids-adventures-in-biohacking.11480/post-139392>

It's. Not. About. The. Food. Need to get that tattooed underneath my eyelids so I am always reminded.

Also in more personal news, my boyfriend is not even a little bit happy about all of this. He thinks this is crazy and does not want to change his diet or lifestyle (which is fine, I don't pressure him) but he hates what this does to "our" schedule. He wants to watch TV late at night, eat "normal" food, etc. I cook normal food for him and then just eat the protein and fat and vegetables.

He thinks the CT is absolutely crazy and possibly dangerous (he's a hot water guy, loves sauna and hot tub and hates being cold) even though I've explained the science to him. He does want me to be healthy, but thinks that I can be healthy doing "normal" things like exercising 3-4 times a week, eating a "balanced" SAD diet and that will be fine. He says this is fringe science and a cult of personality, etc. He's such a wonderful partner in every other way but does not approve of biohacking and thinks it is weird and frightening and obnoxious. I am sad about that but I am not going to ignore the results of N= 1.

I was reluctant to share that, this being the internet and all, but it's been bothering me and I figured others on the forum had had similar experiences with spouses and partners thinking this is crazy.

I have NOT told him about the Magnetico. He would decide I belonged in the looney bin.

### notsoperfick — 2014-08-30T16:04:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/winter-mermaids-adventures-in-biohacking.11480/post-139393>

"During my training we are required to be in therapy, and I found that I instinctively could not trust a therapist who did not seem a lot healthier than me on every level. What could I possibly learn from them?"

I feel that way about doctors, sitting there with rolls of fat and smelling of cigarettes and telling me that I should lose weight, don't they see the irony? They appear to value toeing the party line as far more important than getting to grips with why generic health advice isn't working for a lot of their patients.

I love your journal, you are working things out for yourself and not allowing yourself to be unduly influenced but using the advice and being thoughtful about the effects on you personally. It's helpful and interesting, I like the style and the navel dwelling .

It's a tricky issue trying to become healthier when you aren't supported by those whose views would otherwise count. If you aren't imposing on your partner what you are trying on yourself, where's the conflict? CT hasn't killed or otherwise damaged you, nor has changing your eating habits. I suspect a little insecurity is rearing its head, maybe he needs reassurance that all your efforts to change won't affect your feelings for him. The trouble is can you really affirm that, people who make changes like losing a big amount of weight can become a different person and why not? If your best beloved won't come along for the ride, maybe he will get left behind lol. You have to do what's best for you no?

> I feel that way about doctors, sitting there with rolls of fat and smelling of cigarettes and telling me that I should lose weight, don't they see the irony? They appear to value toeing the party line as far more important than getting to grips with why generic health advice isn't working for a lot of their patients.
>
> I love your journal, you are working things out for yourself and not allowing yourself to be unduly influenced but using the advice and being thoughtful about the effects on you personally. It's helpful and interesting, I like the style and the navel dwelling .
>
> It's a tricky issue trying to become healthier when you aren't supported by those whose views would otherwise count. If you aren't imposing on your partner what you are trying on yourself, where's the conflict? CT hasn't killed or otherwise damaged you, nor has changing your eating habits. I suspect a little insecurity is rearing its head, maybe he needs reassurance that all your efforts to change won't affect your feelings for him. The trouble is can you really affirm that, people who make changes like losing a big amount of weight can become a different person and why not? If your best beloved won't come along for the ride, maybe he will get left behind lol. You have to do what's best for you no?

Navel dwelling! I love it. I hope I don't live there, only visit from time to time. Because I'm a therapist I am usually on the receiving end of such reverie- which I quite enjoy.

I know it is a risk of losing your relationship if you make a big personal change during it- I have seen it many times. We have a lot of love for each other but we are very different kinds of people. He says he is "unsettled" by the changes, which is his right. He hates my blueblocker glasses, he hates that I don't eat the same food at the same meal, and additionally he loves my body the way it is and would prefer if I don't lose much weight. I told him I'm going to let my body decide what it will look like when I have all biochemical cylinder's firing (at which point he said "I'm dating a mad scientist. Good thing I like you so much"  ).

He wants to watch TV late at night, eat "normal" food, etc. I cook normal food for him and then just eat the protein and fat and vegetables.
>
> He thinks the CT is absolutely crazy and possibly dangerous (he's a hot water guy, loves sauna and hot tub and hates being cold) even though I've explained the science to him. He does want me to be healthy, but thinks that I can be healthy doing "normal" things like exercising 3-4 times a week, eating a "balanced" SAD diet and that will be fine. He says this is fringe science and a cult of personality, etc. He's such a wonderful partner in every other way but does not approve of biohacking and thinks it is weird and frightening and obnoxious. I am sad about that but I am not going to ignore the results of N= 1.
>
> I was reluctant to share that, this being the internet and all, but it's been bothering me and I figured others on the forum had had similar experiences with spouses and partners thinking this is crazy.
>
> I have NOT told him about the Magnetico. He would decide I belonged in the looney bin.

It is not about food. From EE6:
SQUIDs work because of a fundamental property of superconductors: When exposed to an applied magnetic field, a ring of superconductors generate currents which produce [magnetic flux exactly canceling the external field.](http://www.nature.com/nature/journal/v492/n7429/fig_tab/492358a_F1.html) When the current reaches a critical value (determined by a junction or weak link in the ring), it jumps by a discrete amount, allowing a quantized amount of flux to penetrate the ring. Measuring the effect of that action on the current across a junction allows one to measure the strength of the applied field, and SQUIDs are routinely employed to detect very weak fields such as those produced by brain waves or nerve impulses in muscle tissue. [Learn more about the controllable atom SQUID here.](http://jqi.umd.edu/news/first-controllable-atom-squid)

To my knowledge no one in neurobiology realizes that the pia mater, CSF water and the DHA cell membranes of neurons form the biologic SQUID in our subarachnoid space for the [quantum computer in our head](http://forum.jackkruse.com/energy-and-epigenetics-5-the-quantum-brain/). To form a SQUID, all one needs is two superconductors separated by a thin insulating barrier. The pia forms this barrier between CSF and the pi electrons of the cell membranes in neurons. This is how the human brain detects the earth’s native electromagnetic field. It is also how modern technology disrupts that ability as well. When this sense is lost, you lose the coupling of metabolism to food and sleep. This effect succeeds every other etiology for illness your brain can imagine. This is why food is not the primordial problem for neolithic disease generation. **Loss of circadian sense is.**

Nevertheless Mr. (?) Prana, one needs to be careful, "no man is an island", we all need somebody!

“When you think yours is the only true path you forever chain yourself to judging others and narrow the vision of God. The road to righteousness and arrogance is a parallel road that can intersect each other several times throughout a person's life. It’s often hard to recognize one road from another. What makes them different is the road to righteousness is paved with the love of humanity. The road to arrogance is paved with the love of self.”
― [Shannon L. Alder](http://www.goodreads.com/author/show/1391130.Shannon_L_Alder)

I have seen it many times in my work: when a family is very troubled and one member decides to become healthier - whether physically, emotionally, financially or socially- it can lead to envy and retribution, and ultimately to the person cutting those ties and truly individuating. It is not always required, but it is always a risk one takes.

Love and connection are quite important to me but not at the price of personal integrity. It's always a push-pull.

### WereBear — 2014-09-01T17:10:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/winter-mermaids-adventures-in-biohacking.11480/post-139551>

Congrats on the book breakthrough! That is a high energy activity I hope to get back to myself. Soon. I've already resumed works on my short pieces, which is a great relief... and my job has stepped up the writing part of it, as well. Writing really calls for concentration.

My own DH is understanding and supportive, but constrained by years of wrestling with his own chronic illness (CFS/ME) and trying a LOT of things already (eliminating fluorescents in his home, weird food combinations, bizarre treatments etc) which didn't work and left him a skeptic in some ways. But he sees his role as trying to keep me from "running amok" since I'm normally a person of HIGH enthusiasm. He willingly curtails our mutual TV watching, switches lighting at sunset, makes his own meals, and doesn't let his erratic sleep schedule interfere with my own. Seeing my improvements has encouraged both of us, and will help me make bigger improvements down the road, I'm sure.

It seems to me that a great many people pathologically fear change. Oh, they WISH for it, or think they do, but fundamental change frightens them. As a peer counselor, I've lost track of the number of people who want to live their same lives... only with more money. Who won't leave an abusive partner... just wish they would stop being abusive. Who won't leave that awful job even though it's awful, because being miserable is not enough reason for them to be proactive about bettering their situation.

Sometimes I think that adolescence was enough forced change for many people's lifetimes!

### Winter Mermaid — 2014-09-03T01:45:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/winter-mermaids-adventures-in-biohacking.11480/post-139730>

Some updates, small but significant:

\-My fingernails are far, far harder. I used to be able to rip them absentmindedly with my other nails, and now I can't. I'm not sure how that could have happened in less than one month.

\-There are occasional days when I can't eat fish- and those are the days that I stall in weight loss and feel more relief from CT (which must mean I'm more inflamed). I believe I saw someone else say that 'round these parts!

\-I'm being drawn to longer CT sessions or doing it twice a day.

I don't think I'm optimal enough to try it this winter (and maybe it's not wise for a female of childbearing age) but I was thinking if ketosis is natural in winter, wouldn't mild starvation (ie mild calorie restriction) also be indicated?

Woke up fully rested after only five hours- incredibly rare for me, possibly has never happened before. I was still tingling and pulsing all over my body when I woke up. Excited at the idea of potentially sleeping less- even ten minutes less a day adds years to your life!

Ha, that's an angle I did not expect! But the LD50 of LSD is enormously high- it would take many thousands of doses to even get close to toxicity. Microdosing and low doses are associated with positive improvement to mental health as found by numerous studies. It still should not be done often, at high doses, or if a person is predisposed to schizophrenia.

I will assume unless I see proof otherwise (open to it) that the beneficial long-term effects from small doses therefore increase electron flow across different brain regions.

Caroline: Psylocibin (found in magic mushrooms), LSD, DMT (found in the tea ayahausca, made of a DMT containing leaf and another plant), marijuana (which is actually a mild hallucinogen if taken at sufficient doses) and ibogaine (from an African plant) have all been found to have profound positive impact on people with trauma, addiction, cancer and depression. Research is difficult to do in America because they are all declared Schedule I Drugs, with no medical value!

Ayahausca can be like ten years of therapy in one 8-hour experience. One needs to be very careful in finding a trustworthy administrator of these substances, take the right dose, and be ready for the transformation.

I haven't seen that one yet but I will today!

### Winter Mermaid — 2014-09-11T17:03:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/winter-mermaids-adventures-in-biohacking.11480/post-140640>

Biggest success to date: drastic reduction in ADHD symptoms.

I just started my new job at an agency. I have had paperwork problems in every single job before this- I hate doing it, I procrastinate it, I don't do it according to the rules (because I hate it so much I skimp on detail) and then get negative attention from supervisors, etc. My over the top hatred of bureaucracy and red tape have interfered with my ability to do the administrative side of my job. The clinical part is always a joy, and I've never had any problems in that department.

So far it's been two days and I have completed ALL my paperwork for all new clients. This is a first for me. Right after each session I dove right into the 20-page assessments and had them done within 30 minutes of the appointment ending! It often takes people an hour or more to complete these. I had no urge to procrastinate, I was not filled with paralyzing rage, and I was eager to get it over with.

Unbelievable.

I decided that clinic days - when my EMF exposure will be huge - I'm going to be ZERO carb with high DHA. And hopefully ice baths before and after work. I did that the last two days and seems to have helped immensely. I also credit the Magnetico and the awesome sleep I've been getting. Still feeling tingling when I lay down on it, but it's kind of pleasant.

I brought my wifi-recieving laptop on the bed (Dr. Bonlie said it probably wouldnt hurt my computer and it hasnt), and it HURTS! It gives me palpable pain and discomfort in my fingers, hands, and forearms to use it on the Magnetico. No idea what causes that but now I only use the computer outside my bedroom.

P.S. Caroline I have a big test to study for tomorrow but to celebrate I will watch the Webinar. And I would love to go to Santa Fe and hang out!!! .

### Inger — 2014-09-13T21:04:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/winter-mermaids-adventures-in-biohacking.11480/post-140875>

Well, I have worked for over 10 years as a waitress... in restaurants serving raw meat like carpaccio, I was used at it when I started eating all raw. People here eat "mett" on the bread and that is --> minced, raw pork..lol no one is afraid of that. You get it at every corner

In the beginning it was more like a raw vegan diet i did, with lots of fruit, I ate some raw fish and meat tho. I never thought they were bad for me. I was not scared of bacteria either as I just read up about all the dangers, and they seemed way bigger to me with cooked foods  so why would I be afraid? I got really bad food poisoning only once in my life and that was from cooked spinach soup... Botulism is the really dangerous one and that can as well be found in canned stuff and sausages - people get botulism from canned olives but no one are scared to eat those?
So I figured in my head the fear of raw animal food, even if rotten, was nonsense. And how comes, I did wonderful with it and cannot stop

If you have fears read up about what you are afraid of - that always helped me to get the proportions right.

Many things does not make sense at all, people are afraid of eating berries in the forest here because of a parasite - this parasite you get way easier from dogs than from berries... and every other here has dogs.. imagine.. how comes they do not think about that if they choose to be afraid?

I wanted to say.... i quit eating the high fruit raw diet because it did me no good long term.. and that is about 5-6 years ago and since then I have been eating almost only raw meat and raw seafood.

Dr Kruse said in his Nourish Vermont 2017 talk that aromatic amino acids absorb UV light, that they have a benzene ring which is a photon trap.
Psychedelics are indeed known to make us more sensitive to light... I tend to think they make us more sensitive to the "wave"/EMF side of reality, more than to its "particule"/material side. So their effects are grandly affected by the set and setting in which they're used, which is basicaly the intention and the EMF environment. Intention is known to have effects in quantum physics, right?
So, as far as i understand it, that's why they'll end up having positive effects during a healing ceremony in the middle of the jungle in an environment that's high in native EMFs and very low in nnEMFs, and that's how they may end up having more adverse effects if taken for fun in an environment high in nnEMFs and low in native ones. And medicinal properties will be activated best when the body is grounded.
That's how experienced psychonauts may end up telling you it's best to use them in nature with an intention that benefits Life! But maybe a magnetico mattress will do if nature is not available aournd?A regular grounding mat can do too it the ground is clean and if there are low nnEMFs arnound.

# Thread: Curves's remedial corner . . . please help me to understand more deeply . . .
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/curvess-remedial-corner-please-help-me-to-understand-more-deeply.11483/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Curves — 2014-08-08T23:15:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/curvess-remedial-corner-please-help-me-to-understand-more-deeply.11483/post-137318>

What I love about this community is that there is such a collection of brilliant minds, generosity and open-mindedness. It's my experience that those traits don't often go hand-in-hand.

Since I'm struggling to get a deeper understanding of how all the science fits together, I would be most appreciative of your help.

Right now I'm trying to figure out **how the timing of food impacts sleep and how sleep impacts metabolism.** I'm particularly interested in the **peripheral clocks and circadian cycles**.

This may sound like a 3rd grader but here goes . . .

I understand
If you eat 3-4 hours before bed your insulin spikes. This spike prevents leptin from entering the hypothalamus at midnight and results in reduced autophagy and GH (thank you Jude). I also understand that carbs spike insulin more than protein and fats (that holds true for leptin as well)

Question:

Conversely, does the insulin spike and subsequent disruption of autophagy affect your sleep? Is it just that you don't get the benefits of sleep if your insulin spikes or does it prevent you from entering phase 3 or 4 of deep sleep?

Confusion: Why have scientists discovered that when you’re sleepy, your body produces more of the “hunger” hormone ghrelin and less of the “satiety” hormone leptin? Is it that if Leptin can't enter the hypo, there is more of it circulating and the fat cells don't need to release more of it? Maybe this is different for LR and LS individuals.

I have a lot more questions, but thought I'd start here.

Much appreciation to you!!!

### caroline — 2014-08-09T02:57:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/curvess-remedial-corner-please-help-me-to-understand-more-deeply.11483/post-137340>

I don't know how much help I can be ..... but one thing I do know for sure ...for me....

I need to eat between 4:00pm and 5:00pm at the latest. My digestions is optimal and I sleep really well.

### Lahelada — 2014-08-09T03:07:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/curvess-remedial-corner-please-help-me-to-understand-more-deeply.11483/post-137342>

Hi Curves,
I think that maybe you are mixing up two sleepy states. Theren. is the natural sleepiness at nightfall and thenthere is the exhaustion sleepiness at 4pm in the office that has you reaching for the chocolate bar. In the latter state your body is responding to an emergency and ghrelin is releasad as it does not see sleep but a low energy state.

### Jude — 2014-08-10T05:34:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/curvess-remedial-corner-please-help-me-to-understand-more-deeply.11483/post-137452>

Sorry about not addressing yr question ....but........ just read this interesting info re magnetism and sleep/autogaphy/hormone cascade etc and the 12mn effect!

[http://jackkruse.com/energy-and-epigenetics-4-light-water-magnetism/](http://jackkruse.com/energy-and-epigenetics-4-light-water-magnetism/)

How the Magnetic Depletion Effect Drains Living Things of Energy

1.  It adversely affects the magnetic resonance, which is a process were the protons and electrons in your tissues lower their energy levels during autophagy in sleep. This is what powers the PPP at night when we are supposed to be the most chemically reduced during sleep. **This lowered energy state occurs simultaneously when we release all of our anabolic hormones from 12-2 AM after four hours of dark. The release of these chemicals are tied directly to the magnetic field strength during sleep.**

### Jack Kruse — 2014-08-10T06:17:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/curvess-remedial-corner-please-help-me-to-understand-more-deeply.11483/post-137453>

Magnetism is tightly coupled to cold temperatures. Colder temperatures increase the strength of a magnetic field. Why? Cold can have an effect on magnetism, because cold can have an effect of how fast charged particles move. This links the sun’s power to the strength of the magnetic field of the Earth. It varies during night and day. It also links the Schumann resonance strength to the sun. When the sun is shining the Schumann resonance is strongest. When the sun is shining the Earth’s magnetic field is weakest. This implies that the Schumann strength varies inversely with the magnetic field’s strength. At night time the magnetic field is stronger. This allows for magnetic repletion of protons and electrons at night. It is at this time the energies of the subatomic particles are given off to the proteins in our tissues. This is coupled to hormone release of our anabolic hormones. This is why OSA is associated with low sex steroid hormones and Growth Hormone and high leptin levels.

> "People who ate fish once per week were just as well off as those who ate it more frequently"

Additional DHA in the week might go to other uses, like improving eyesight, immunity, skin, hormones, etc. Or else the people who financed the research don't want everyone to run out and buy lots and lots of seafood.

### caroline — 2014-08-10T10:37:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-tilted-quilt-random-musings-4.11488/post-137465>

I love when Dr. Kruse does these ...not as much brain pain!

It is great to go back and read old blogs and realise that my comprehension is better!

I love this quote from CT#6 ..... "you must live congruent to your biology!

Do you have a favorite quote? one that brings it home for you?

I am constantly amazed to realise that Dr. K. did his quilt document so many years ago.... and he has been patiently and surely and with the utmost focus dragging us all forward .....

talk about heavy lifting!

### caroline — 2014-08-10T11:47:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-tilted-quilt-random-musings-4.11488/post-137467>

Dr. Kruse asks us in this blog ..... How discerning are we when "something popular" could be the wrong thing for us?

I am wondering if I can look backward and see a defining moment for me ....... I have to think about this....

Can you see a defining moment when you realised that often things aren't as they seem ...no matter how popular?

Why did I buy every diet book ever printed ..... and think this is going to be the one?

How did all of us who have been here from the beginning and lots and lots of newbies too..... know instinctively that this all just made so much sense?

This must mean that we are discerning ... and given a choice.... we can make good decisions.

To be sure .... it has been a struggle for all ......but we are learning to follow the science and we are learning that Mother Nature knows best and she is the ultimate task master.

In some quarters we are seen as lambs without a mind of our own .......that couldn't be farther from the truth.

Thank goodness that Dr. K. has never given up on us!

### Jack Kruse — 2014-08-12T19:18:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-affected-by-emf-and-their-links.11496/post-137676>

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/15306138](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/15306138)

### Josh — 2014-08-12T19:59:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-affected-by-emf-and-their-links.11496/post-137679>

> **Analysis of the seasonal pattern in suicide.**
> [Partonen T](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed?term=Partonen%20T%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=15306138)1, [Haukka J](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed?term=Haukka%20J%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=15306138), [Nevanlinna H](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed?term=Nevanlinna%20H%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=15306138), [Lönnqvist J](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed?term=L%C3%B6nnqvist%20J%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=15306138).
> **[Author information](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/15306138#)**
>
> **Abstract**
> **BACKGROUND:**
> Suicide has been attributed to social and psychological factors but also to geophysical effects. Of the latter, changes in solar radiation and geomagnetic activities may contribute to the frequency and the seasonal pattern of suicides.
>
> **METHODS:**
> We studied with a population-based, nationwide analysis all the individuals who committed suicide (n=27,469) in Finland during the period of 1979 to 1999. The daily data on the number of suicides, and the mean and maximum levels of geomagnetic activity were compiled and modelled with Poisson regression using the number of inhabitants in each province as the denominator. Time series analysis of monthly numbers of suicides was carried out using a seasonal-trend decomposition procedure.
>
> **RESULTS:**
> There was a strong seasonal effect on suicide occurrence (P<0.00001), the risk of suicide being greatest in spring. The seasonal effect was most pronounced when the number of suicides was relatively low. High levels of solar radiation activity were associated with the increased risk of suicide (P=0.00001), but the effect of geomagnetic activity was weak.
>
> **LIMITATIONS:**
> No individual data on alcohol consumption or mental disorders were available.
>
> **CONCLUSIONS:**
> Suicide occurrence varies markedly by season and needs attention where prevention is concerned.

# Thread: Restless legs and bad sleep after 10km run
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/restless-legs-and-bad-sleep-after-10km-run.11497/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Ben1983 — 2014-08-11T18:04:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/restless-legs-and-bad-sleep-after-10km-run.11497/post-137561>

I normally don't run long distances, in fact I hadn't been for a long run for about a year and a half. Instead, I've been following Dr Doug McGuffs training philosophy of slow, heavy repetitions at the gym once a week. Anyway, I had the urge the other day to go for a run, so I gave into it and ran 10km. I ran solidly but didn't feel like I was pushing myself to an extreme level. However, shortly after the run I felt quite dizzy, and still felt dizzy the next day with headaches also. It's now been 4 days since that run and every night since then I have slept terribly....I toss and turn constantly and my legs feel very restless when in bed. I took an Epsom salt bath on day one and two after the run but it hasn't seemed to have helped. I also have been taking my usual vitamins which include calcium, magnesium and trace minerals (perhaps I just need to up the dose). Anyway, does anyone have any idea as to why I'm feeling like this after the run and what I can do to go back to sleeping well again?

### Jack Kruse — 2014-08-11T20:14:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/restless-legs-and-bad-sleep-after-10km-run.11497/post-137570>

Because your mitochondria are not prepared for it. OSF #8 lays that out. Read it.

Cheers, jack. I'll have a read.

### michellewise — 2015-07-31T04:55:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/restless-legs-and-bad-sleep-after-10km-run.11497/post-173578>

For years I had restless legs at night after vigorous exercise. It would drive me crazy. I have recently realized that it is electrolyte depletion. This was such a great discovery for me! Try sodium and potassium!

### Psyche — 2015-07-31T17:41:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/restless-legs-and-bad-sleep-after-10km-run.11497/post-173604>

I have had issues with restless leg in the past, and it's always been a mystery to me. I am now more inclined to consider blue light toxicity. ESPECIALLY because of this very strange thing that happened to me in my house a while ago. Whenever I sat on the couch in my living room and turned on the TV, I experienced restless leg within MINUTES. It would freak me out. If I moved away and sat on the floor, to the side of the TV, it would go away.

# Thread: Epigenetics and stem cells
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epigenetics-and-stem-cells.11500/>
**Subforum:** Mitochondrial Rx
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2014-08-12T14:43:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epigenetics-and-stem-cells.11500/post-137658>

As many as 50% of regulatory sequences are established and opened during intermediate stages of cell development. This means that epigenetics is active at stages in which it had been thought that cell destiny was already set.

[http://medicalxpress.com/news/2014-08-dna-epigenetics-large-blood-formation.html](http://medicalxpress.com/news/2014-08-dna-epigenetics-large-blood-formation.html)

### caroline — 2014-08-13T01:59:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epigenetics-and-stem-cells.11500/post-137695>

you have been saying this forever.....

### Gracelind — 2014-08-13T06:06:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epigenetics-and-stem-cells.11500/post-137716>

This would argue that a patient contemplating stem cell therapy (using one's own stem cells) should avoid meds that function by altering DNA. An example might be Mycophenolate (CellCept).

[https://www.rheumatology.org/Practi...ellCept)\_and\_Mycophenolate\_Sodium\_(Myfortic)/](https://www.rheumatology.org/Practice/Clinical/Patients/Medications/Mycophenolate_Mofetil_%28CellCept%29_and_Mycophenolate_Sodium_%28Myfortic%29/)

_Mycophenolate targets an enzyme in the body called inosine monophosphate dehydrogenase that is important for the formation of deoxyribonucleic acid (DNA) in cells. By interfering with DNA, the medication impairs function of immune system cells that become overactive in autoimmune diseases such as lupus._

### Jack Kruse — 2014-08-14T16:12:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epigenetics-and-stem-cells.11500/post-137829>

Ideas are, almost inevitably, networks of other ideas. We take the ideas we've inherited or stumbled across, and we jigger them together into some new shape. When an idea takes a new shape or size the idea’s thermodynamics change and from this action a new idea or concept is born.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-08-14T16:13:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epigenetics-and-stem-cells.11500/post-137830>

maybe now you understand why I an careful with supplements and drugs now?

### Jack Kruse — 2014-08-14T16:16:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epigenetics-and-stem-cells.11500/post-137832>

In crawfish, blood cells are attracted to the niche, a nursery of baby neurons at the base of the brain. Each cell splits into two daughter cells, precursors to full neurons, both of which migrate out of the niche. Those head to two clumps of nerves in the brain called clusters 9 and 10.

[http://www.cell.com/developmental-cell/abstract/S1534-5807(14)00405-5](http://www.cell.com/developmental-cell/abstract/S1534-5807\(14\)00405-5)

### nonchalant — 2014-08-18T23:02:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epigenetics-and-stem-cells.11500/post-138200>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=137830)
>
> maybe now you understand why I an careful with supplements and drugs now?

Like rice with its mRNA?

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-your-optimal-work-schedule-hack-my-job.11507/post-137850>

Hello citizen-scientists!

If you had to keep your current job but could structure your schedule as you pleased, what would be your ideal setup for the day? I'm interested both in your N=1 and, only if you like puzzles, your opinion on mine.

I'm less than two weeks into Reset and CT. I'm starting a new job in the beginning of September. I have the opportunity to create a totally customized schedule. Here are the constraints:

I know exercising in the morning is NOT optimal due to cortisol, rather 2 pm (hard at most jobs). And that it's better not to work after the sun goes down (very hard in the Northeast during the winter months).

\- I must work a minimum of 25 hours. This can be broken up into whatever I want. I will have keys and can schedule with clients as late or early as I please.

\- I want to minimize car time. I \*could\* bike (16 miles a day round trip, 1 hour each way), but then I wouldn't be able to do 2 pm all-weather swimming/CT at a pond that is a 30 minute drive away. Better to bike - in which case I would choose to come in later to avoid "morning exercise" - or to drive (at non-traffic times, 20 minute drive through dense city) and have the option to leave in the afternoon to CT/swim?

\- I can't wear blue-blockers at work (clients will think I'm insane) and must use the computer 15 minutes out of every hour I'm there. I plan to bring in my own lamps with blue-blocker bulbs but can't totally avoid blue light at night exposure. Thus would like to try to limit hours worked at night. Unfortunately most clients want to meet between 3 pm and 8 pm.

\- I want to schedule regular mealtimes, which most likely includes lunch and dinner at the office since I want to be done eating by 6 or 7 pm. I gained lots of weight at my last job through lack of regular mealtimes.

Thinking out loud, one sample schedule might be:

Leave for work at 10:20 am.
See one client at 11 am.
Lunch 12-12:30
See client 12:30-1:30.
Leave for the pond, arrive at 2, swim for 30 minutes, back by 3ish.
See clients at 3:30 and 4:30.
Dinner at 5:30-6.
See clients at 6 and 7
Leave at 8.
Home 8:30
Bed before 11 pm.

That's six clients a day, which would be 30 in a week (a good number because of no-shows, probably end up with 25 per week).

Opinions? This looks obsessional to me, all this planning! But I think that's the only way to survive a stressful job, and this is the first time I've had control over this.

My one regret is not being able to change my schedule to reflect the seasons. Not yet anyway . . .

### nonchalant — 2014-08-14T19:06:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-your-optimal-work-schedule-hack-my-job.11507/post-137853>

I think the most important thing is to work during daylight hours only. So your evening work might be a problem, in the winter.

### Inger — 2014-08-14T19:18:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-your-optimal-work-schedule-hack-my-job.11507/post-137854>

Well, Sally is right... to be optimal a bit late in winter. But not too bad tho... if you think about many other works like mine used to be as a waitress....

Here are a few ideas to optimize it.

Eat a big big breakfast, so it holds you until 3 or 4 PM, then eat dinner, that way you would save lunch time
Go to the pond on the way to work in the morning after that BAB? That way you would save time too and be able to quit earlier?
You will get so much power from the Epi paleo ketogenic diet that you will do just great without many breaks at all, I promise you! If you drive between clients you could also just eat in the car by driving... works for me - just cut your meat or fish up in pieces beforehand

So then you might be home by 6 or 7 PM......

### Winter Mermaid — 2014-08-14T19:24:38+0300

Inger and Nonchalant, both valid points!

Sitting in traffic in an EMF-crazy city really increases my stress. I want to avoid rush hours, which are 7-10 am and 3-630 pm in my city (anyone who has lived in Boston can confirm how ridiculous this is. It can literally take you an hour to go three miles). So that means a day shifted earlier or later. Since this diet has me going to bed and waking up early perhaps shifting early is better. But then I can't swim at the proper time in the afternoon . . . how bad IS it to exercise with morning cortisol? I rather like early morning exercise but will avoid it if its very important.

I fear I might be being ridiculous. It is hard to tell at the beginning of a new endeavour, trying to follow all the rules! There are a lot of them.

### Inger — 2014-08-14T19:31:43+0300

Ok, I understand the traffic issue.. I think i would rather go earlier.... I would not worry too much about exercise... you will get a great body without much of it at all on this diet. Maybe you can do exercise on the weekends? Swim all you want then at the proper time

As for blue light avoidance, my Holy Grail is blue-blocking contact lenses. I'm working on it right now. A surprisingly difficult materials science problem. Some tints exist but no one has tested them to see if they completely block blue. A work in progress that I hope to be able to share with others if I can put out a product I am satisfied with personally.

P.S. to Caroline: I put on my crazy blue blocker glasses at my job (today's my last day, new one starts soon), and the woman who comes in to clean got really curious about them and asked me about them. She's five months pregnant and having trouble sleeping so I explained all about them and she's going to buy some! That literally happened right after I read your post . I was nosy and asked about Omega 3's, she said she takes DHA but said her OBGYN said she shouldn't eat any fish because the baby will get too much mercury and pollutants. Very interesting what happens when you share this stuff!

You need to share stuff.

1)The key point in life is not to prioritize what’s on your schedule, but to schedule your priorities.

2) The main thing is to keep the main thing the main thing.

3) Live out of your imagination, not your history.

4) Trust is the glue of life. It’s the most essential ingredient in effective communication. It’s the foundational principle that holds all relationships.

5) Most of us spend too much time on what is urgent and not enough time on what is important.

6) I am not a product of my circumstances. I am a product of my decisions.

7) You have to decide what your highest priorities are and have the courage—pleasantly, smilingly, nonapologetically, to say “no” to other things. And the way you do that is by having a bigger “yes” burning inside. The enemy of the “best” is often the “good.”

8) I teach people how to treat me by what I will allow.

9) Love is a verb. Love – the feeling – is the fruit of love the verb or our loving actions. So love her.

### Winter Mermaid — 2014-08-15T15:43:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-your-optimal-work-schedule-hack-my-job.11507/post-137929>

I have a small private practice and will start working for an agency soon. At my previous agency I seriously thought about attending medical school because many patients referred for therapy actually had medical problems. One lady referred for "psychosomatic" symptoms actually had a rare genetic disorder inherited from her mother, but her mother never shared her medical history so it was never tested for. We worked through her difficult relationship with her mother in therapy and she decided she wanted to get back in touch- they had not talked for years. When she shared her "mysterious" symptoms the mother said "Oh! That's like what I have!" She was able to get tested and get treatment. I had previously urged her to get full panels from her doc and they turned up zip.

That encapsulates pretty well the link between mind, emotions, health and knowledge. I always felt like I had to rule out every medical thing before I proceeded with treatment which was not shared by all of my colleagues. They would tell me I'm not a doctor, stop speculating! Medical school still tempts me sometimes even though I would be an old student.

That is pretty much what happened to me.......once I had an ASI done and had talked to Jack - it was pretty easy to see why I had issues - my PG/E2 ratio was 3 .... it should have been above 300.
so - it really was Jack who nailed it ..... it should have been the first order of business for a therapist - in my view ...to understand - or to get some perspective on the medical stuff - instead of just wanting to give me anti depressants.

They kept insisting I was depressed because of what I had been thru and needed a script. At least i recognised that I wasn't depressed .......I appeared to be very healthy - a regular blood work tells you pretty much zero.

### Penny — 2014-08-17T23:31:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/healing-is-voltage-healing-eye-diseases-jerry-tennant-md.11508/post-138102>

I took CoQ10 before a red eye 2 weeks ago - like I thought you said to do - and drank a gallon of water on the plane - they finally just handed me the liter bottle... but I couldn't sleep on the red eye presumably because of the Coq10? It has had that effect on me before for some reason... and it sure isn't because I have too many electrons - God knows...

My husband's uncle is at the stage of Parkinson's where the l-dopa isn't working as long - my biochemist brother-in-law said the receptors - I guess dopamine receptors - are gone and there is nothing I could do to change it... but then I came across this little gem:

**Is progesterone a pre-hormone in the CNS?**
[Ramirez VD](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed?term=Ramirez%20VD%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=3320543)1, [Dluzen D](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed?term=Dluzen%20D%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=3320543).
**[Author information](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/3320543#)**

**Abstract**
In this paper, experimental evidences have been presented indicating that progesterone per se appears to be a powerful modulatory steroid of presynaptic striatal dopaminergic terminals of the central nervous system of the rat. This effect of the progesterone signal is concentration as well as infusion mode dependent. Low pulsatile doses of the steroid positively modulate the mechanism by which dopamine terminals respond to amphetamine stimulation and increase tissue dopamine concentration. Whereas, continuous and/or high doses of this steroid negatively modulate the response of the dopamine terminals to amphetamine stimulation and decreases tissue dopamine concentration. This effects occurs through a membrane mediated mechanism either upon the dopamine neuron directly and/or upon an interneuron. Pregnanolone a 5- beta-3 beta-metabolite of progesterone known to activate the hypothalamic LHRH neural apparatus at the level of the hypothalamus of ovariectomized estrogen primed rats in both in vitro as well as in vivo preparations was completely ineffective at the level of the corpus striatum of similar animal preparations. Therefore, it is reasonable to assume that site specific mechanisms exist within the central nervous system which may control differentially the final action of progesterone. In the hypothalamus, pregnanolone appears to be the final signal for its action on the LHRH neural apparatus, whereas in the corpus striatum, the steroid per se, and dependent on the modality and/or the strength of the signal can either directly or indirectly up-regulate (stimulatory component) or down-regulate (inhibitory component) the activity of striatal dopaminergic terminals.

And I don't know if I've shared this priceless find yet so here it is:

**Brain proteome response following whole body exposure of mice to mobile phone or wireless DECT base radiation.**
[Fragopoulou AF](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed?term=Fragopoulou%20AF%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=22263702)1, [Samara A](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed?term=Samara%20A%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=22263702), [Antonelou MH](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed?term=Antonelou%20MH%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=22263702), [Xanthopoulou A](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed?term=Xanthopoulou%20A%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=22263702), [Papadopoulou A](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed?term=Papadopoulou%20A%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=22263702), [Vougas K](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed?term=Vougas%20K%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=22263702), [Koutsogiannopoulou E](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed?term=Koutsogiannopoulou%20E%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=22263702), [Anastasiadou E](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed?term=Anastasiadou%20E%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=22263702), [Stravopodis DJ](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed?term=Stravopodis%20DJ%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=22263702), [Tsangaris GT](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed?term=Tsangaris%20GT%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=22263702), [Margaritis LH](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed?term=Margaritis%20LH%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=22263702).
**[Author information](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/22263702#)**

**Abstract**
The objective of this study was to investigate the effects of two sources of electromagnetic fields (EMFs) on the proteome of cerebellum, hippocampus, and frontal lobe in Balb/c mice following long-term whole body irradiation. Three equally divided groups of animals (6 animals/group) were used; the first group was exposed to a typical mobile phone, at a SAR level range of 0.17-0.37 W/kg for 3 h daily for 8 months, the second group was exposed to a wireless DECT base (Digital Enhanced Cordless Telecommunications/Telephone) at a SAR level range of 0.012-0.028 W/kg for 8 h/day also for 8 months and the third group comprised the sham-exposed animals. Comparative proteomics analysis revealed that long-term irradiation from both EMF sources altered significantly (p < 0.05) the expression of 143 proteins in total (as low as 0.003 fold downregulation up to 114 fold overexpression). Several neural function related proteins (i.e., Glial Fibrillary Acidic Protein (GFAP), Alpha-synuclein, Glia Maturation Factor beta (GMF), and apolipoprotein E (apoE)), heat shock proteins, and cytoskeletal proteins (i.e., Neurofilaments and tropomodulin) are included in this list as well as proteins of the brain metabolism (i.e., Aspartate aminotransferase, Glutamate dehydrogenase) to nearly all brain regions studied. Western blot analysis on selected proteins confirmed the proteomics data. The observed protein expression changes may be related to brain plasticity alterations, indicative of oxidative stress in the nervous system or involved in apoptosis and might potentially explain human health hazards reported so far, such as headaches, sleep disturbance, fatigue, memory deficits, and brain tumor long-term induction under similar exposure conditions.

[http://electromagnetichealth.org/electromagnetic-health-blog/mice-proteome/](http://electromagnetichealth.org/electromagnetic-health-blog/mice-proteome/)

# Thread: Anneh's Journey to Optimal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/annehs-journey-to-optimal.11512/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Anneh — 2014-08-15T20:45:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/annehs-journey-to-optimal.11512/post-137957>

Hello, my name is Anneh (pronounced Anne). I am a 42 year old woman. I live in British Columbia, Canada. I am a new member and very happy to be here.

I have had chronic daily headache/migraine for the last 9 and a half years. The pain is constant — I have not had one pain free moment for 9.5 years. The pain is also quite high on a daily basis.

I have run the gamut of both conventional and alternative medicine - the drugs, techniques, supplement, and interventions are too long to list here - but nothing has helped, reduced, or eliminated the pain.

The only thing that helps the pain is ice packs wrapped around my head. I cried when I read the blog entry that explained why cold works on head pain. I feel that for the first time since I got sick, finally there is a person, Dr. Kruse, who actually understands what is “wrong” with me.

The migraines began in university in my early 20s, both episodic, and for short (month long) daily/chronic periods. These migraines were mainly episodic until about 12 years ago (age 30), when they began to become chronic, with some intermittent relief, until finally, 9.5 years ago, the migraine settled in and hasn’t left since.

Other symptoms that have accompanied the migraines include “brain fog”, inability to concentrate, increased pain from concentration/thinking/reading. My memory - both short and long term - is absolutely shot. I “lose” words when speaking, and need to stop and “find” them. When there is too much stimulation (noise, lights, voices, people, objects), my head spins and I can’t keep track of everything that’s happening. I am hyper-sensitive to, and triggered by, lights (esp. fluorescent, and sadly also the sun), smells, noise, weather changes, stress, some foods, heat, and exertion (I simply cannot do any exercise other than short walks, and can’t do even the slightest cardio, without exacerbating the headache/migraine).

In addition the the headaches/migraines, I have constant exhaustion. I am not sure if this is “Chronic Fatigue” per se, or simply a part of the migraine condition (and after reading this blog I’m not sure that it really matters in terms of healing). It doesn’t matter how much I sleep, I wake up exhausted. And it seems I only have a certain amount of energy per day, which is “spent” quite quickly, leaving me even more exhausted, and in more pain, and “out of it” by late afternoon every day. The more I try to do in a day the more exhausted I am. Even very simple things take it out of me. If I push myself and do too much, I can end up on the couch for several days to recover.

I also have chronic long-term depression and anxiety. My first depressive episode occurred at age 25, followed about 2 years later by severe anxiety. Several times in the last 15 years I have been suicidal - not because of my life, but simply because of the depression/anxiety. Luckily my mood is being well managed these days by an antidepressant (Cipralex). (I’m not a fan of pharmaceuticals, but this drug has saved my life given how bad the depression/anxiety was.)

I have been diagnosed with PCOS. I had absent/irregular periods immediately upon beginning menstruation, and was first diagnosed at age 17. I took the birth control pill for about 10 years, until about age 29. I currently take Prometrium, which seems to help, (but managing the dosage is like throwing a dart in the dark).

At age 39, I began to experience hot flashes and extreme night sweats. The hot flashes are less now, but the night sweats are still with me. I literally drench my pyjamas and sheets, and need to get up in the middle of the night and change it all, sometimes needing 3 or 4 changes a night.

About the same time the hot flashes and night sweats started 3 years ago, I also developed insomnia. I would wake up at about 4 a.m. and not be able to get back to sleep for about 3 hours. I tried to deal with it without pharmaceuticals for about a year, but it finally drove me crazy. I have been taking 10 m.g. of Amitryptaline before bed since then, and it keeps me asleep through this time period. If I don’t take it, I wake up like clockwork.

I also have chronic adult acne. I treated it in my mid/late 20s with Accutane. (I did not understand at that time the costs to my health of making that choice). I have also treated it with laser therapy. It persists to this day.

Two years ago I was diagnosed with cervical dysplasia and underwent a LEEP procedure (which follow-ups show was successful).

I have low thyroid, both T4 and T3, right at the bottom and just below “normal”, but my doctor does not suggest treatment. I did have another doctor who treated me with supplementation for a short time, which did raise my T4, but after stopping this medication (as I was not tolerating it due to nausea) my numbers have been steadily dropping.

I am freezing cold all the time, especially my feet, but also my core, and in the winter I just can’t get warm. I am not exaggerating — I am cold beyond cold (but don’t have Reynauds specific symptoms).

Every time I increase my water intake to increase hydration, it seems that all I do is pee it out. Really, the amount I have to urinate makes it very hard to hydrate.

I would not have said I have an Autoimmune disorder (unless migraines are “autoimmune”, which none of my conventional doctors classify them as). However, in the last two weeks I have developed pain in my hand and fingers. Today I will go for an X-ray of my hand and a blood test for rheumatoid factor. From what I can glean, my hand pain is presenting very much like RA.

I have learned that I am homozygous for the MTHFR C677T mutation.

I have cravings for sugar that dominate my thinking and my life. It is clear to me that I have been a “sugar addict”, truly, all of my life. I have done regular paleo, or Atkins, sometimes successfully for months or even 1-2 years at a time. But always I come back to the sugar. I feel powerless over it. I gained 55 pounds eating this way, and over the last two years slowly lost that weight by eating correctly. But now the sugar demon is back and I am struggling. I have started to eat epi-paleo about 3 weeks ago, and am following the prescription completely. However, I am still overwhelmed by, and give into, sugar binges (which I know is not part of the prescription). This is devastating to me.

Because of the migraines and associated neck pain/problems, I have undergone several CTs, MRIs, and X-rays.

I have had multiple head injuries (4) from a variety of causes throughout my life.

When I was a child, up until the age of about 10, I had “fits”. I would “drop” to the ground without much warning. When this happened, it felt like blacking out to me. I never got a diagnosis of epilepsy, but from the research I’ve done it sounds like it could have been. These “fits” often also presented as a kind of dissociation, in which I would not drop, but would be “freaking out”, awake and speaking, but I would not remember afterwards what I had said or done or how I ended up where I was. Often the episode would end with me vomiting, and then “coming to”. The last time this happened I was 18 years old.

Also as a young child I had “temper tantrums”. I was a behavioural problem in the classroom, although highly intelligent and successful academically. I’m very glad there were no drugs to put kids on in those days, or I would have been medicated for sure. I slowly grew out of this behaviour.

As a child I had constant nightmares. These decreased somewhat in frequency as I became an adult, but have stayed with me. It is part of why sleep is troubling for me, because the nightmares are very distressing, and it seems to me that they are possibly tiring as well.

As a teenager I had several episodes of extreme nose bleeds with no obvious cause.

Also as a young teen I often felt faint, especially if I was upside down. I passed out in the middle of a gymnastics vault, in the air, and was prohibited from continuing with gymnastics. My migraines are immediately exacerbated if a bend over and have my head below my waist.

I hope that this is sufficient to give context into my state of health. I will post what labs I have in my next entry.

I am truly grateful to be here with all of you, finally empowered to save my own life!

Blessings,
Anneh

BIA's re quite variable and I find that they are only useful for trend improvements or declines with therapy.

# Thread: Hello again! Can I please get some help with my labs?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-again-can-i-please-get-some-help-with-my-labs.11521/>

### Tms — 2014-08-18T16:49:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-again-can-i-please-get-some-help-with-my-labs.11521/post-138165>

I say hello again, because I was on this thread before but dropped off. I would say I am still new.
I don't have much money for good labs and have a super high deductible. Been working with a doctor on my plan for about 8 months. He dx with hypothyroid and low testosterone. He put me on bio identical testosterone but it did nothing so I stopped it. I am still on 130 grains of nature thyroid but don't see much change there. I've been looking over my labs and the numbers seem strange to me. Maybe there are fine but I just want to be sure I'm getting the right treatment. I tried iodine supplementation but has a terrible detox reaction so stopped it. I would try again though with the right supplementation support... Which I did not do thoroughly enough. I am supplementing with selenium now. Hoping to gather some money to do Genova test so I can get my rt3 number.

Symptoms:
Up early.. Cortisol issues
Mood dysregulation
Anxiety
Digestive/ leaky gut stuff
Chronic fatigue
No sex drive
Hard to lose weight although at an average weight (5'6, 145)

Labs 12/11/13:
Ferritin 13
Testosterone 37
DHEA 316.4
Free t3 2.52
Free t4 0.62
Tsh 1.77

Labs: 3/5/14:
No DHEA numbers
No ferritin numbers
Testosterone 35
T3 2.44
T4 0.72
Tsh 0.72

Labs 6/8/14
DHEA 205.1
Testosterone 23
T3 3.04
T4 1.14
TSH 0.19

I really appreciate any help!
Thanks
TMS

Hi Caroline,
I live in LA. EMF is a huge issue. I wear blue blockers and limit artificial light after dark. I did LR months ago, and now follow more of a regular paleo diet. ### caroline — 2014-08-20T00:48:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-again-can-i-please-get-some-help-with-my-labs.11521/post-138330>

Don't forget about frozen seafood .... it can often be fresher than stuff sitting in a seafood counter - because it is flash frozen on ships when caught.

Buy whole fish and then use the frames and heads etc for stock....

Develope a relationship with a fish monger/market - there are plenty of less expensive seafood ..... like mussels etc.
Check out Chinese markets

I often buy salmon offcuts - from when they are trimming the salmon to make them look beautiful and perfect for the fish counter!

You must have a Costco etc????

smart meter ....on your house.....

the leptin reset is about timing ..... you need to reset the rolex in your brain .... maybe re read that blog....

What about water? are you drinking good spring water?

# Thread: Spacey after bone broth
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spacey-after-bone-broth.11524/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Ben1983 — 2014-08-18T19:24:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spacey-after-bone-broth.11524/post-138177>

I feel mildly dizzy/spacey after I drink my bone broth. Any idea why this might be? I cook it on low for 24-30 hours.

### Clayton — 2014-08-18T19:37:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spacey-after-bone-broth.11524/post-138179>

Could be a reaction to histamine, bone broth has quite a bit. This reaction should decrease as your gut flora shifts. Have you seen the resistant starch webinar? Are you swallowing food with minimal chewing? Eating a source of prebiotics (ie raw almonds, pistachios, raw dark chocolate)?

### Clayton — 2014-08-18T19:38:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spacey-after-bone-broth.11524/post-138180>

You could also try decreasing the cooking time

### Jack Kruse — 2014-08-18T20:24:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spacey-after-bone-broth.11524/post-138187>

histamine has a huge effect on the electrostatic field that involves protein functions.

### Gracelind — 2014-08-18T23:08:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spacey-after-bone-broth.11524/post-138201>

Bone broth provokes coughing fits for me--same reaction as to other sensitive-for-me foods such as celery (salt), basil, pepper. Time for brewing doesn't seem to make a difference.

### Ben1983 — 2014-08-19T06:27:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spacey-after-bone-broth.11524/post-138271>

If it is a histamine issue, should I keep pushing through with the broth or hold off in it for a while? Clayton, I have bulletproof dark choc once or twice a week (two teaspoons each time), I eat a few almonds each day (maybe 10), I am possibly not chewing food enough, I take "prescript assist as my probiotic (I'll have to check if it includes prebiotic a too).

### Jack Kruse — 2014-08-19T07:24:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spacey-after-bone-broth.11524/post-138276>

histamine issues always link back to an seriously altered field.

### caroline — 2014-08-19T07:31:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spacey-after-bone-broth.11524/post-138278>

> [Ben1983 said:](/goto/post?id=138271)
>
> If it is a histamine issue, should I keep pushing through with the broth or hold off in it for a while? Clayton, I have bulletproof dark choc once or twice a week (two teaspoons each time), I eat a few almonds each day (maybe 10), I am possibly not chewing food enough, I take "prescript assist as my probiotic (I'll have to check if it includes prebiotic a too).

minimal chewing is better....

### Ben1983 — 2014-08-19T11:52:17+0300

# Thread: Curtis- from Vancouver Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/curtis-from-vancouver-journal.11527/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Curtis R — 2014-08-18T21:05:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/curtis-from-vancouver-journal.11527/post-138194>

Hi Everyone!

Just new to the forum, have been toying with HF/LC Paleo for a about a year now after learning that I am intolerant to wheat, eggs, and non-fermented dairy; leading to gut issues and wieght gain...I have historically been interested in Diet and Nutrition but never really found anything that did much to move the needle for me in terms of noticeable difference. I have toyed with calorie restriction, but honestly never made much sence to me..my wife has historically a low calorie/low fat advocate, but it didn't make a lot of sense to me when I travelled to Asia and saw the fat levels that the Chinese ate and still remain slim. I have tried Atkins but had most success with the Zone, but didn't find it made me reach "homoeostasis" in the end. I also had a few years of Veganism/Vegatarianism in my college days that left me skinny fat and hypoglycemic most of the time....Now I'm just fat. Over the last year did about 3 months ketogenic..lost 10 lbs and then plateaued...tried Carb nite solution...no further dice. I like the LC diet...don't like fruit anyways, don't miss bread, do enjoy booze, wish eggs didn't bother me as I do miss that....I grew up in a hunting family so I enjoy pretty much any kind of meat and LOVE fat.
My recent read list over the last year.. in order: Wheat belly, Grain Brain, Good calories/Bad Calories, Cholesterol clarity, Epi-paleo Rx, Keto-Clarity. Also been steeping myself with youtube videos from Robert Lustig/Able James/Sisson/Jimmy Moore/Taubes/Dave Asprey/JJ Virgin/David Wolfe/ John Keifer
I must say that approaching nutrition from the perspective of how did we ancestrally grow our big brain is very appealing, and I have some background in Circadian Rhythms having studied with Ralph Mistleberger (circadian rhythms researcher at SFU) in my college days.
Currently Obese by any standard, low energy, very low libido, suffer from IBS like symptoms, have a Diverticuli, periodic PVC, sleep well enough(but a night owl), inflammed joints (particularly with grains esp wheat)

My Data:
Age: 46
Hieght 5'11"
Weight 235 lbs (big belly)
BP 135/80
Fasting blood sugar: 105
cortisol morning/noon/night 5.7/7.5/2.9 NG/ML respectivly
estradiol 45 pg/ml
Testosterone 119 ng/dl
Ratio: T/shbg 0.3
DHEAS 215 ug/dl
SHBG <15 nmol/L
PSA <0.5 ng/dl
Free T4 2.2 ng/dl
Free T3 3.9 pg/ml
TSH 1.3 uU/ml
TPO 44 IU/ml

Currently taking:

-   zinc citrate
-   D3
-   k2
-   B6
-   Chromium (ionic)
-   Magnesium
-   Fish Oil (Barleans)
-   B12 (methylcobalamin)
-   Curcumin
-   Resveratrol
-   Nascent Iodine (on skin and orally)
-   MCT (periodically as it raises my BP through the roof)
-   Butter (cultured grassfed)

I'm going to take my before Pic and start Leptin reset Sept 1..with CT...been working up my tolerance in mountain glacial streams this summer...I'm debating if I should consider bio identical Testosterone cream until I get my body turned around (Jack?) or just wait for my body to right itself naturally.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-08-18T23:11:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/curtis-from-vancouver-journal.11527/post-138202>

welcome youre in the right place. I am not a fan of creams. IF youre going to use T use the IM injections with your doc.

### Curtis R — 2014-08-18T23:37:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/curtis-from-vancouver-journal.11527/post-138214>

lol...guess that's out then...seeing as I'm phobic of needles!

### nonchalant — 2014-08-19T01:27:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/curtis-from-vancouver-journal.11527/post-138238>

A relative of mine that cannot tolerate egg yolks has found that he can eat guinea eggs and duck eggs with no problems.
Hope you drop that night owl habit fast!

### caroline — 2014-08-19T01:38:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/curtis-from-vancouver-journal.11527/post-138242>

..We have ordered some blue blocking glasses and will be working on normalizing our circadian light and bedtime routines etc...we live in vancouver so winters are pretty wet and rainy...my wife and I run a couple group homes for uber high risk teens so work can vacilate between extremely boring and then very very stressful....been working through all the podcasts and currently trying to wrap my bean around the CT series and the Brain gut series.......I'm a bit annoyed that the TEDx talk has been taken down though...has anyone been able to source this video?

So it's hard to tell if thier is any difference ..but I will trust the test and avoid them for the now and experiment with them later when I'm firing on all 8 cylinders so to speak.

**8/July/2014 Urea 6.8 mmol/l (2.5-6.1)

''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''

I am freezing cold all the time, especially my feet, but also my core, and in the winter I just can’t get warm. I am not exaggerating — I am cold beyond cold (but don’t have Reynauds specific symptoms).

Ask doctor to prescribe you natural thyroid hormone (NTH) from pigs.
You may also buy it from e-bay. Thyroid-S (they are one grain pills)
Try 1-3 grains. Start with one pill, observe your self for a week, then eventually increase to 2 pills, and so on. Do not use other thyroid hormones (T4 or T3) while taking NTH.

Get body thermometer that is liquid based. Ideal would be mercury.
Measure under arm, hold for 10 minutes.
Ideal temperature is 36.6C=97.88F

Measuring thyroid get:
Total T4
Free T4
TotalT3
FreeT3
ReverseT3
TSH

All above values are important to know.
\---
take
2/day-->Super Selenium Complex, 200 mcg 100 capsules (lef.org)
after you took selenium for a month, take

1 dropperfull/day=48mg/day

[http://www.amazon.com/Lugols-Iodine-Solution-oz-Twin/dp/B001RRH814/ref=sr\_1\_1?s=hpc&ie=UTF8&qid=1408803496&sr=1-1&keywords=Lugol's Iodine](http://www.amazon.com/Lugols-Iodine-Solution-oz-Twin/dp/B001RRH814/ref=sr_1_1?s=hpc&ie=UTF8&qid=1408803496&sr=1-1&keywords=Lugol%27s+Iodine)

Lugol's Iodine Solution(2 oz.) Twin Pack(2 bot.)

If you would take different product, remember that it is about (iodine + potassium iodide), not only about iodine.
If you would have any adverse reactions stop immediately.
You would have to go thru detoxification process first. (rare).

Iodine is halide, so are bromine, chlorine, fluoride
Minimize exposure to harmful halides.
watch this video:
Iodine: The Most Misunderstood Nutrient Presented by David Brownstein MD

\--------------------------------------------------
Every time I increase my water intake to increase hydration, it seems that all I do is pee it out. Really, the amount I have to urinate makes it very hard to hydrate.

You may be low on vasopressin, ADH antidiuretic hormone.
Ask your doc to prescribe to you nasal spray or pills for trial.
\-------------------------------------------------------------
......

# Thread: Preterm delivery in progress
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/preterm-delivery-in-progress.11537/>

### digital — 2014-08-19T22:49:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/preterm-delivery-in-progress.11537/post-138316>

Hello JK Community-

My wife is currently in preterm labor (33 weeks). This is our second child. The first was full term and had a normal delivery.

We both and 2yr old son, have had followed an epi-paleo template pretty extremely all year round (and for us parents last three years). However we live in NYC so there is that environmental factor.

With that being said- while we are in the hospital... are there tips for the duration and after coming home for both mom and baby?

They are injecting her with steroids (for baby lung development) and antibiotics via IV as a precautionary measure for the preterm baby (bit upset about this as it will effect the flora during delivery).

Thank you for reading and for any input.

\--- Worried Father

### digital — 2014-08-19T22:53:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/preterm-delivery-in-progress.11537/post-138317>

Forgot some additional tid-bits:

\- she is dilated 6cm
\- they are not for inducing delivery
\- they have her on IV
\- has been taking a lot of Mg Malate since beginning of pregnancy
\- drinking about 1 to 1.5 gallons of RO/distilled water (normal routine for the past few years)

### cantweight — 2014-08-19T22:58:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/preterm-delivery-in-progress.11537/post-138318>

Go hold her hand, rub her back and tell her how beautiful she is and how much you love her.

Babies are amazingly resilient, with your wifes good diet her breastmilk will be the most important thing, that and lots of human skin to skin contact.....give that baby love and electrons

Congratulations

### Shijin13 — 2014-08-20T14:44:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/preterm-delivery-in-progress.11537/post-138384>

> [digital said:](/goto/post?id=138352)
>
> We delivered a baby boy at 7:51pm ET.. Appears to be healthy, alert, crying and breathing on his own... Now in the NICU.. Mom will be getting ready to pump temporarily until baby is ready to be breastfeed directly.

@[digital](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/2083/) Congrats

Tips for your wife. get up and walk around as much as possible. Go to nicu and use kangroo care with your boy (both of you but especially mom) mom should pump every 2 hours for 20 mins using a hospital grade pump.+ 5min of hand expression after last drop of milk Tell mom not to be discouraged if it doesn't look like she's got lots of milk. its really hard to express colostrum. try to get baby to latch - otherwise feed with small syringe. Always offer Breast as often as you can, then follow up with the syringe, then pump.
Make sure you're taking good care of mom... feed her well. She will need to pump at least 8-10x/day with the best pumping time btwn 2-4am (yeah total suckage for sleep) until baby comes home and she can co-sleep and nurse laying down.

I have a mom friend who nursed successfully 3 premies w/o issue.

### digital — 2014-08-20T16:11:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/preterm-delivery-in-progress.11537/post-138387>

Thank you all so much and for the info!

If there are any other advise, especially for the little one let me know- I have the 'watch feature' set for this thread.

One of my other concerns are that as a premie he did not complete DHA transfer from mommy as that takes place in the last weeks of pregnancy. I hope via breastfeeding and eventually introducing solids (e.g., salmon roe, oysters...) will get him caught up and develop a big optimal brain

### Chrysanthemama — 2014-08-23T05:45:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/preterm-delivery-in-progress.11537/post-138655>

Wow! That's great! Congrats, and I fully second everything Gretchen said. And Jack. Nurse nurse nurse, pump pump pump. Baby next to skin as much as possible and more. Avoid as much artificial light as possible. Remember, due dates are just scientific guesses, so if your little one is nursing strong and doing well, it's possible the "due date"was off by a bit. Blessings to you all! Sounds like you are off to a strong start!
