### spalmi — 2014-09-19T16:10:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/snp-genes.11733/post-141402>

"SNP's are altered by electrons.", Even postnatal? Is this covered anywhere in the blogs or written about in scientific literature?

The SNP itself may change the structure, which may change the function of the protein. In the case of a receptor, this would mean that you do not have working receptors, hence cells can not communicate correctly. In order for an SNP to survive in the population this error can not be deadly in youth, however they play a part in determining who you are, your strength, weaknesses and can have health effects. But my curiosity lies in the idea that some SNP's create a situation in the body that the body can not compensate for, hence creating a weakness compared to the general population. The primary fix would be to modify that SNP to code optimally. I don't think this is possible (without high tech science), but I would be fascinated to hear ideas. In regards to working with the SNP's you have, we know very little about the brain, so understanding what an exact SNP does, how that plays out with your other SNP's, epigenetics, environment, lifestye ect.. is not always possible.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-09-21T22:08:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/snp-genes.11733/post-141546>

Pre and post natal. We know a lot more than you think. No one seem to understand how transgenerational epigenetic switches are handled because they don't understand how water iodine DHA and light work in the brain or germ line. For example, all women who have autistic kids are extremely low in iodine.

Light polarizes water and the electrons in water alter the hydrogen bonding network around SNP's to alter their ability to lead to health or illness. How this effect is generalized to your epigenetic switches and how mitochondria handle electrons is among the most complex dances in biology. It is not easy to understand but it begins with the marine sources of electrons, to capture sunlight's electrons and photons by using DHA amazing abilities. Without DHA the sun is just a star. With it.........life eats our sun's frequencies of light in different ways within mitochondria and around nucleic acids to organize some amazing abilities.

Without iodine from seafood, DHA won’t work optimally in human tissue to change light into an electrical signal properly. In the brain, iodine is found in the choroid plexus, the area on the ventricles of the brain where cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) is produced. For example, children with autism have been found to have a poor ability to concentrate iodine in their CSF. The interaction of iodine and DHA to move electrons well is fundamentally how circadian signaling begins in the suprachiasmatic nucleus of the human brain. This is where circadian signaling begins. It is a critical to understand how the brain deciphers environmental signals.

The general picture that emerges within the brain that T4 and T3 hormones enter the brain through
specific CNS transporters as the brain forms. T4 is converted to the active hormone, T3, in glial cells, astrocytes, and tanycytes, although the main target cells of these hormones are neurons and maturing oligodendrocytes within the suprachiasmatic nucleus. This is ultimately how the direction of time is initially set within this newly forming brain. T3, acting through the nuclear receptors, controls the expression of genes involved in myelination, cell differentiation, migration, and signaling. I went over this in energy and epigenetics 1. Few people understand why iodine and DHA are critical in human SCN and myelination patterns. In addition to transducing the proper T3 signal, the nuclear receptors also have activity in the un-liganded state. When circadian signaling is off in the maternal germ line before fertilization, this favors the un-liganded state and this strongly favors the development of hypothyroid phenotype centrally within the brain. This is where leptin resistance begins. This is why early onset hypothyroidism in the maternal tree is always a key clue to the astute clinician that a circadian mismatch is the key feature in many diseases. MS, Hashimoto’s, and melasma being common one’s in modern women. This un-liganded action occurs via aporreceptors that repress normal thyroid transcription. This is how epigenetic switches are set in the germ line. This effect is how transgenerational hypothyroid phenotypes occur in subsequent generations of children. These children do not even need to face the same environment their mothers did to get the same disease. They will manifest the disease much earlier in life if they face a lack of iodine or DHA in their first 6 years of life. They begin with the breakdown of the quantum chemistry between iodine, DHA, and water in the unfertilized egg. These effects then become generalized within the developing embryo after fertilization to effect brain function and mitochondrial development in all tissues. This is how the redox shift occurs transgenerationally in humans. The interaction between iodine DHA and water is critical in setting the proper signaling in how mitochondria can handle electrons at a very fundamental level. This is why understanding SCALE matters. When you don't see the scale you act like it never exists because you can not set up experiments to measure the effect.

mimic = imprint = memory = transgenerational inheritence = water's hydrogen bonding network. Here we return to one of lady evolution's legs of her 3 legged stool. WATER. I spoke about this in Energy and Epigenetics 4, you might want to review it.

You can scoop up a bag full of facts and try to make sense of it, but that would be an exercise in futility.

Biochemistry is knowledge, but true wisdom requires that knowledge to be connected in a fractal. A fractal is self similar design which is a another way of saying it is a rhyme………..

### Josh — 2014-09-21T00:34:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ms-blog-is-live-disease-of-light.11734/post-141473>

> **Abstract**
> Of the several aquaporin (AQP) water channels expressed in the central nervous system, AQP4 is an attractive target for drug discovery. AQP4 is expressed in astroglia, most strongly at the blood-brain and brain-cerebrospinal fluid barriers. Phenotype analysis of AQP4 knockout mice indicates the involvement of AQP4 in three distinct processes: brain water balance, astroglial cell migration and neural signal transduction. By slowing water uptake into the brain, AQP4 knockout mice manifest reduced brain swelling and improved outcome in models of cytotoxic cerebral oedema such as water intoxication, focal ischaemia and meningitis. However, by slowing the clearance of excess water from brain, AQP4 knockout mice do worse in models of vasogenic oedema such as brain tumour, abscess and hydrocephalus. AQP4 deficient astroglial cells show greatly impaired migration in response to chemotactic stimuli, reducing glial scar formation, by a mechanism that we propose involves AQP4-facilitated water flux in lamellipodia of migrating cells. AQP4 knockout mice also manifest increased seizure threshold and duration, by a mechanism that may involve slowed K(+) uptake from the extracellular space (ECS) following neuroexcitation, as well as ECS expansion. Notwithstanding challenges in drug delivery to the central nervous system and their multiplicity of actions, AQP4 inhibitors have potential utility in reducing cytotoxic brain swelling, increasing seizure threshold and reducing glial scar formation; enhancers of AQP4 expression have potential utility in reducing vasogenic brain swelling. AQP4 modulators may thus offer new therapeutic options for stroke, tumour, infection, hydrocephalus, epilepsy and traumatic brain and spinal cord injury.

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/18655912](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/18655912)

Schematic depicting three distinct roles of AQP4 (green circles) in brain function: (A) brain water balance, (B) astroglial cell migration and (C) neuronal excitation. (A) Green. Routes of oedema formation in the two types of brain oedema (cytotoxic — through AQP4, vasogenic — through interendothelial spaces). Orange. Oedema fluid is eliminated by AQP4 through the glial limitans into subarachnoid CSF, through ependyma and sub-ependymal astroglia into ventricular CSF, and through astroglial pericapillary foot processes into blood. (B) AQP4 polarizes to the leading edge of migrating astroglia and accelerates cell migration. AQP4 facilitates water entry into lamellipodial protrusions in response to intracellular hyperosmolality produced by actin depolymerization and ion influx. (C) AQP4 deletion reduces neuroexcitation. Active neurons (neuron a) release K+ into the extracellular space (ECS). Increased extracellular \[K+\] depolarizes quiescent neurons (neuron b). AQP4 deletion increases ECS volume and reduces astroglial cell K+ reuptake. This buffers the increase in extracellular \[K+\] by active neuron a, preventing depolarization of quiescent neuron b. See text for further explanations. (See Color Plate 46.1 in color plate section.)

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/18655912](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/18655912)

### Josh — 2014-09-21T01:25:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ms-blog-is-live-disease-of-light.11734/post-141476>

With molecular diagrams and interactions:

> **ABSTRACT**
> Aquaporin (AQP) 4 is the predominant water channel in the mammalian brain, abundantly expressed in the blood–brain and brain–cerebrospinal fluid interfaces of glial cells. Its function in cerebral water balance has implications in neuropathological disorders, including brain edema, stroke, and head injuries. The 1.8-Å crystal structure reveals the molecular basis for the water selectivity of the channel. Unlike the case in the structures of water-selective AQPs AqpZ and AQP1, the asparagines of the 2 Asn-Pro-Ala motifs do not hydrogen bond to the same water molecule; instead, they bond to 2 different water molecules in the center of the channel. Molecular dynamics simulations were performed to ask how this observation bears on the proposed mechanisms for how AQPs remain totally insulating to any proton conductance while maintaining a single file of hydrogen bonded water molecules throughout the channel.

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2678640/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2678640/)

### Josh — 2014-09-21T01:55:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ms-blog-is-live-disease-of-light.11734/post-141481>

How they are made:

> **Abstract**
> The past decade has witnessed remarkable advances in our understanding of aquaporin (AQP) structure and function. Much, however, remains to be learned regarding how these unique and vitally important molecules are generated in living cells. A major obstacle in this respect is that AQP biogenesis takes place in a highly specialized and relatively inaccessible environment formed by the ribosome, the Sec61 translocon and the ER membrane. This review will contrast the folding pathways of two AQP family members, AQP1 and AQP4, and attempt to explain how six TM helices can be oriented across and integrated into the ER membrane in the context of current (and somewhat conflicting) translocon models. These studies indicate that AQP biogenesis is intimately linked to translocon function and that the ribosome and translocon form a highly dynamic molecular machine that both interprets and is controlled by specific information encoded within the nascent AQP polypeptide. AQP biogenesis thus has wide ranging implications for mechanisms of translocon function and general membrane protein folding pathways.

[http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0005273606001738](http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0005273606001738)

### PaulG — 2014-09-23T10:05:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ms-blog-is-live-disease-of-light.11734/post-141705>

This article entitled "[**Cell-to-cell signaling through light: just a ghost of chance?**](http://www.biosignaling.com/content/11/1/87)**"** concludes

> The conclusion we offer is that cellular signaling through light, if it exists as a general phenomenon, employs principles and techniques which are not expected to exist in living cells based on the currently accepted understanding of biophysical processes. So far, it seems that cellular signaling through light (on the level of single cells) is either
>
> 1\. an example of Foster’s “mechanisms paradox, _i.e._ the absence of established mechanisms by which electromagnetic fields at levels found in ordinary environments could produce observable changes in biological systems” [34](http://www.biosignaling.com/content/11/1/87#B34)\], or
>
> 2\. is not accomplishable under natural conditions and the reported experiments mentioned in the introductory section should be attributed to another phenomenon.
>
> In this paper, we have shown how the idea of cellular signaling through light as a general phenomenon has fundamental limitations given by the ambient noise, which has, in the majority of circumstances, a much higher intensity than the endogenous cellular signal. Cells indeed emit light. However, as a by-product of bio-chemical processes, it has a very weak intensity. This light may bear information about its generation processes, but the detection of such a weak signal would require such mechanisms that would be very difficult, if not outright impossible, to find in biological systems. We may conclude that the signaling between cells which employ electromagnetic waves is possible in principle, but it is very likely limited to very special circumstances or it would require physical mechanisms which are inapplicable in biology or yet unknown.

> Mainly, there are no generally accepted mechanisms for generation of UV emission via endogenous chemical excitation in biological systems.

but there is no mention of Popp and I think they are missing the Quantum piece.

### PaulG — 2015-03-17T05:38:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ms-blog-is-live-disease-of-light.11734/post-158736>

Anyone got a link to pdf of [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/25188068](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/25188068)

Neuronal Cellular Responses to Extremely Low Frequency Electromagnetic Field Exposure: Implications Regarding Oxidative Stress and Neurodegeneration
[http://www.plosone.org/article/fetchObject.action?uri=info:doi/10.1371/journal.pone.0104973&representation=PDF](http://www.academicjournals.org/article/article1378991538_Ahuja%20et%20al.pdf)

"Although our ELFEMF exposure condition induced an early rise in NOS and O2 2, and adaptive cellular changes in CAT activity, it did not exert any cytotoxic or phenotypic changes, as confirmed by the fact that cell morphology and proliferation were unaltered after 6 and 24 hr ELF-EMF"

> Abstract Neurodegenerative diseases comprise both hereditary and sporadic conditions characterized by an identifying progressive nervous system dysfunction and distinctive neuopathophysiology. The majority are of non-familial etiology and hence environmental factors and lifestyle play key roles in their pathogenesis. The extensive use of and ever increasing worldwide demand for electricity has stimulated societal and scientific interest on the environmental exposure to low frequency electromagnetic fields (EMFs) on human health. Epidemiological studies suggest a positive association between 50/60-Hz power transmission fields and leukemia or lymphoma development. Consequent to the association between EMFs and induction of oxidative stress, concerns relating to development of neurodegenerative diseases, such as Alzheimer disease (AD), have been voiced as the brain consumes the greatest fraction of oxygen and is particularly vulnerable to oxidative stress. Exposure to extremely low frequency (ELF)-EMFs are reported to alter animal behavior and modulate biological variables, including gene expression, regulation of cell survival, promotion of cellular differentiation, and changes in cerebral blood flow in aged AD transgenic mice. Alterations in inflammatory responses have also been reported, but how these actions impact human health remains unknown. We hence evaluated the effects of an electromagnetic wave (magnetic field intensity 1mT; frequency, 50-Hz) on a well-characterized immortalized neuronal cell model, human SH-SY5Y cells. ELF-EMF exposure elevated the expession of NOS and O2 2, which were countered by compensatory changes in antioxidant catylase (CAT) activity and enzymatic kinetic parameters related to CYP-450 and CAT activity. Actions of ELF-EMFs on cytokine gene expression were additionally evaluated and found rapidly modified. Confronted with co-exposure to H2O2-induced oxidative stress, ELF-EMF proved not as well counteracted and resulted in a decline in CAT activity and a rise in O2 2 levels. Together these studies support the further evaluation of ELF-EMF exposure in cellular and in vivo preclinical models to define mechanisms potentially impacted in humans.

NEURODEGENERATIVE DISORDERS
[http://www.who.int/peh-emf/publications/Chapter 7.pdf](http://www.who.int/peh-emf/publications/Chapter%207.pdf)

A Paper on Austism
[http://www.academicjournals.org/article/article1378991538\_Ahuja et al.pdf](http://www.academicjournals.org/article/article1378991538_Ahuja%20et%20al.pdf)

# Thread: Any suggestions on how to improve my hormones?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-suggestions-on-how-to-improve-my-hormones.11736/>
**Subforum:** Heal Your Hormones
**Posts:** 39
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### melrito — 2014-09-19T00:01:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-suggestions-on-how-to-improve-my-hormones.11736/post-141358>

I would desperately like to get my dhea levels up, they are literally in the toilet (along with all the others including estrogen, progesterone, thyroid, and cortisol). I am eating a clean epi-paleo diet (with seafood and sea veggies every day), CT in the winter (I give myself a break in the summer) and have been doing this for almost 3 years no with no improvement in my hormones whatsoever - in fact, they have all gotten worse. I wear blue blockers after dark until I go to sleep shortly after, have no wifi or cordless phone, and use my cell phone about 10 minutes a month (the rest of the time it's on airplane mode as there is no cell service where I live). I live in the middle of nowhere, so no cell towers or other towers or antennas anywhere within 3 miles of my home - there isn't even a paved road. I get natural sunlight every day, get natural forms of movement and walking every day, and sleep well. I have no idea why my hormones continue to go south. I feel pretty good overall with improved energy, but I have been gaining weight the past year (up 30 lbs. so far even though I wasn't thin before) and because I haven't changed anything, I assume it is my bad hormone levels finally catching up to me (I am in perimenopause). I was estrogen dominant despite low estrogen levels, but do supplement with progesterone and now have a good ratio, although estrogen is still very low (1.1 where the range is 1-5). In an attempt to improve my DHEA-S level (1.3 where the range is 2-10), I supplemented with oral DHEA for a while about 2 years ago and my body converted it mostly to testosterone, causing me a lot of aggression, so I had to stop, even though it did improve my DHEA-S level. I have recently tried adding DHEA cream (twist DHEA), hoping that would not convert to testosterone as readily, and have only been using this a week, but it is causing my breasts to be very sore. Has anyone else has experienced this or can anyone shed some light as to what could be going on? Any suggestions are greatly appreciated, I am at a total loss at this point.

I use blue blocker glasses after dark if I want to watch a movie (which I play through a computer which has f.lux). I use a cell phone very rarely and it is normally turned off since there is no cell service at my home. I am at home 95% of the time, so I am not in other environments often. I have eaten all raw in the past for several months, but enjoy my food more when it is cooked (steaming or crock pot usually). I haven't had any high meat. ### melrito — 2014-09-21T15:00:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-suggestions-on-how-to-improve-my-hormones.11736/post-141521>

Inger, I ate raw animal foods and some raw vegetables exclusively and I did gain weight on that as well. I like raw animal foods, especially fish and beef, but do like to mix it up with cooked foods - and particularly like things like roast done in a crock pot. It's probably a seasonal thing since it is getting colder here, or a mental thing (wanting warm food in the wintertime, even though I don't find raw foods cooling).

Josh, I have done parasite testing (using 2 different tests and then a retest after eradicating a parasite that was found), intestinal permeability testing (using the BioHealth 304 that Dr. Kruse recommended in his book) and have checked my methylation through my 23andme results working together with a specialist - there was nothing spectacular there. I have done several food sensitivity tests (IgE, IgG, and a mediator release test) and was sensitive to tons (even without testing I pretty much knew what was bothering me and testing backed that mup) and removed those foods. I have been able to add most of them back about a year later without problems, but still do not tolerate eggs at all or wheat/gluten (due to an IgE wheat allergy) and avoid all grains and legumes. I have added back raw butter from a local farmer and feel better when I eat some from time to time. I have done several hair mineral analyses as well as the cyrex array which tests for antibodies to environmental toxins. It's time for me to retest everything again since it has been about 6 month since last testing. I know there is something going on that I'm not finding (and the doctors and practitioners I have worked with haven't been able to find) that is holding me back - whether it's environment or internal - it's just frustrating to not be able to figure it out. I am considering trying to find another practitioner to help me, but I haven't figured out who yet.

I really appreciate all the input everyone has had - it motivates me to go back over everything and be sure I don't leave any stone unturned.

-   Electromagnetic -both activates and attracts energy

### kovita — 2014-09-26T10:55:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adjusting-to-keto-high-fat.11739/post-141972>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=141969)
>
> I seem to remember oner of the men also saying that his little girl would barely eat anything ...but if she could choose - she would eat boatloads of protein..... all most as much as him!
>
> Would be a good "ask Jack" question ...would a kid gravitate to a good diet for themself - like their own n=1? totally unscripted and just make natural choices according to the seasons and what their body wanted?
>
> I am pretty positive too that Fressia said the same thing about her kids ...when fruit was in season they would eat their fill ...and then be done with it. Really interesting .....

form my experience with my two children, I have to agree completely. There are certain foods my kids choose thorough the year, it is mainly meat, fish, butter and organ meats. They are not very keen to eat exotic fruits, but they go wild anytime there is something edible in the garden. They stuff themselves to that point, that for example in the currant season their poop may look like the bird poop, full of seeds. I am adding seaweed to some dish everyday and they absolutely love cruciferous vegetables and artichokes (now is the season here). I am teaching them to find the food in the nature, they love it and it is a great fun. Just last weekend we were in the countryside and the game was to find and bring as much wild food as possible. I have to say I felt very proud because even my 3 y old would easily survive a day on what he collected (early autumn is particularly rich for wild foods).

Are you completely comfortable letting your children just choose for themselves ...or do you need to prompt them sometimes?

### BTA — 2014-09-20T00:33:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-your-grass-fed-beef-safe.11740/post-141436>

I've super thankful to have a number of organic grass fed farms in my area. Everything from lamb, pork, raw milk & raw milk products, beef, elk, ect.... & I'm only 40 minutes from the Ocean - fresh seafood too.

Now I see Monsanto has figured out a loophole to use GMO-grass to be used on animal farms & it's all legal. What this means is farm animals can legally eat GMO-grass & still be able to call it "organic" grass fed meat at the grocery store. The grass will be laced with the weed killer herbicide called Roundup. Suppliers are not required to label it or let anyone know what's going on. Just scary

[http://www.infowars.com/kiss-your-grass-fed-beef-goodbye-gmo-grass-about-to-be-approved/](http://www.infowars.com/kiss-your-grass-fed-beef-goodbye-gmo-grass-about-to-be-approved/)

# Thread: Global Warming Update
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/global-warming-update.11741/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BTA — 2014-09-20T07:20:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/global-warming-update.11741/post-141441>

Looks like it's going to be hard to hide the fact the planet will be a bit colder, Maunder Minimum.
Now Al Gore and other world experts on Climate Change announce that due to the planet warming up so much we should expect to see extremely cold winters starting this year. Hot makes things cold? ha!

[http://www.infowars.com/actual-head...se-colder-and-snowier-winters-scientists-say/](http://www.infowars.com/actual-headline-global-warming-likely-to-cause-colder-and-snowier-winters-scientists-say/)

Then scientists worldwide ditch evidence and now base their results on a consensus of what other science experts think. Who needs facts when you can just have a poll of what other experts think?

[http://jonrappoport.wordpress.com/2014/09/18/un-climate-change-1000-scientists-say-no/](http://jonrappoport.wordpress.com/2014/09/18/un-climate-change-1000-scientists-say-no/)

### Jack Kruse — 2017-09-22T01:21:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/global-warming-update.11741/post-224101>

Climate scientists are just marketers who are looking for dollars from governments. Marketing is nothing more than applied psychology. It’s about connecting with people, persuading them, and, if good marketing helping them. Much of marketing is done to take advantage of people by separating them from their dollar by selling and incomplete story of the truth. This is why many people think marketing is a nasty or immoral thing. It is not when you have the proper context down. It is also something to keep in mind when evaluating someone' pitch to you. Marketing entrepreneurs are dopamine junkies that have found their calling. Just realize what is good for them may not be optimized for your biology. HOW SCIENTISTS GOT THEIR GLOBAL WARMING SUMS WRONG AND BULLIED EXPERTS
And all because a tiny bunch of scientists got their sums wrong and scared the world silly with a story about catastrophic man-made global warming.
This scare story, we now know, was at best an exaggeration, at worst a disgraceful fabrication. But while a handful of reviled and derided skeptics have been saying this for years, it’s only this week that those scientists have fessed up to their mistake. [https://www.thesun.co.uk/news/4503006/global-warming-sums-experts-bullies-james-delingpole-opinion/](https://www.thesun.co.uk/news/4503006/global-warming-sums-experts-bullies-james-delingpole-opinion/)

### Jack Kruse — 2014-09-24T05:10:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/holy-cow-where-to-begin.11744/post-141794>

**What happens in a mismatched enviroment? (Light or Carbs)**
When we live in a mismatched environment to light or carbs, we can not compensate well, so what happens? We begin to lose estrogen and progesterone in our brain and it begins to collect in our gut, our fat cells and in testes and breasts. The excessive estrogens can’t be cleared by the liver and the excesses lead to neolithic diseases when this occurs chronically. Light levels correspond to food sources everywhere on this planet. This also implies we are not well adapted for traveling long distances and seeing different food sources. Our gut was not designed for intercontinental travel it appears. Maybe this is why jet lag is not well tolerated? Moreover, it ruins the cortisol/DHEA/melatonin axis where light is a major player in the brain circuitry and this alters our gut flora. This maybe why the environment we grew up in has such fond memories for us and why we seem to adapt awfully fast when we return to it? The information in the food sources are transmitted to our brain by the gut flora’s action. There is a transaction of micronutrients that happens at our gut and our immune system sits right there to check the score. The brain is also paying attention to this accounting too, even if modern science is not. This dance is very complex, but fascinating to me.

**So how is this signal transmitted in the brain?**
**Simple, it is the hormone response of the epigenetic signal given to us from the gut flora.**

Our brain has more neurons in our gut than any other place in the body. We often call it our second brain. Moreover, the brain does not have a hard wired circuit to every single cell in the gut, or every bacteria in the gut, so it transduces the signal of these 100 trillion cells using a hormone response to modulate this signal. This is how the environmental signal of food is transmitted to the neural signal of the brain’s alphabet. This is why in [Brain Gut 5](http://www.jackkruse.com/brain-gut-5-paradigm-drifts-paradigm-shifts-epi-paleo/), I told you hormones are the Rosetta Stone for deciphering what is best for the brain. I told you there to **think of food as hormone information, not as a metabolic fuel.**

We can decipher the message once we understand the language of the gut flora to the brain. This is why understanding hormones is critical to obtaining health.

**Becoming an Epi-Paleo Rx Translator of Heath**
When we see an altered hormone panel in clinical practice it might signal something small now, but it could morph into something you mind might not even comprehend. If this continues long enough you will wind up with some major health changes that become very complex and very difficult to treat. These diseases are treated only when they become firmly entrenched and obvious to most clinicians but the majority of patient suffering from them have the problem persist for years undetected until things worsen.

**Today’s analogy to bring this home**
**Non Geek Alert:** Today, we are going to use a candida infection (fungus) of the gut to illustrate for you how it can radically alter your healthy gut flora. We generally ingest candida from foods and organic matter from the environment but our immune system does a rather good job of keeping this yeast under control when allostasis is maintained between our environment and our hormones. When this balance is tilted out of our favor, we wind up favoring the situation that supports colonization from the yeast, and if the situation is allowed to persist long enough, it could become a frank sub clinical infection of the gut. If left longer still, it could become a systemic problem that could cause major diseases like breast cancer or prostate cancer. That might seem like a pretty big jump in disease risk to some people, but understanding how it all happens is important. It shows you how important great gut health is to a modern human’s physiology. **The most common way the gut flora is altered in our modern world is via indiscriminate carbohydrate and antibiotic use.** These create opportunities for candida grab some real estate on the gut flora’s monopoly board.

When a candida infection begins, it is because there are too many simplified carbohydrates being eaten at the same time as the inoculation of the yeast. This favors its growth because candida metabolizes sugars. If the diet is truly seasonal in carbohydrate exposure this is not a usually a problem, but in our modern world, carbohydrates are now available 24/7 and they are quite bio available and cheap. Modern medicine also uses antibiotics in many clinical situations which might be over kill. I have personally tried to curb my own use of prophylactic antibiotics before and after surgery now because of this issue. I have personally found the most effective way to stop infections is to re-engineer my patients gut flora about three weeks before surgery using a special protocol and cocktail that I developed. I follow this up with a strict [Epi-Paleo Rx](http://jackkruse.com/brain-gut-6-epi-paleo-rx/), I call the [EPCOTx Protocol](http://www.jackkruse.com/the-epcotx-rx/) that is designed to limit inflammation. [Check this link out to get a good idea of how complex things can get.](http://circ.ahajournals.org/content/106/3/289.full)

This is done to dramatically lower inflammation and leptin levels. Candida is known to flourish in high leptin level states (inflammed). These people also tend to be leptin resistant. When leptin levels are high it activates the inflammatory cascade via cytokines. This is all mediated through [poor intracellular signaling of NF kappa beta](http://www.cavemandoctor.com/wp-content/uploads/2012/08/Ancestral-Health_2012_Poster_Colin_Champ.gif), which is the ‘911’ number in a cell.

**So who suffers from Candida?**
This availability causes the gut flora to simplify, in both species of bacteria, and in shear numbers. We normally have 100 trillion gut bacteria and about 150-250 species of bacteria/yeasts/parasites in a normal gut. When the mix simplifies it decreases and changes the bacterial microfilms that the gut bacteria grow on. This is the perfect opportunity for candida to take over a gut. There are many ways this can happen, but I am just explaining to you the two most common ways it happens in our modern world. Diabetics have the largest battles with candida. This is why T2D, candida, and an obesity type flora tend to occur together. The situation for T1D or a Moby/LADA diabetic is quite different.

**And what symptoms do they have?**
We already mentioned T2D are a big portion of the sufferers. But what are the symptoms of a candida infection and altered gut micro-biome? Symptoms vary wildly between people because their gut flora does as well, and this makes it a tough diagnosis for many physicians. Most MD’s are taught to look for the low hanging fruit symptoms, like a white patch on mucous membranes that can be scrapped off to show a red map-like covering of the surface. We see this is in the aero-digestive tract and in the genitourinary system often. The more “**zebra like**” symptoms can be chronic fatigue and loss of energy. They can be related to depression and progress to regular or migraine headaches, bloating, excessive gas as well. One of the more interesting ones I see often as a neurosurgeon is pain or tightness in and between the shoulder blade area. This is commonly seen in degenerative disc disease (or herniation) of the neck and in gallbladder disease. An astute clinician must be aware of the unusual presentation of this yeast infection. That ability was more adept in the days before evidence based medicine. A cookbook approach, like evidence based medicine, won’t find these things often in my view. Candida can also contribute to brain fog, mood swings, memory loss, itching, multiple region joint pain with normal X-rays, chronic indigestion, ulcers, many sinus conditions and cancer generation as well.

**Who else is at risk?**
When the situation becomes entrenched by a western diet, modern antibiotics or any chronic stressor, we might see the development of many diseases we see in modern man. Chronic candida can also contribute to irritable bowel syndrome, ulcerative colitis, Crohn’s disease, multiple food intolerances, chronic heartburn and gastric acid reflux disease (GERD). This can get so bad that a form of candida can become invasive and burrow into organs and be deadly. I have taken care of many brain abscesses from candida in those people. Since candida albicans produces alcohol and acetaldehyde in its normal life cycle, it may also contribute to many other subtle dysfunctions, including general toxicity, liver dysfunction, nutrient deficiencies (Cu, Mg, Zn,I, Se) and more as I mentioned above.

Any production of alcohol means that it will cause the formation of higher levels of estrogens like estrone and estradiol in many tissues of the body. These hormones continue to collect and overwhelm the normal detox pathways in the liver called phase 1 and 2 detoxification pathways. You can think of phase 1 as being responsible for breaking things down and then sending the raw materials to phase 2, which builds new substances from the raw material by adding molecules to them (this is called conjugation).

Phase one detox is carried out by the P450 enzyme system in the liver. A diet low in protein-all too common in women who are trying to lose weight with a low-fat diet can dramatically slow phase II detoxification. Aspirin and other non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs), including ibuprofen, also slow phase II detoxification.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-09-24T05:10:25+0300

**Want some easy clinical symptoms to pay attention too to fix your inner guru?** Does garlic make you sick to your stomach? Does your urine have a strong smell after you eat asparagus? Did you suffer from toxemia during your pregnancy? Did your Vitamin D level drop quickly? Any of these symptoms might indicate problems with phase II estrogen clearance. (Phase 1 depletes methyl donors (low B12 and betaine) and phase 2 has defective conjugation) When these pathways do not work well it can cause many radical changes in the remainder of the flora to continue to co evolve and create the perfect storm to cause cancer in the thyroid, pancreas, ovary, colon, prostate, breast, and in the uterus. These tissues are all epithelial based tissues. This is why cancer is exploding in all these organs. The recent explosion of thyroid and pancreatic cancer has caught my profession off guard. It makes complete sense to me because I understand how circadian mismatches can kill us. (Channel Steve Jobs, Walter Payton, Gilda Radner, Joe Piscopo)

Candida infections often are linked to insulin resistance and epithelial cancers with massive depletion in fat soluble vitamins (A,D. K, E) and micronutrients in foods. You might be seeing why now why all these cancer types have risen dramatically in the last 112 years. **We now live in that perfect storm.** Colon cancer and breast cancer rates have been growing at exponential rates over the last 50 years in particular. Curcumin, the compound that gives turmeric its yellow color, is interesting because it inhibits phase I while stimulating phase II. This effect can be very useful in preventing certain types of epithelial cancers. This is also why it is a vital part of my [EPCOTx PROTOCOL Rx](http://www.jackkruse.com/september-2012-webinar/) for the gut.

# Thread: Question Regarding BAB

### BTA — 2014-09-22T05:11:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-regarding-bab.11748/post-141574>

Just wondering if it's a concern that we can't digest & utilize most of our protein we ingest? A very healthy adult can only breakdown 15%-20% of the protein they ingest. However, very few of us are in prime shape; the most we can utilize is 1%-5% of the protein we ingest. The rest going to fat storage - not good...! And, the fallout of high protien results in a number of problems from kidney function to high levels of NH4+

I'm wondering if a high dose Protease Enzymes would be of benefit? This why bodybuilders take high doses of steriods - it helps to maximize protein uptake to nearly 100%. Which results in massive muscles.

The more you can break down the better & avoid getting fat ???

Thoughs - ideas?

[Int J Sport Nutr Exerc Metab.](http://forum.jackkruse.com/#) 2006 Apr;16(2):129-52.
[Neuropharmacology.](http://forum.jackkruse.com/#) 2003 Mar;44(4):524-32.
[http://www.nap.edu/openbook.php?record\_id=10490&page=589](http://www.nap.edu/openbook.php?record_id=10490&page=589)
[Am J Clin Nutr.](http://forum.jackkruse.com/#) 2009 Jan;89(1):161-8. doi: 10.3945/ajcn.2008.26401. Epub 2008 Dec 3.

My thought is this. A lot of the CW about protein is BS. A lot of what paleo says about protein is BS. Context matters. Gut flora matters. Leptin sensitivity matters. Quality of protein source matters. A lot of other things matter. And finally, any protein that "can't be broken down" would not be "going to fat storage," so that's just wrong.

### BTA — 2014-09-22T19:14:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-regarding-bab.11748/post-141620>

you can only breakdown so much protein - I know that's a fact from my own research and background knowledge. Which is a rate limiting factor. The more you can breakdown the better you'll be in the long run. Just wondering about how this effects health & having such a big BAB won't go to good use? It seems to me Enzymes to break down food would a key factor in this epi-paleo diet?

### NeilBB — 2014-09-22T19:19:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-regarding-bab.11748/post-141621>

A couple of years ago, I tried to lose the last of my old belly fat by severely limiting my protein, like to Jimmy Moore, Ron Rosedale levels. Massive fat with very strict protein controls. I tested my blood ketones too. Was making plenty of betahydroxybuterate. But my belly got bigger and I lost muscle mass. Not too much, but noticeable. Was definitely stalled. Also felt low energy.

Then, I went epi-paleo. I added a ton of seafood and CT, still ate a ton of fat, but now ate a LOT of protein too with more aggressive hydration. Haven't limited protein since, AT ALL... I eat 24 ounce steaks for breakfast routinely in addition to a lot of seafood. Have lost the belly fat, gained muscle, and improved in virtually every other detectable way I can think of.

I skimmed Jimmy's new book, Keto-Clarity this weekend at bookstore. Nothing at all in it about DHA, omega-3 or seafood importance. Only seafood recipe he throws in is bacon-wrapped salmon, cause "his wife loves it so much and she has it once a week." It's an almost all fat, very low protein, low seafood approach. It's only a half-truth, and will likely discourage a lot of people who try it. That's a shame to me.

### NeilBB — 2014-09-22T19:24:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-regarding-bab.11748/post-141623>

> [BTA said:](/goto/post?id=141620)
>
> you can only breakdown so much protein - I know that's a fact from my own research and background knowledge. Which is a rate limiting factor. The more you can breakdown the better you'll be in the long run. Just wondering about how this effects health & having such a big BAB won't go to good use? It seems to me Enzymes to break down food would a key factor in this epi-paleo diet?

Your body is designed to make those enzymes. The stomach also needs to be acidic to facilitate protein digestion. Most of the world is on antacids, H2 blockers, and/or proton pump inhibitors... Not good...

It's not about food or supplements. It's about the construction of the cell and the environment it finds itself in. If the cells functions are optimized, the ingested protein will be maximally utilized. Anything else is a band-aid.

All eaten raw. This is a normal meal for me.. and i do such large meals 3 times a day.... sometimes only 2. one 0f three might be a little smaller, depends.... I never count calories or protein or fat.. I just eat - as much as my heart desires
I have way over 100 grams of protein / day... I eat like this for years already. I eat more seafood than land mammals.
And it has not made me fat at all.... i guess I do 3000 calories / day average? I am 166 cm and I guess 55 kg and I am strong but I do no exercise. I feel amazing
But I do almost all raw. Sometimes a steak gently warmed in the pan so it is still raw inside... but you get the picture. I barely use my stove

I have no regrets, because I really feel like needing those electrons.... go figure

But I really avoid EMF... I ground so much.. and I spend so much time outside in the sun... I am very strict about avoiding blue light and go early to bed every day... my sleep is sacred!

### NeilBB — 2014-09-23T02:17:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-regarding-bab.11748/post-141655>

> [BTA said:](/goto/post?id=141620)
>
> you can only breakdown so much protein - I know that's a fact from my own research and background knowledge. Which is a rate limiting factor. The more you can breakdown the better you'll be in the long run. Just wondering about how this effects health & having such a big BAB won't go to good use? It seems to me Enzymes to break down food would a key factor in this epi-paleo diet?

You can only _assimilate_ so much protein at a time. (Protein synthesis). You can generally _breakdown_ a whole lot more. (Digestion/Proteolysis/Absorbtion). The limiting factor for bodybuilders, etc, is not in absorbtion, it's in synthesis in the muscles. In order for "protein to turn to fat," it has to first be broken into amino acids (or dipeptides) to even be absorbed at all, and then metabolically converted to fat to be stored. So again, I'd say no, these digestive enzymes will not help in any way to prevent the excess ingested protein turning to fat. Also, protein does a lot more than hypertrophy muscles in a healthy body. Proteins are everywhere in the body. The anabolic steroids in bodybuilders are typically diverting resources away from other vital structural functions in order to hypertophy skeletal muscles.

### NeilBB — 2014-09-23T18:26:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-regarding-bab.11748/post-141740>

> [BTA said:](/goto/post?id=141738)
>
> so you're ok with accepting BAB with zero scientific proof - interesting

I was trained extensively in science but I also know philosophy and epistemology. I know what's wrong with today's science. This site is about a new scientific paradigm. Much of what is published today is inaccurate for a number of reasons. I believe in the scientific method, not the peer-reviewed dogma of its current proponents.

Clinical scientific literature does not control for variables that it does not recognize and it is biased for many socio-politico-economic reasons. Basic science is somewhat more reliable, but you still need to know how to assemble it into a complete system. That's what's going on here. Those who would rather accept the existing clinical literature at face value, rather than question scientific fundamentals typically flock to MDA or Kresser's site. That's not what Jack is about.

And the science of the BAB has more to do with chronobiology than probably any other science. I haven't heard you talk much about that science.

I also believe in results. If all "excess protein turned to fat" as you say, I'd be a 300 lb sick-slob right now. But fortunately, that's not the case. I'm as lean as I have been in over 30 years. And the amounts of protein that I can eat at one meal would boggle your mind...

I had a friend who would bury meat for her dog and the dog would wait a certain number of days before digging it up and eating it. Pretty much the same as high meat. I am not so brave yet. Hard to get my wife to buy in.....

> **Hákarl** or **kæstur hákarl** (Icelandic pronunciation: [\[ˈhauːkʰadl̥\]](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Help:IPA_for_Icelandic)) ([Icelandic](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Icelandic_language) for "shark") is a [food](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Food) from [Iceland](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iceland) consisting of a[Greenland shark](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Greenland_shark) or [sleeper shark](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sleeper_shark) (_Somniosus microcephalus_) which has been cured with a particular [fermentation](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fermentation_\(food\)) process and hung to dry for four to five months. Hákarl is an [acquired taste](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Acquired_taste);[\[1\]](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/H%C3%A1karl#cite_note-herz-1) it has a very particular [ammonia](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ammonia)\-rich smell and fishy taste.
>
> Hákarl is served as part of a _[þorramatur](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/%C3%9Eorramatur)_, a selection of traditional [Icelandic food](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cuisine_of_Iceland) served at _[þorrablót](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/%C3%9Eorrabl%C3%B3t)_ in midwinter. It is readily available in Icelandic stores and is eaten year round.
>
>
>
> -   **Consumption\[[edit](http://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=H%C3%A1karl&action=edit&section=1)\]**
>
> [](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:H%C3%A1karl.JPG)
> Hákarl in a store
> Rotten shark is chosen instead of fresh shark meat because the meat of the [Greenland shark](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Greenland_shark) is poisonous when fresh,[\[2\]](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/H%C3%A1karl#cite_note-2) due to a high content of [urea](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Urea) and [trimethylamine oxide](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Trimethylamine_oxide), but may be consumed after being processed (see below). Allowing the shark to fully decay and cure removes retained uric acid from the flesh, making it edible.[\[3\]](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/H%C3%A1karl#cite_note-3) It has a particular ammonia smell, similar to many cleaning products. It is often served in [cubes](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cube) on [toothpicks](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Toothpick). Those new to it will usually gag involuntarily on the first attempt to eat it because of the high [ammonia](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ammonia) content.[\[1\]](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/H%C3%A1karl#cite_note-herz-1) First-timers are sometimes advised to pinch their nose while taking the first bite, as the smell is much stronger than the taste. It is often eaten with a shot of the local [spirit](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alcoholic_beverage), a type of [akvavit](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Akvavit) called[brennivín](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brenniv%C3%ADn). Eating hákarl is often associated with hardiness and strength.[\[4\]](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/H%C3%A1karl#cite_note-fish-4)
>
> It comes in two varieties; chewy and reddish _glerhákarl_ (lit. "glassy shark") from the belly, and white and soft _skyrhákarl_ (lit. "[skyr](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Skyr)shark") from the body.
>
> **Preparation\[[edit](http://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=H%C3%A1karl&action=edit&section=2)\]**
> Hákarl is traditionally prepared by gutting and beheading a [Greenland](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Greenland_shark) or [basking shark](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Basking_shark) and placing it in a shallow hole dug in gravelly sand, with the now cleaned cavity resting on a small mound of sand. The shark is then covered with sand and gravel, and stones are placed on top of the sand in order to press the shark. In this way the fluids are pressed out of the body. The shark ferments in this fashion for 6–12 weeks depending on the season.
>
> Following this curing period, the shark is then cut into strips and hung to dry for several months. During this drying period a brown crust will develop, which is removed prior to cutting the shark into small pieces and serving. The modern method is just to press the shark's meat in a large drained plastic container.[\[5\]](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/H%C3%A1karl#cite_note-suite101-5)
>
> It is possible to witness the traditional preparation process at Bjarnarhöfn Shark Museum[\[6\]](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/H%C3%A1karl#cite_note-6) on [Snæfellsnes](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sn%C3%A6fellsnes).
>
> **Reactions\[[edit](http://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=H%C3%A1karl&action=edit&section=3)\]**
> Chef [Anthony Bourdain](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Anthony_Bourdain) described hákarl as "the single worst, most disgusting and terrible tasting thing" he has ever eaten.[\[1\]](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/H%C3%A1karl#cite_note-herz-1)
>
> Chef [Gordon Ramsay](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gordon_Ramsay) challenged [James May](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/James_May) to sample three "delicacies" (Laotian snake whiskey, [bull penis](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bull_penis), and hákarl) on _[The F Word](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_F_Word)_; after eating hákarl, Ramsay spat it out, although May kept his down. May's only reaction was, "You disappoint me, Ramsay."[\[7\]](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/H%C3%A1karl#cite_note-7)
>
> On season 2's Iceland episode of Travel Channel's _[Bizarre Foods with Andrew Zimmern](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bizarre_Foods_with_Andrew_Zimmern)_, [Andrew Zimmern](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Andrew_Zimmern) described the smell as reminding him of "some of the most horrific things I've ever breathed in my life," but said the taste was not nearly as bad as the smell. Nonetheless, he did note that hákarl was "hardcore food" and "not for beginners".

[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hákarl](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/H%C3%A1karl)

> [Int J Sport Nutr Exerc Metab.](http://forum.jackkruse.com/#) 2006 Apr;16(2):129-52.
> [Neuropharmacology.](http://forum.jackkruse.com/#) 2003 Mar;44(4):524-32.
> [http://www.nap.edu/openbook.php?record\_id=10490&page=589](http://www.nap.edu/openbook.php?record_id=10490&page=589)
> [Am J Clin Nutr.](http://forum.jackkruse.com/#) 2009 Jan;89(1):161-8. doi: 10.3945/ajcn.2008.26401. Epub 2008 Dec 3.

Hi BTA

Except for the 1300+ page book you referenced, the other links are bad.

You keep referencing about high protein making one fat. Neil, Inger, myself and others are not overweight so your assertion is not universally valid. It seems that you aren't put together the causative factors of 'fatness' with many other concepts and references presented by Jack.

It is commonly understood that the amino acids that the body discards are converted to energy or stored energy. If the BAB is 50 g of protein and you convert the protein to fat 100% efficiently, it would amount to 0.78 oz pure fat \[fat=4086 Calories/lb (454x9); 50g protein = 200 Calories (4x50); 200/4086=0.0489 lbs of fat = 0.78 oz fat equivalent\]. Of course, that does not happen because it takes energy to breakdown protein and then build fat from the carbons. Kinda reminds me of that hypothetical extra teaspoon of sugar/day being responsible for those love handles 10 yrs later.

Consider also that if your body needed significant cysteine but not other aminos so much, it would need to strip out cysteine from everything else. Does that mean that protein is excessive? Not if you are lacking any aminos. So I don't think that it is about protein rather about amino acids.

How could my brain/body ever push the thyroid to function better when I kept telling it we're all good with meds?

I feel better than I have in quite some time. I am running a ton of labs while off all supplements and medication. I am still lacking in energy and clarity and I suspect methylation and or neurotransmitter issue.
Also I still suck overall at my blue light avoidance...which I know is a big part of the problem.

I too am shocked at the number of people with thyroid issues. I had always thought it a fat older ladies issue until a few years ago when my children all tested hypo...even the under weight one. But the straw that broke the camels back was when my dog tested hypo thyroid...THAT is what made me take a long hard look at our environment and decide to move in 2011. We didnt share the same food so it wasnt diet....lol

### Andrea — 2014-09-22T19:19:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-meds-and-leptin-rx.11752/post-141622>

I have been taking Levothyroxine (synthetic T4) and Liothyronine (synthetic T3) for a bunch of years. For quite some time I had had the feeling the the T4 med didn't do me much good, but I have no idea why. Last fall, after I had started with the Leptin Rx, I slowly started to decrease my dose. My T4 and free T4 dropped to far below the reference values, and my TSH increased to the top end of the reference values. As I slowly decreased the dose, the T4 values remained the same, but TSH got higher and higher.
Now it's been more than two months since I last took a tiny dose of T4, and my recent labs show that my T4 now is in the lower end of the reference value, and TSH has dropped to the top end of the ref.values.
For this time, I haven't noticed any difference in how I feel - with regard to decreasing my T4 dose.

I'm still taking Lio though. I tried to decrease my dose a bit a couple weeks back, but after a few days I was feeling cold, tired and had bad brain fog. So it didn't work out. But the winter is coming, and I intend to use it to try to decrease my dose again. Maybe a bit slower than the last time.

Jack Kruse](https://www.facebook.com/jack.kruse?fref=ufi)Another interesting observation: Suprarenalectomy (adrenalectomy) in rabbits was done 90 years ago and showed a dramatic effect in arresting thymic involution and results in dramatic thymic regeneration. So why does everyone seem to have AF on testing? Because your brain is tying to protect you from something in your environment PEOPLE. GUESS WHAT?

### River — 2014-09-25T04:24:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-meds-and-leptin-rx.11752/post-141872>

@[cantweight](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1283/) - I have a few questions for you, if you don't mind. I am in the process of downsizing / eliminating my thyroid meds like you are. My motives are the same of yours as I came to the same conclusion - that as long as I take thyroid medications I'll never know if my thyroid has healed from it's initial Hashi's diagnosis 10+ years ago.

You said you started with 180mg, and now you are taking no medication at all - how long did you take to reduce? I thought in a previous post you mentioned a year but wasn't sure. I'm starting at 120mg, which I have taken for many years. I have been taking only 60mg for the last two weeks and using my temperature, pulse, and how I feel in general as a guide.

I'm hoping that my ketogenic marine diet, with plenty of DHA plus adhering to all of the protocols including lots of nature, natural grounding, EMF mitigation, no lights after sunset or before sunrise, etc, that my thyroid may have what it needs now to begin regenerating or waking up, whatever is required.

I'm wondering how quickly I should start cutting back the 60mg that I am on now. My original plan was to stay on 60mg for six weeks, then cut that in half to 30mg for 6 weeks, then nothing for 6 weeks, then take a blood test. Is that similar to what you did?

Thanks for sharing your labs. Do you mind if I ask about your temperature? I take mine everyday and was planning on using it as one of my main feedback systems. When I was first diagnosed with Hashi's, it ran 94.0 most of the time and my pulse was only 50 or 52 (along with all the regular low thyroid symptoms).

After complete elimination of thyroid meds, how high should we let our TSH go, and how low should we let our T4 go? I know the thyroid needs time to receive the higher TSH and start working again.. are we trailblazers here or have there been any studies or people before us who are trying this while their bodies are supported by an epi-paleo diet and lifestyle?

I guess I'm wondering, when off meds completely, will our thyroids require a re-learning time? Or is the cellular memory intact and as soon as that TSH signal comes in, and if there is viable Thyroid tissue, will it just start cranking out the hormones?

It sounds like you've studied and pondered this quite a bit so anything additional you want to share is very meaningful. Thank you for what you have shared thus far!

# Thread: Circadian Success!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circadian-success.11753/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nonchalant — 2014-09-22T19:52:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circadian-success.11753/post-141625>

Ya'll are going to have to call me Dr. Non now. I fixed DH's rash!

Saturday evening he broke out in an extensive, blotchy rash over most of his body, especially in the less tanned areas like the back, insides of the arms and legs, etc. He got some relief that night from an OTC hydrocortisone ointment, but Sunday morning at 3am he was very uncomfortable. He didn't awaken me, poor thing. That morning I asked him to come outside and watch the sun rise with me. He stayed out in the sun for about 20 minutes in shorts, with bare feet and chest. He said the itchiness was getting worse, and I saw that the rashes were more red. He said even his head was itchy. But then in a few minutes he started feeling some relief, which rapidly took all of the itchiness away.

We came inside, because it was getting hot. But when his coffee was ready, he went back outside to sit in the shade of a tree. He asked why the sun had this effect, and I explained about the cortisol cycle and sunshine. I told him early sunshine would make cortisol peak earlier than it would otherwise. He was interested and listened closely. Maybe his wife isn't just a nut after all?

So I had the success of getting DH interested in the circadian rhythm! But wait, there's more!

Sunday evening his hives reappeared, though they weren't itchy yet. We drove home, so that we could visit the doctor in the morning. That night DH asked about putting some of the cream on as a preventive measure, but I convinced him to hold off. This morning, I asked him how he felt, and he said he was fine. Um... I was a bit surprised. He had a few of the blotches, but nothing like the evening before. And he slept through the night with no itching. He went outside to see the rising sun again with me.

We were both quite surprised. A while ago DH had a similar, though not as severe a rash, and it was a slowly progressing nightly ordeal for weeks until we went to the doc and got an Rx for a course of Prednisone tablets.

We both feel great now! Thanks Dr. K!

### Inger — 2014-09-22T20:18:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circadian-success.11753/post-141629>

Yay......... very cool!

Congratulations Doctor Sally - nothing better than when we are able to helps our loved ones

### nonchalant — 2014-09-22T21:59:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circadian-success.11753/post-141635>

Thanks! Nonchalant takes a bow.

I was wishing I had internet access, so I could ask about it on the forum. But then I realized I already knew the answer, I just had to figure out which protocol was key. That, and the fact that the rash was practically screaming "circadian rhythm" if you listened closely.

I told him that a less inflammatory diet would help, but he heard "no sweet tea" among the list and that was that... Oh well, one step at a time.

As to what caused the rashes, I think it was niacin. The previous rash was confusing, because poison ivy and travel/hotel stays were involved. I removed several supplements from his pillbox, but it didn't help. After he got the Rx, and a week passed with no issues, I gave him a tiny dose of 100 mg niacin on mondays only. The second rash occurred almost a week after the second such dose. And like I said, the second rash was more widespread and fast acting than the one before. A bit scary, actually.

I think his redox is low, and he cannot tolerate the niacin. And with his level of inflammation, perhaps one of those cytokine storm thingies was happening, despite the removal of the cause. We were away from the city, but you can't fix all the effects of a bad environment with a quick trip to the country. CT was also out, though he slowly (over several years) is tolerating more and more cold.

### fitness@home — 2014-09-22T23:08:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circadian-success.11753/post-141640>

Congrats on helping him with the rash Dr. Non

The next thing you know, you'll be posting a picture on the CT thread of the both of you CTing oustside in matching water troughs!

> I told him that a less inflammatory diet would help, but he heard "no sweet tea" among the list and that was that... Oh well, one step at a time.

^^ That sounds like someone I know too...

I have been thinking about buying a faraday canopy for my bed, as I can get 1 almost full and 2 very weak signals from neighbors into my bedroom. I have a faraday canopy that do not filter magnetic radiation in my thoughts. Swiss daylite.

### Erik — 2015-01-26T22:35:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-faraday.11758/post-153919>

### Jude — 2015-01-27T01:54:23+0200

### Martin — 2015-01-27T15:26:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-faraday.11758/post-153992>

Inger, the faraday enclosure seal must be flawlessly sealed up because any microwave signals that get in through cracks or holes will bounce around inside. You will feel like that "bit of steak in the microwave oven" that Dr Kruse references! But! You are on the ground level, and with a well-constructed canopy, it may be possible to achieve a good seal. Testing with a really good meter would be essential.

### Lahelada — 2015-01-27T16:47:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-faraday.11758/post-153996>

Quite inscientifically put but if it can get in then you must let it get out,otherwise you are in a microwave.
That would probably be the case for yuu as well Eric in your room.

### Lahelada — 2015-01-27T18:16:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-faraday.11758/post-154003>

This seems to be an answer that makes sense and is non geek accessible,for someone like me, like..

"At most frequencies, aluminum --\*especially\* the thin stuff, isn't all that great at absorbing EMI, and if you need to keep the RF from \*escaping\* from your design, you'll find it tends to bounce the RF around until it finds a seam somewhere else to escape. On the EMC side, if you're trying to protect your gear from inbound EMI, it can serve better in that fashion. On the other hand, steel will do a better.."
Full text here.

[https://answers.yahoo.com/question/?qid=20070413162340AAGdJCy](https://answers.yahoo.com/question/?qid=20070413162340AAGdJCy)

Wow. From Da Man (aka Dr Jack Kruse)....had to share this here:

Totally random thought for you all to consider, but how the hell does Japan have an obesity rate of 4%, when a huge chunk of its population is exposed to crazy amounts of EMF, 20+% of its population is smoking every day? Their diet is loaded with a crap ton of carbs all year round. Circadian cycles are definitely screwed for anyone in the working population and lower, with night life being a big thing. They don't even get much sun, with a city like Tokyo getting only 180hrs at most (6 hrs a day) due to summers being very rainy.

Could it be either there's a genetic thing going on (unlikely, since EMF will screw with all your epigenetic switches), or there's something about the region that is protecting them? One hunch is that the geological stressors which cause them to get hit by millions of earthquakes also are exposes them to a much stronger native magnetic field as a result is likely helping. But the big idea might be DHA. No country eats more of it constantly.

This shows how important DHA might be doesn't it?

# Thread: Why Bees Are Dying

### BTA — 2014-09-25T18:49:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-bees-are-dying.11773/post-141936>

Research shows why bees are dying at an alarming rate - nothing to do with EMF or magnetic shift ect....
neonicotinoids are to blame - a very nasty insecticide. Similar to the nicotine found in cigarettes. This chemical was suppose to be a safer alternative to the good ol' days when it was still legal to spray with organophosphate based insecticides. Earth has way too many people to support all of us - the over population has lead to the use of more and more deadly chemicals to force crops to grow at high yield levels. Forcing Mother Nature to do something she wasn't designed to do will have a negative fall out for all of us.

The link does contain references to the research.

[http://www.collective-evolution.com...udy-proves-why-the-bees-are-all-disappearing/](http://www.collective-evolution.com/2014/05/15/new-harvard-study-proves-why-the-bees-are-all-disappearing/)

Until we encounter the axiomatic foundational concepts currently modeled somewhat in quantum physics and rigorously apply them to our modern lives, we are little more than the energy sources for the robots modeled in the Matrix movies.

It's part of the struggle for power in the very fabric of civilization. No one is ever immune.

Read this: [hyperlink.](http://www.alternet.org/media/most-depressing-discovery-about-brain-ever) The “positions” are naturally typically either economically or emotionally favorable to the person or the group holding them. But these “positions” are also often absorbed from others and blindly habituated as well. Many organizations specifically exist today primarily to divert your mind from seeking truth or even from its other “positions” to instead support their particular “positions”: public relations firms, political strategists, all forms of advertising and marketing, religions, lobbies, charities, special interests groups, the media, etc…
>
> The point is that you actually have to actively and continually choose to keep your mental resources focused on truth. Or they will be diverted, by the formidable winds of society, like a sailboat with no one at the mast.

### Josh — 2014-09-25T23:02:21+0300

> [BTA said:](/goto/post?id=141936)
>
> Research shows why bees are dying at an alarming rate - nothing to do with EMF or magnetic shift ect....
> neonicotinoids are to blame - a very nasty insecticide. Similar to the nicotine found in cigarettes. This chemical was suppose to be a safer alternative to the good ol' days when it was still legal to spray with organophosphate based insecticides. Earth has way too many people to support all of us - the over population has lead to the use of more and more deadly chemicals to force crops to grow at high yield levels. Forcing Mother Nature to do something she wasn't designed to do will have a negative fall out for all of us.
>
> The link does contain references to the research.
>
> [http://www.collective-evolution.com...udy-proves-why-the-bees-are-all-disappearing/](http://www.collective-evolution.com/2014/05/15/new-harvard-study-proves-why-the-bees-are-all-disappearing/)

From a less metaphysical perspective, these pesticides are nicotine analogues. Dr. Kruse explained that in a person with a good redox, nicotine can do good things. In a person with a poor redox, "all hell brake loose". A bee exposed to EMF would have a challenged redox and would thus be highly susceptible to nicotine analogues causing "all hell brake loose". The Bee's no doubt have a threshold for being able to maintain metastability and function, including directional/magnetic sense, when this is exceeded "all hell brake loose"........ **_Interestingly, the history of these pesticides and their use almost exactly mirrors the development of the cellphone and other wireless technology as it has rolled out over the planet._** This means that there was an ever increasing EMF stress to go along with the persistent use of these. It suggests that one might study the stress from nnEMF in conjunction with exposure and see where the threshold was exceeded where the bees began to crash. One could see whether it coincided with a significant increase in either factor and or whether it it was first in areas with higher levels of EMF like America. One could go on from there and begin to look at parallels in humans persistently exposed to nicotine and nnEMF such as comparing smokers in areas with early nnEMF technology penetration versus smokers where it came late or even not at all. Just some random thoughts on what now seems obvious to me.

### Josh — 2014-09-25T23:10:48+0300

[http://ecowatch.com/2014/07/23/citizen-scientists-track-bee-health-colony-collapse-disorder/](http://ecowatch.com/2014/07/23/citizen-scientists-track-bee-health-colony-collapse-disorder/)

> Individuals can be part of the solution—and the fun—by keeping bees themselves. To construct the hives, keepers don’t need glue or screws, only a 4 foot by 8 foot piece of plywood and a CNC router. Those who aren’t quite as tech savvy with computer controlled saws can buy a prefabricated kit. There are two models: the smaller Colorado Top Bar (pictured) and the multi-tiered Barcelona Warre. They are easily shipped due to their flat design and packaging.
>
> These “smart hives” will be connected to [Smart Citizen](http://www.smartcitizen.me/) through a piece of hardware installed within the hives. Users can view data online as well as through a mobile app. Information such as CO levels, humidity, bee count, noise intensity and more will be sent via Wi-Fi. An upcoming feature will notify beekeepers when there is something amiss within their hive.

At 1:10 he talks about migratory birds flying into radio towers. From 1990 to 1995 I worked as an RF engineer for a public TV station. Every fall during migratory season it was common to come to work in the morning and find dead birds scattered all around the base of the transmitter tower, sometimes the count was in excess of 20 birds. It was always a sad sight to see.

### Jonathin — 2014-10-12T22:27:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-bees-are-dying.11773/post-143620>

I have bees and many people in our area are beekeepers as well. Sudden bee death is a complicated issue. Some issues not raised is that all honey bees are not the same genetically. They have deliberately messed with for decades by researchers and "developers".
What are their conditions of existence? Commercial hives often get sugar water in the spring, numerous chemicals deliberate and collateral, foraging on poor quality plants, questionable water, etc. EMFs are no doubt an issue, too. I have seen hives go down quickly in the spring that had plenty of their own honey stored in the hive and massively productive the year before. It is sad and frustrating. I don't care about the honey these days but their activity on flowering plants is amazing and no doubt beneficial in ways we don't understand fully.

# Thread: Adding H2O2 to the bathwater during CT may be superior to CT plus HBOT?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adding-h2o2-to-the-bathwater-during-ct-may-be-superior-to-ct-plus-hbot.11776/>

### ColinGorham — 2014-09-26T10:09:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adding-h2o2-to-the-bathwater-during-ct-may-be-superior-to-ct-plus-hbot.11776/post-141970>

In several interviews Jack Kruse has explained that CT increases Oxygen within the mitochondria and ultimately this increases the magnetic field of the body. He goes on to explain that HBOT (Hyperbaric Oxygen Therapy) works in much the same way as CT and therefore one might likely augment the other. Majid Ali, MD is likely one of the most knowledgeable physician in the world in terms of oxygen therapies. He explains that in his clinical experience H2O2 Baths (Hydrogen Peroxide) (for instance 3 bottles of 3% H2O2 in normal sized bathtub) is much more effective than HBOT at $2 a treatment verse $150-300 per treatment for HBOT. For this reason, I have been adding H2O2 to the bathwater during CT. I also use Hydrogen Peroxide in place of Chlorine to keep the water clean and only drain the water once a week. (Caution there may be danger in combining high levels of chlorine and H2O2)
