### ColinGorham — 2014-12-04T21:31:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adding-h2o2-to-the-bathwater-during-ct-may-be-superior-to-ct-plus-hbot.11776/post-149241>

[http://majidalimd.me/2014/12/03/dr-alis-peroxide-salt-bath-protocol-majid-ali-md/](http://majidalimd.me/2014/12/03/dr-alis-peroxide-salt-bath-protocol-majid-ali-md/)

**Dr. Ali’s Peroxide-Salt Bath Protocol**
**An Excellent Effective, Safe, and Inexpensive Oxygen Therapy**
Majid Ali, M.D.

**Hydrogen peroxide soaks are very helpful in nearly all cases of inflammatory, autoimmune, degenerative, and malignant tumors. The most dramatic demonstration of the benefits are seen in cases of chronic low-grade inflammation, tissue swelling, and poorly healing injured ligaments and joint tissues. Recent research has shown how minute amounts of hydrogen peroxide can activate several genes that are responsible for clearing tissue debris and improve venous and lymphatic circulation.**

For people who enjoy baths, I highly recommend the following protocol for doing peroxide bath. Please read the following text carefully twice and test it out.

**First Bath**

1\. Fill the bath tub with warm water with temperature you prefer.

2\. Add one quart of 3% hydrogen peroxide bottle to the bath tub.

3\. Add one-half teaspoon of sea salt to the bath water (Baleine is a good brand).

4\. Avoid direct contact of the bath tub water with eyes.

5\. After five minutes, add a second one quart bottle of 3% hydrogen peroxide to the bath water, if there is no tissue irritation is experienced (see caution note below).

6\. After five minutes, add the third quart bottle of 3% hydrogen peroxide to the bath water.

7\. Stay in the bath from 20 to 25 minutes.

**Second And Later Baths**

1\. Fill the bath tub with warm water with temperature you prefer.

2\. Add three one-quart bottles of 3% hydrogen peroxide to the bath tub.

3\. Avoid direct contact of the bath tub water with eyes.

4\. Stay in the bath from 20 to 25 minutes.

**Caution**

1\. Use bath water temperature that is comfortable for you.

2\. Some women with symptoms of vaginitis may experience temporary irritation and may need to use only one-half or one bottle of peroxide until tissue irritation clears up.

3\. Some persons experience lightheaded if they stand up suddenly after a warm bath. It is a necessary safety step that this possibility be kept in mind and one should get up slowly, holding the safety bar or the side of the bath tub.

**Note**

Brown colored 1 quart bottles of 3% hydrogen peroxide are generally available from drug stores and other stores, such as A&P. It pays to do comparative shopping.

[Peroxide Soaks – How Often Majid Ali MD](http://vimeo.com/92690414) from [Majid Ali](http://vimeo.com/user26130417) on [Vimeo](https://vimeo.com/).

The following text is taken from Dr. Ali’s Dysoxygenosis and Oxystatic therapies, the third volume of The Principles and Practice of Integrative Medicine. As is the case with all other information on this website, it is being offered as educational material, and is not a substitute for medical advice based on full clinical evaluation of the case.

My colleagues at the Institute and I routinely prescribe hydrogen peroxide baths for patients with all chronic circulatory disorders, lower leg edema caused by peripheral arterial insufficiency, varicose veins, unresolved trauma, low-grade chronic infectious and atopic processes. Based on clinical results obtained in several hundred patients, I now consider this therapy to be the safest and most effective therapy for those conditions.

[Precious Peroxide for Heart Failure Leg Edema Majid Ali MD](http://vimeo.com/95911997) from [Majid Ali](http://vimeo.com/user26130417) on [Vimeo](https://vimeo.com/).

**Peroxide Soaks for Inflammatory, Autoimmune, Degenerative, and Malignant Conditions**

Hydrogen peroxide soaks are very helpful in nearly all cases of inflammatory, autoimmune, degenerative, and malignant tumors. The most dramatic demonstration of the benefits are seen in cases of chronic low-grade inflammation, tissue swelling, and poorly healing injured ligaments and joint tissues. Recent research has shown how minute amounts of hydrogen peroxide can activate several genes that are responsible for clearing tissue debris and improve venous and lymphatic circulation.

[Peroxide Soaks Simplified Majid Ali MD](http://vimeo.com/91185657) from [Majid Ali](http://vimeo.com/user26130417) on [Vimeo](https://vimeo.com/).

**Disease Is Evolution In Reverse**

Disease states, at their core, are states of evolution in reverse—respiratory-to-fermentative shift in ATP energy generation. In 1999, I introduced the term oxystatic therapies for treatments that restore oxygen homeostasis in the body. I focused on all the elements that impede oxygen-driven functions of the body—energetic, metabolic, detergent, immune, detoxification, and regenerative—and so interfere with body’s healing responses. This is a crucial issue in the reversal of all chronic disorders.

[Spontaneity of Oxidation in Nature Part Three – Peroxide](http://vimeo.com/96932439) from [Majid Ali](http://vimeo.com/user26130417) on [Vimeo](https://vimeo.com/).

At the Institute, we have observed many empirical benefits of hydrogen peroxide for other clinical conditions. The mechanisms of action of hydrogen peroxide in those situations involve reduction of oxidative stress and restoration of oxygen metabolism.

The soaks can be done for the hands or feet at 20-30 minutes each soak. The soaks may be done at different concentrations of hydrogen peroxide. Any suitable vessel can be used for the soak.

[Peroxide Rubber Boot Club](http://vimeo.com/92690413) from [Majid Ali](http://vimeo.com/user26130417) on [Vimeo](https://vimeo.com/).

**Hydrogen Peroxide Baths With Meditation/prayer**

Fear and anger are crucial compounding factors in all serious threats to health — real or imagined — that creates the very conditions that threaten cellular health: oxidosis (too much oxidative stress), acidosis (too much acidity), and dysoxygenosis (dysfunctional oxygen metabolism). Thus, in my view, must be considered one of the top priorities in seasonal flu epidemics. Combining meditation/prayer with hydrogen peroxide foot soaks is the best approach to the twins problems of fear and threatened cellular oxygen metabolism.

****Majid Ali MD receives no financial remuneration from this website – all sales and donations go to adding as much free information as possible for you and future generations.****

### cinnamon — 2015-02-23T02:10:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adding-h2o2-to-the-bathwater-during-ct-may-be-superior-to-ct-plus-hbot.11776/post-156331>

I love magnesium oil!  I used to buy it already prepared, but then I found a place I could buy Magnesium Chloride flakes. The label says "Nigari" "Japanese Natural Bitterns". The brand I found is Mitoku and it is made in Japan. I make a super-concentrated solution by adding the flakes to a jar of good water. I add more flakes the next day and the next, until it is taking the whole day to dissolve any more. Then I half fill a small spray bottle with the concentrated solution and top it up with good water. It is way cheaper than buying the prepared stuff!

I use it on my skin twice a day. Be careful where you spray or rub this stuff, though! If you have any kind of nicks or cuts, it burns like crazy!  You will remember that you were warned when this happens to you!

I don't think it would be a problem, Christina. I don't recall it being an issue back when H2O2 was originally discussed.

[@ColinGorham](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/9094/) You have been so kind in answering so many of my questions and am hoping you can answer another - I think I may be rushing the building up of my skin callous - I let myself get a little sunburned, especially on my face and am worried I did irrerairable damage. Do you think I have and how did you build up you skin callous? My skin is quite fair.

### nonchalant — 2018-08-23T21:34:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adding-h2o2-to-the-bathwater-during-ct-may-be-superior-to-ct-plus-hbot.11776/post-249216>

Do you think I have and how did you build up you skin callous? My skin is quite fair.

I think you'll be surprised tomorrow at your skin's response to the burn. A sunburn that would have previously caused me to peel will now heal with just a tiny bit of flaking. I used to burn within an hour, any time of the day or year but now I'm quite resistant to burns. Don't worry, the damage is repairable, especially if you are sleeping well at night. The best way to build up your skin callous is to expose that skin to red morning (or evening) sunlight. Then when you sunbathe later in the day, your skin proteins are already hydrated, condensed and conditioned, ready for those UV rays.

If I were you, I'd expose that burned skin to this evening's red light, to jumpstart the healing, and sleep dark, cool, and with an emptied stomach.

Thank you agian for everything.

However, the iphone was in my pants pocket so I'm not certain it was from iphone. On the topic of devices that are supposedly lower in emf, I have found many of them to be a real hit and miss kind of situation. I have purchased things you plug into walls to reduce emf exposure which made me feel terrible, other items you just place around the house but don't plug into the power socket which also made me feel worse. I wear a pendant which supposedly reduces emf exposure and that is the only thing designed to reduce that doesn't seem to make me feel like crap.

Page 1:

IFA, B Burgdorferi G/M/A >80

Interpretation:
<40 Negative
40 Equivocal
\= or >80 Positive

Page 2:

LYME IgM Western Blot

Igenex IGM Result Negative
CDC/NYS Result Negative

18 kDa, -
\*\* 23-25 kDa -
28 kDa -
30 kDa -
\*\*31 kDa -
\*\*34 kDa -
\*\*39 kDa -
\*\*41 kDa IND
45 kDa -
58 kDa -
66 kDa -
\*88-93 kDa -

Page 3:

Multiplex B. Burgdorferi

Negative - Genomic: B burgdorferi DNA not detected
Plasmid: B burgdorferi DNA not detected

Positive - Genomic: B burgdorferi DNA not detected
Plasmid: B burgdorferi DNA not detected

Genomic: B burgdorferi Negative
Plasmid: B burgdorferi Negative

Page 4:

Lyme IgG Western Blot

Igenex IGG Result - Negative
CDC/NYS Result - Negative

By Igenex criteria, IgG Western blot is considered positive if two or more of the double starred bands below are present from either Group 1 (23-25, 31, 34, 39 and 83-93 kDa) or Group 2 (23-25, 34, 39, 41 and 83-93 kDa).

Presence only one double starred band or IND double starred bands may indicate clinical significance.

18 kDa, -
\*\* 23-25 kDa -
28 kDa -
30 kDa -
\*\*31 kDa -
\*\*34 kDa -
\*\*39 kDa IND
\*\*41 kDa +
45 kDa -
58 kDa +++
66 kDa -
\*88-93 kDa -

**The first one is an MRI**. MRIs are just like a spectroscopy exam of a star. It tells us precisely how much positive and negative charge are within tissues. MRIs of the brain and spine is the most accurate measure of redox potential I have, and it gives me a clue how bad off someone really is.

### Lauralei — 2014-09-26T22:11:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pulmonary-embolism.11782/post-141997>

Good afternoon, everyone.

Any suggestions would be helpful concerning the following:

I developed a pulmonary embolism, hospitalized (Aug. 25 - 29, 2014) with a blood oxygen level of 70, after a surgical procedure. I am 62, with a double C677T MTHFR defect. Currently taking the blood thinner Xarelto 15 mg daily; no other prescription meds. Taking CoQ-10 and adrenal supplement, plus multi-vitamin, multi-mineral.

Short of breath, 25% heart rate increase when resting, up to 50%+ when moving around (slowly). No energy, no stamina, very poor appetite (losing weight). Low grade fever every evening for about 1 hr.

Last week had an appointment with an alternative MD.

Anyone on the forum had any experience with this condition? The inactivity is causing depression & crankiness!

Thanks for any suggestions!

# Thread: Don't fool yourself, energy consumption counts.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dont-fool-yourself-energy-consumption-counts.11783/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ColinGorham — 2014-09-26T22:38:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dont-fool-yourself-energy-consumption-counts.11783/post-141999>

Don't fool yourself, energy consumption does count. Experiment with Calorie Monitoring/ Restriction during weight loss plateaus. I have read 150+ books on nutrition and have heard a lot of conflicting information and through much experimentation I have learned a lot. One can lose weight on a variety macronutrient ratios with Ad Libitum Feeding. (food available at all times with the quantity and frequency of consumption being the free choice of the animal.) For my body, I find that a Whole Food High Fat, Low Carb, Moderate Protein diet high in fiber, Seafood, and phytochemicals works best at naturally suppressing my appetite and provides the lipolytic enzymes necessary to burn stored body fat. This may be because High Insulin blocks Leptin signaling at the brain and when the brain can't get the leptin signal the body perceives starvation. Jack Kruse has provided us a myriad of non-dietary approaches to restore Leptin sensitivity: CT, EMF Mitigation, Blue Light Mitigation, Intermittent Fasting, Exercise, and the nutrition piece the EPI-Paleo Diet, etc.... If you are obese, here is the point. Obesity is a brain disease in that the brain is having a hard time creating optimal energy balance and this situation is exacerbated by mitochondrial dysfunction. Every time you hit a prolonged plateau in weight loss while doing everything else correct, get out the food scale and start weighing your food and calculating your intake to provoke an energy deficit. (With Siri this can be done in a about 1 minute per meal, Ask Siri how many calories in 5 ounces of Salmon etc...) A measure of Ketosis, whether, Blood beta hydroxybutyrate, Urine Ketone Bodies, or Acetone in the Breadth just means the body has toggled from glucose/ glycogen to fat (both ingested & stored) as a primary fuel of metabolism. The goal is not Ketosis alone, we ultimately want to burn stored body fat. In other words, we want to empty out fatty acids out of long term storage within the fat cell not simply burn recently ingested fatty acids that have not yet been stored within the fat cell. During weight loss plateaus, a degree of calorie restriction will induce the repair mechanism of autophagy to cleans diseased mitochondria and also ensure we are burning a high percentage of stored body fat for energy. (Note: Basel Metabolic Rate drops as you lose weight and so you require less calories to maintain your new normal weight) When you are keto adapted this process should be painless as you may have hundreds of thousands of stored calories residing within your fat cells for you to use for energy. Forget about what you think you know about energy consumption and weight loss. Be a scientist and experiment when you can’t sort out conflicting claims about energy balance.

### Sue-UK — 2014-09-27T16:49:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dont-fool-yourself-energy-consumption-counts.11783/post-142030>

When I first started reading Jack’s blogs I was already in the calorie counting doesn’t work club, and actually had a bet on with someone who believes in exercise more eat less. I said I could double my normal maintenance calorie intake and lose weight without exercising. It worked, I lost weight and had good energy levels, but a fatfest wasn’t a healthy solution, (and as expected I went back to my previous weight). To satisfy the bet, I had to eat whether I was hungry or not, and eat foods in relation to their calorie content, rather than what my body needed. For a couple of weeks it was interesting as a biohack, and it actually spurred me on into sticking with the Epi Paleo journey.

One of the things that stopped me continuing with it (I admit I was tempted ......) was that I knew my weight problems weren’t caused by lack of stuffing my face. I had already experienced my problems weren't caused by lack of self-imposed calorie deprivation. I originally gave that up when I realized I was teaching my then very young daughter to diet …… and to base self-worth on size …..

I have thought for a long time that healthy, leptin sensitive people don’t need to count calories, aren’t obsessed about food, will sometimes under eat (taking the extra advantage of autophagy) and if they do overeat their brains will account for the excess energy and will deal with it (for example as heat/natural increase in NEAT/naturally decreased appetite). I wanted a brain that could do that,  but thought I had damaged my metabolism too far, and that not getting worse was the best I could hope for. Thanks to Jack's teaching I now know differently.  Naturally occurring calorie restriction that results from my brain reading my energy status and lowering my appetite, naturally increasing NEAT etc is my goal. Self imposed calorie restriction doesn’t support my n=1. For me its forget calories, follow the electron ......

### Jack Kruse — 2014-09-27T17:35:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dont-fool-yourself-energy-consumption-counts.11783/post-142032>

^^^^I like that last statement.

### Krapule — 2014-09-27T23:47:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dont-fool-yourself-energy-consumption-counts.11783/post-142044>

Simple example of the principle Sue-UK: like Jack I spend my time in an operating room...when I work I get craving like crazy as the other people I work with...Today day off, one bulletproof coffee, a light snack for lunch and just did an obstacle race with my daughter and not very hungry yet and full of energy!
You have to be very obsessive to be healthy but you know better...

### Jude — 2014-09-28T13:44:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dont-fool-yourself-energy-consumption-counts.11783/post-142080>

> [Sue-UK said:](/goto/post?id=142030)
>
> For me its forget calories, follow the electron ......

Just gotta love those electrons eh!

### Sue-UK — 2014-09-29T11:25:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dont-fool-yourself-energy-consumption-counts.11783/post-142137>

> [Jude said:](/goto/post?id=142080)
>
> Just gotta love those electrons eh!

My weight has plateaued for about 9 months, but it doesn't worry me at all, I don't think it's a real plateau, its only one part of the equation. If anyone asks how much weight I've lost, the answer is none ....... I've lost volume ..... So if volume x density = weight, if my weight is the same but my volume is decreasing, my density must be increasing.? I'm changing shape and body comp - (the electron re-modelling diet ? ) I may find out in time that I'm at the right weight now, I may lose some, or I may even put some on, it will be interesting to see where electron remodelling takes me

### kovita — 2014-09-29T12:00:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dont-fool-yourself-energy-consumption-counts.11783/post-142138>

> [Sue-UK said:](/goto/post?id=142030)
>
> When I first started reading Jack’s blogs I was already in the calorie counting doesn’t work club, and actually had a bet on with someone who believes in exercise more eat less. I said I could double my normal maintenance calorie intake and lose weight without exercising. It worked, I lost weight and had good energy levels, but a fatfest wasn’t a healthy solution, (and as expected I went back to my previous weight). To satisfy the bet, I had to eat whether I was hungry or not, and eat foods in relation to their calorie content, rather than what my body needed. For a couple of weeks it was interesting as a biohack, and it actually spurred me on into sticking with the Epi Paleo journey.
>
> One of the things that stopped me continuing with it (I admit I was tempted ......) was that I knew my weight problems weren’t caused by lack of stuffing my face. I had already experienced my problems weren't caused by lack of self-imposed calorie deprivation. I originally gave that up when I realized I was teaching my then very young daughter to diet …… and to base self-worth on size …..
>
> I have thought for a long time that healthy, leptin sensitive people don’t need to count calories, aren’t obsessed about food, will sometimes under eat (taking the extra advantage of autophagy) and if they do overeat their brains will account for the excess energy and will deal with it (for example as heat/natural increase in NEAT/naturally decreased appetite). I wanted a brain that could do that,  but thought I had damaged my metabolism too far, and that not getting worse was the best I could hope for. Thanks to Jack's teaching I now know differently.  Naturally occurring calorie restriction that results from my brain reading my energy status and lowering my appetite, naturally increasing NEAT etc is my goal. Self imposed calorie restriction doesn’t support my n=1. For me its forget calories, follow the electron ......

Sue, the last column is fantastic. I think you hit the secret. I can give you a point of view of someone who was always leptin sensitive and you just describe how I work. I never counted calories and my weight is stable as long as i remember despite of having two children. If I eat too much, I generate a lot of heat and sometimes I eat almost nothing and the other day I may have a bit too much. I am pretty sure, your body composition is improving. I have never went over 18% body fat, even with the pregnancies, but epipaleo improved and stabilized my body composition despite of the fact I almost stopped exercising and all I do is walking and some HIIT (never more than 60 min a week and always between 5-6pm).

BUY, HOLD strategy.
There is a bubble in real estate about 5 years from now. This is due to the post recession teens who will finally be buying into a mortgage.
Interest rates are ridiculously low.. Buy what you can develop.
In the meantime, your life will be balanced. You can outsource marketing and focus on operations, meaning lots of outdoor time.
Look for locations within 2 hours of a major airline hub.

# Thread: How to tell when it is safe to start eating ketogenic
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-tell-when-it-is-safe-to-start-eating-ketogenic.11787/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Ben1983 — 2014-09-27T19:13:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-tell-when-it-is-safe-to-start-eating-ketogenic.11787/post-142036>

How do I know when it is safe to start eating to go into ketosgenically? Can I determine this without getting tests done? I'm just cautious as I don't want to have any major energy crashes by starting it at a time when I may not be ready for it. Also, do you recommend having a carb refill day once a week like dave asprey suggests? Is it recommended to be in ketosis all year round or just in winter....because doesn't jack recommend eating some more carbs in the warmer months which I assuming would take us out of ketosis?

### prAna303 — 2014-09-27T19:32:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-tell-when-it-is-safe-to-start-eating-ketogenic.11787/post-142037>

I keep it very simple and i also have the big advantage of spending most of my time in the elements, nature.

Living in Sweden is simple because we have two big seasons with small transitions between them...

Fall - Mid september until mid november, here i start to go away from carbs, i follow the signals from nature which says, they get rotten and bad.

Winter - Mid november until late march, not a carb in sight, if they are there, snow is hiding them and they would taste like shit. Here i go with fish, seafoods, game and fats, mostly butter or lard or animal fats.

Spring - Early april until late may, not that many carbs, just some leafs here and there, things start to grow.

Summer - June until mid september, here i act like the great bear. Berries everywhere, mushrooms and other stuff.

Now this is for ME and i do like this because i live like i do... If you are in a city, sleep in a city, work in a city and get entertained in the city i would perhaps go with fats and proteins most of the time.

Now i do not like words we put on stuff, same with ketogenic, we get fucked up about terms instead of thinking what is most important for me, right here and right now.

Doing these carb loadings feel for me like mumbo jumbo, if you can follow nature you see that is is quite impossible to do in that state. Just because we can buy it does not mean we should do it....

Also, i tend to do this really strict because i love to be in sync with nature and its elements, why? I do most of my passions there and with that boost of energy i get i can survive a little better in modern world.

### caroline — 2014-09-28T00:10:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-tell-when-it-is-safe-to-start-eating-ketogenic.11787/post-142049>

Ben .... please remember that dr. K. is a clinician ....... Dave Asprey is not...

Maybe start re reading the blogs .... you have missed a lot!

do you have Jack's book?

### Ben1983 — 2014-09-28T03:54:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-tell-when-it-is-safe-to-start-eating-ketogenic.11787/post-142056>

Yes, I bought his book when it first came out but much of it went over my head. I feel like I would understand much more though if I read it a second time so I'll have to go and do that,

### Ben1983 — 2014-09-28T12:56:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-tell-when-it-is-safe-to-start-eating-ketogenic.11787/post-142078>

And yes, I need to SCHEDULE time to read the blogs or I will never have time for it.

Last day. I did CT two times. The last time at 7.00pm. I tried to sleep at 9pm but it was to early for me, couldn't fall asleep so I read till 10.30 with candle light and then after going to sleep (my feet were still cold) I fell asleep almost immediatley. I just have woken up at 6.50am .
Maybe you're trying to go to early to bed for your time zone and you slowly have do adapt to go earlier.

# Thread: Journal Update - incl BP coffee, metamatrix, tim jackson..
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-update-incl-bp-coffee-metamatrix-tim-jackson.11791/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### margor_j — 2014-09-28T13:56:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-update-incl-bp-coffee-metamatrix-tim-jackson.11791/post-142081>

Hi hi all,

Its been ages i know! What a journey this life of mine is! Heres an update:

1) WEIGHT LOSS! i put it all down to bulletproof coffee! Well, it certainly helped going from BAB to BPC.. i do still add protein to my coffee / consume some protein, but BPC has also been a life changer, because, i can have coffee!! I have found, im better not trying to push through to 2pm either. some days it happens, but its not a conscious effort. I think thats adrenal trash thats put paid to that. If i ever feel out of sorts ie travel etc, then i SMASH bpc - load up on butter, have maybe two in a morning.. it sorts me out so quickly

2) TRAVEL - iv just come back from Japan. I was there for a weeks work (bike race). We went to the Tour of Hokkaido. Its an 8 hour flight to get to. Pretty shit sleep over there, but WOW! the hotel buffets were amazing - every single meal. So i was loading up on raw fish, miso, seaweed, cooked fish, duck.. and really nice deep baths in the hotel rooms, with massive ice dispensing machines on certain floors, hello ice baths! and daily earthing / looking at the sun.. but something was amiss.. i was experimenting with a pharma sleeper, but taking like a quarter of a tab (im trying to hard to get off melatonin) and it proved to me again, how much i cannot handle pharmas.. anyway, i was uber drowsy by day, so was it that that made me a bit ratty? which i havent had for a long time.. plus my feet got a bit puffy on the flights. Flying, especially over nite, throws me out. its pretty much a pick at an airplane meal and thats me, done, toasted  but the time difference was only one hour... ticket to wellness, right? hmmm

3) came back to aus from japan, straight to another race... wow, tired. definitely felt a bit flat that week. did all i can (earthing, high fat etc) finished that race, had four days off and was pre menstrual - wow, i was like a lactating hippo, i thought id never stop eating :-o i cant remember ever being that bad! dr TJ reckons the circadian disruption can do that... hmm i thought id nailed that.. anyway, got a period and back to not needing much food / obsessing about food.. lol

4) FISH OIL - well, i decided to self medicate with fish oil. i decided im not getting enough seafood, so il supplement. First time in MONTHS that i stopped getting the debilitating hormonal headaches that can last up to ten days post period.. that was a BREAK THROUGH! they were so bad not even my super strong migraine suppositories could move them - and thats saying something... iv noticed this month, some headache-ines coming into the picture.. hmm interesting to note, considering hte shocking build up and feeling so out of whack..

5) DR Tim.. well, i had another consult this week, then we found out he didnt have much info from metamatrix, cos htey hadnt sent him the full results. thats a very expensive mishap for me, but ... i cant change it now. I am happy to be working with him though.. im now using a curcumin skin cream to help lower inflammation (im going to start adding a bit to my BPC i think), and starting on homeopathic treatment for parasites / viruses. he also wants me to get other viruses tested for - i do think he doesnt realise that he needs to be specific! I could have had all the tests he wants me to have, in thailand. and im back in australia now.. grrr im really reluctant to fork out $60 to some GP that will tell me to go home, exercise more and eat less - as past history has shown.. anyway. il also do another hormone panel an ASI.

6) EXERCISE - well, im now ABLE to go out walking! even up hill! i used to have crippling lower back pain... was it the extra weight? (oh, so iv lost around 6kg i think? ) i enjoy walking, some days i try a more of a HITT style work out (but i do feel uncomfy still trying to 'sprint', better if i have a hill i can do that up or a stationary bike, but even to get out and walk, i love it! yoga, im doing daily again, and qi kung. i like to do my qi kung on wet grass !  id love to be weight training again one day, but im still also aware that im not 100%...

7) BLUE LIGHT: well, one aspect of my job thats awesome is i spend a lot of time outside! so i always try to make the most of barefoot when i can. and expose my eyes to daylight. Iv also made a conscious effort ot use my blue blocking shades lately and im sure they actually make a pronounced difference! I was having a shocking time with sleep pre japan, it seems to come in waves, which is why i think its possibly virally / parasitic related..??! as opposed to just cortisol..

8) THAILAND - well, i was in thailand for june and july.. for the last month or so there, i was very depressed, sad, crying, helpless, hopeless... it was awful! i was eating a lot of oysters, doing BPC, coconut oil etc. .all that good stuff.. but i was also staying in a room that had around 12 - 18 wifi signals. this rang alarm bells and has for sure, put me off going back to that same place. i also wonder about the powerlines - in asia they tend to be very low, with loads and loads of wires.. the difference in me was hideous! then i flew back to australia, pretty much straight to another race, and i INSTANTLY felt better. :-o (wow, JK really knows what hes talking about LOL!! no, seriously, its quite astounding to read his posts and then have it hit you square on... )

well, i m sure il think of sommit else, but thats where im at for now! I have another trip for work, to china, but its for two weeks. so that will hopefully be ok, but it could have a 3 week hong kong prelude. Which would be a holiday. Im just a bit nervous now that il have a shocker of a time there.. eek. keep on ticking along.. sometimes i still get frustrated but, aside from the last two weeks, i dont feel like an elephant, and thast a really nice feeling!

### caroline — 2014-09-29T00:48:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-update-incl-bp-coffee-metamatrix-tim-jackson.11791/post-142101>

hey i asked TJ about HcG - he said i can do it BUT its going to be more of a strain on my already straining body... im happy to wait til the time seems really right. i had such a weird feeling about doing it before.. and i guess the mix was all skewiff cos i wouldnt listen to my gut feeling on it..

# Thread: Hot bath after CT
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-bath-after-ct.11793/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Ben1983 — 2014-09-29T03:58:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-bath-after-ct.11793/post-142110>

Does having a hot bath immediately after CT negate the effects of CT? I can't really function very well after getting out of the ice bath and don't have a lot of free time to wait around to warm up before I go to work which is why I've been warming up I'm hot bath.

### Ben1983 — 2014-09-29T12:28:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-bath-after-ct.11793/post-142144>

It was a hot day today in Sydney (for spring) so I thought I'd test warming myself up in the sun instead of having a hot bath immediately after. I say outside for 15 as that was all the spare time I had left and I then had to go and prepare my meals for work....the kitchen is downstair and our downstairs stays very cool.....so when I was downstairs in the cool I felt very cold again (but it felt awesome outside in the sun)....I was probably only cooking for about 15 minutes and then I just realised that I wasn't cold anymore....so it actually didn't take me too long to warm up.....not sure how well that would work on a colder day though.

### Lahelada — 2014-09-29T12:30:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-bath-after-ct.11793/post-142146>

I found that I warm up quicker if I dont put on clothes after CT for as long as possible.

### Ben1983 — 2014-09-29T12:31:43+0300

# Thread: symbiosis of Malick and Lynn Margulis
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symbiosis-of-malick-and-lynn-margulis.11794/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JoeBranca — 2014-09-29T06:27:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symbiosis-of-malick-and-lynn-margulis.11794/post-142124>

fascinating information on a way to further Margulis' passion, and how it ties into a film that has resonatned more for me than any other.

[http://www.huffingtonpost.com/nathan-currier/evolution-helps-those-who\_b\_5817298.html](http://www.huffingtonpost.com/nathan-currier/evolution-helps-those-who_b_5817298.html)

> Right now you have a chance to help the late evolutionary biologist [Lynn Margulis](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lynn_Margulis), one of the most important scientists the United States has ever produced, help us all a little bit. If you enjoyed [Terrence Malick](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Terrence_Malick)'s film [_The Tree of Life_](http://www.imdb.com/title/tt0478304/), a bit of gossip is that Lynn offered her expertise in pre-Cambrian biology as a consultant for the movie, in return for help from Malick in rescuing a series of films in developmental biology, considered by Lynn and others to be a treasure of science but now fading and in dire need of digitization.
>
> Malick and his assistant Nick Gonda did indeed help, but this only covered about half of the full series, and there is [currently a Kickstarter campaign](https://www.kickstarter.com/projects/540514942/unobtainium-digitizing-the-reel-life-films-of-lynn), begun by some assistants and friends of Lynn Margulis, to help save the rest, such that all the films will be preserved at the Library of Congress, as well as online for your enjoyment and edification. (The 38 films Malick helped Margulis with are now preserved at the Library of Congress.)

# Thread: Okay, here's a weird question...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/okay-heres-a-weird-question.11797/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hansen Kenimer — 2014-09-29T17:47:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/okay-heres-a-weird-question.11797/post-142159>

Is it possible to improve your redox potential so much that YOU affect electronics instead of the other way around? Yesterday on two occasions I walked into the room where my daughter was watching TV (satellite) and the picture pixels went funny for a moment...perfectly clear day, TV only a few months old, and she'd been watching for a while. It's never happened before, it didn't happen that day prior to those moments, and it didn't happen again afterwards. And this morning driving to work I had my phone plugged in to the mp3 jack and there's this one song that touches me to the core, making me very emotional, that was I singing along with, and it kept stopping. Not pausing like it was buffering (it's all downloaded on my phone), but I literally had to keep pressing the play button to get it to keep playing. Finally I got so frustrated I let it loop through the playlist, and never had another issue.

Just WEIRD. But two days ago I entered into a relationship with "aura man" with whom I have this amazing energy connection (some of you will remember the story from another thread from a few weeks ago) and have been feeling pretty through the roof. Okay, I'm levitating. ;-)

Am I crazy?

### kovita — 2014-09-29T19:14:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/okay-heres-a-weird-question.11797/post-142162>

my grandmother is regulary discharging AAA type batteries anywhere around her. She has some special gifts and skills i must say. So I think perfectly possible ;-)

### Hansen Kenimer — 2014-09-29T19:36:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/okay-heres-a-weird-question.11797/post-142168>

Kovita, I am intrigued! What are your grandmother's special gifts and skills?

### Josh — 2014-09-29T19:43:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/okay-heres-a-weird-question.11797/post-142169>

I met someone who was twice struck by lightening and he discharges batteries, makes streetlights flicker and other stuff for 30 years now.

### bionaut — 2014-09-29T20:26:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/okay-heres-a-weird-question.11797/post-142171>

I would think that a persons cns would have a major electrical deficit to be able to strip electrons from atoms with higher atomic masses from physical contact with a device.

Your heart and head emit a pretty big magnetic field, perhaps that had something to do with it.

### kovita — 2014-09-29T20:33:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/okay-heres-a-weird-question.11797/post-142173>

> [Hansen Kenimer said:](/goto/post?id=142168)
>
> Kovita, I am intrigued! What are your grandmother's special gifts and skills?

she can find places where the bones have been broken or damaged without touching, she can find and relieve pains without touching and she can sense what I am doing and where I am (this seems to be working with me much better than with other people). I have never tried to find an explanation but landing on this page and it's scientific facts and having eyes and mind opened to see and feel things that are on the verge of paramormality opened a lot of possible explanations.

### Shijin13 — 2014-09-29T21:02:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/okay-heres-a-weird-question.11797/post-142176>

I would say yes this is fully possible. speaking from experience. 2 weeks ago I crashed a special token reader. My client's IT was aggog at what happened. they'd never seen anything do that. I test new systems for my companies IT dept - b/c I crash systems in ways no one else ever does. and those crashes find bugs they never knew were in the programs. Last week we were preparing for a briefing - and I like to think and walk - so was walking around my co-workers office - as I got over to his work station suddenly his keyborad, and mouse stopped working, as soon as I'd walk away it would start working again. I had just consumed a massive amt of oysters... This is why I use a mac at home - rarely crashes - bu then the down side is its probably sucking more electrons than my work PC.

### caroline — 2014-09-30T00:07:24+0300

We are having pretty weird wether in OZ the las couple of days ..... Two days ago it was 17c and yesterday it was 95F .... today supposed to be the same and then tomorrow a huge drop.

I've had the most excruciating headache yesterday afternoon ...like my head was in a vice! I don't even remember the last time I had a headache - let a lone a mind bender like this one!

Also a very sore throat and very low energy...

Any one else finding the same????

### Gracelind — 2014-09-30T09:42:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weather-patterns.11798/post-142233>

Hope you're feeling better today . . . and that your weather goes back to perfect.

We had a rainstorm that mostly put out a huge wildfire and left snow on the peaks all around. Very pretty. But it seems to happen every year so can't say it's that unusual. Back to clear sunny days now and dry for a couple more weeks.

More rain showers than usual this summer though it didn't put a dent in the drought. Prospects for a much-needed heavy snowfall this winter seems ever dimmer. As we always say, we'll never know till it happens.

### kovita — 2014-09-30T13:07:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weather-patterns.11798/post-142237>

we are having pretty usual weather for this part of the year here in central europe. still quite a lot of sun, some days wamer other cooler and some rain (in general the last rwo years were quite dry). There was some intense rainfall on the beginning of september so everything is unusually lash and green, one would not believe we are in the autumn. What is spectacular this year, are the sunsets. Maybe it is just me paying attention but man, are they incredibly red and orange and pink and clear. The sky looks like the most beautifull painting, my girl was saying me yesterday that the sky has the most beautifull colour she had ever seen and that I have to find her a dress like this ;-)
We have a wave of viral attacks, I rarely get sick at all, but I have produced such amount of mucus, it is hard to believe. To me the spring and the autumn are always more challenging, the transition seasons. I see it around me and in me every year, it affects my material body and my psyochoenetic body the same way. Curiously it is exactly when the nature is at the peak of it's beauty. It almost feel like that to get all the energy to create such a wonders like spring or autumn the energy must be redirected out of our bodies and so we struggle in the name of nature beauty. I think I got too far..

Anyway, i hooe both of you ladies are doing good today

# Thread: Where to get oysters?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-get-oysters.11799/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### lakers — 2014-09-30T00:47:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-get-oysters.11799/post-142196>

I live in socal, about an hour from the beach. LA County to be specific.

Any recommendations on where to buy? Are canned oysters ( for example, from Trader Joes ) frowned upon? Or are they better than nothing, or are they not recommended?

### fitness@home — 2014-09-30T01:43:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-get-oysters.11799/post-142203>

Try a local seafood shop or Oriental market. I also frequent local oyster bars and Mexican seafood restaurants.

The Mexican seafood place near where I live offers a dozen oysters for $6.99 at lunchtime, Monday thru Friday.

I also used to buy them frozen at Costco.

### JoeBranca — 2014-09-30T22:38:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-get-oysters.11799/post-142304>

The canned are still good, but you can get live oysters probably witout too much hassle, probably more in L.A. but these are spots I know of:
_
\- Zion Market_ (there's one near me in Orange County) have good sized live pacific oysters for .99c each. There are Zion markets in Long Beach and L.A., check them out.

\- Also _HMart_ (another Korean market) often have live oysters and live mussels.

\- The _99 Ranch_ markets often have live oysters too but I seem to get a higher amount of less-than-fresh ones from them, so I stick to Zion.

\- If you don't mind spending more for some more variety of oysters, check out _Santa Monica Seafood Co._ in Santa Monica.

\- the _Redondo Pier fish market_ in San Pedro have all kinds of live and local seafood. Haven't been there for a while so I don't recall their prices.

\- Whole Foods usually have one or two kinds of live oysters

\- some farmer's markets have seafood booths, check them out and if there is one with oysters ask the vendor if they cut a deal for quantity.

When you get live oysters, obviously make sure the shell is completely closed, and also make sure to knock each oyster against a hard surface or with a shucker (or have the employee do it if they're getting the oysters for you), listen for it having a hollow sound or solid sound. If it's hollow, put it back in the bin.

Good luck!


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Everything Mae-Wan Ho
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/everything-mae-wan-ho.11800/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PaulG — 2014-09-30T03:31:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/everything-mae-wan-ho.11800/post-142209>

**Biography**
[Mae-Wan Ho](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mae-Wan_Ho) (b. 12 November 1941, [Hong Kong](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hong_Kong); [UK](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_Kingdom) citizen) is a [geneticist](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Genetics)[\[1\]](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mae-Wan_Ho#cite_note-1)[\[2\]](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mae-Wan_Ho#cite_note-Independent_Science_Panel-2)[\[3\]](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mae-Wan_Ho#cite_note-Public_Hearing-3) known for her critical views on [genetic engineering](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Genetic_engineering) and [neo-Darwinism](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Neo-Darwinism).[\[4\]](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mae-Wan_Ho#cite_note-Dr._Mae-Wan_Ho_bio-4)[\[5\]](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mae-Wan_Ho#cite_note-5) Ho has authored or co-authored a number of publications, including 10 books, such as [_The Rainbow and the Worm, the Physics of Organisms_ (1993, 1998)](http://www.amazon.com/The-Rainbow-Worm-Physics-Organisms/dp/9812832602), _Genetic Engineering: Dream or Nightmare?_ (1998, 1999), _Living with the Fluid Genome_ (2003) and [Living Rainbow H20](http://www.amazon.com/Living-Rainbow-H2O-Mae-Wan-Ho/dp/9814390895/ref=la_B001JOI1UW_1_1?s=books&ie=UTF8&qid=1412034566&sr=1-1).

[**Books by**](http://www.amazon.com/Mae-Wan-Ho/e/B001JOI1UW/ref=dp_byline_cont_book_1)

**[Main Website (http://www.i-sis.org.uk/)](http://www.i-sis.org.uk)**

[**CV**](http://www.i-sis.org.uk/MWHcv.php)

**Recent Articles**
[Illuminating Water and Life (](http://www.mdpi.com/1099-4300/16/9/4874)2014)
[Story of Phi (6 Parts)](http://www.i-sis.org.uk/The_Story_of_Phi-Part_1.php) (2014)
[Colours of Water (A Festival of art, science and music inspired by water)](http://www.i-sis.org.uk/coloursofwater/) (2013)
[Quantum Coherent Water and Life](http://www.i-sis.org.uk/Quantum_Coherent_Water_Life.php) (2011)

[**List of Articles relating to water in the organism**](http://www.i-sis.org.uk/SO_water.php)

[**YouTube Videos**](https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=Mae-Wan+Ho)

### smart cat — 2015-05-12T15:00:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/everything-mae-wan-ho.11800/post-163952>

Commun Integr Biol. 2013 Mar 1; 6(2): e23149.
Published online 2013 Mar 1. doi: [10.4161/cib.23149](http://dx.doi.org/10.4161%2Fcib.23149)
PMCID: PMC3609844

**The rainbow and the worm**
Establishing a new physics of life

[Tam Hunt](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=Hunt%20T%5Bauth%5D)\*
University of California Santa Barbara
What is life? Many have asked this question, and no definitive answer is yet widely accepted. (...) There is, however, an intriguing new set of ideas that have been developed by Mae-Wan Ho, a biophysicist and science activist (as she calls herself) based in London. Ho’s basic assertion is that life exists on a spectrum and is at its root organized, quantum coherent energy. Ho’s work attempts to bridge the gap between physics and biology by recognizing that there is no real gap at all—just a gap in current methods and habits of thinking.

I had the pleasure of interviewing Mae-Wan via email about her books, _The Rainbow and the Worm: The Physics of Organisms_ (now in its third edition) and _Living Rainbow H2O_, her newest work about the startling properties of water and its relation to life as we know it.

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3609844/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3609844/)
